title: Instant Pot Pulled Pork
description: Chunks of spice-rubbed pork butt, seared then pressure-cooked in a smoky broth until they shred effortlessly, finished with barbecue sauce or the pot juices.

items: 4 lb pork butt roast, boneless or bone-in
½–1 cup barbecue sauce, to taste

steps: Trim the excess fat from the pork and cut it into four even chunks.
Whisk the brown sugar, salt and all the spices together in a large bowl, then roll the pork chunks in the rub until well coated.
Heat the oil on sauté until the pot reads hot. Sear the pork in two batches, about 2 minutes per side, and set aside on a plate.
Cancel sauté and pour in ½ cup of the broth, scraping the browned bits off the bottom. Add the remaining broth, the Worcestershire sauce and the liquid smoke.

Return the pork to the pot, spaced out in the liquid. Lock the lid, set the valve to sealing, and pressure cook on high for 60 minutes, followed by a 20-minute natural release (or until the pin drops).
Shred the pork in a large bowl with two forks. Dress with barbecue sauce, or ladle over some of the cooking liquid — it carries most of the flavour.

notes: The pot juices make an excellent alternative to barbecue sauce; skim the fat and spoon over.
Leftovers keep 3–4 days refrigerated and freeze well for 3 months.

title: Instant Pot Boneless Pork Chops
description: Thick boneless chops in a sweet-smoky rub, browned in butter and pressure-cooked to juicy in under twenty minutes.

steps: Mix the brown sugar and spices and rub the mixture into both sides of the chops.
Melt the butter on sauté (high). Brown the chops 1–2 minutes per side, then set aside and cancel sauté.
Pour in the broth and scrape the bottom of the pot clean. Stir in the Worcestershire sauce and liquid smoke, then return the chops to the liquid.
Lock the lid, set the valve to sealing, and pressure cook on high for 7 minutes. Let the pressure release naturally for 12 minutes, then quick-release the rest.
Rest the chops for about 5 minutes before serving.

notes: The timing is calibrated for 1-inch chops; thinner ones will overcook.

title: Instant Pot Ribs
description: Pork ribs in a chilli-garlic rub, steamed tender over apple juice and liquid smoke, then slathered in barbecue sauce and flashed under the broiler.

items: ½ cup apple juice (or ¼ cup apple cider vinegar)

steps: Peel the membrane off the back of the ribs if it's still attached, then rub the seasoning mix over both sides.
Set the wire trivet in the pot and add the water, apple juice and liquid smoke.
Stand the ribs upright on the trivet, meat side facing out — one large rack can circle the inside of the pot.
Lock the lid, close the vent, and pressure cook on high for 25 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 15 minutes, then quick-release the remainder.
Move the ribs to a foil-lined baking sheet, brush generously with barbecue sauce, and broil 2–3 minutes to caramelise if you like.

notes: Liquid smoke is optional but does most of the work of a smoker.
The broil step is worth it — it sets the sauce into a proper glaze.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 219 kcal · Carbs: 18 g · Protein: 14 g · Fat: 9 g · Sodium: 456 mg · Sugar: 15 g

title: Instant Pot Mac and Cheese
description: Whole wheat macaroni cooked straight in the pot, turned creamy with butter, Greek yogurt and two cups of sharp cheddar melted in by hand.

items: 8 oz uncooked whole wheat elbow macaroni (or shells)
2 tbsp unsalted butter, cut into pieces
1 (5.3 oz) container full-fat Greek yogurt (about ½ cup)

steps: Stir the pasta, water and ¼ tsp of the salt together in the pot. Pressure cook on high for 4 minutes (5 for shells), then quick-release as soon as the timer sounds.
Open the lid; if the pasta looks very wet, drain off all but a thin layer of water and keep what you drain.
Stir in the butter until melted, then the mustard, pepper, cayenne, nutmeg and remaining ¼ tsp salt.
Stir in the Greek yogurt.

Add the cheddar a small handful at a time, stirring each addition until fully melted before the next — rushing makes it lumpy. Loosen with reserved pasta water if it tightens up, flick on sauté briefly if it cools, and season to taste.

notes: Use full-fat yogurt; fat-free splits.
Shred the cheese yourself — pre-shredded is coated in starch and melts grainy.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 504 kcal · Carbs: 46 g · Protein: 26 g · Fat: 26 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Pot Roast & Potatoes
description: A whole seasoned chuck roast seared in the pot, pressure-cooked with baby potatoes, carrots and onion, then finished with a gravy made from the braising broth.

items: 4 large carrots, cut into large chunks

steps: Set the pot to sauté. Stir the salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder and paprika together and rub over every side of the roast.
Add the oil, wait 30 seconds, then lower in the roast. Leave it untouched 3–4 minutes per side, turning with tongs, until browned all over.

Arrange the potatoes, carrots and onion around the roast and pour the broth and Worcestershire sauce over everything. Lock the lid, set the valve to sealing, and pressure cook on high — 60 minutes for a 3 lb roast, up to 80 for 5 lb.
When the timer ends, leave the pot alone for a 10-minute natural release, then vent the remaining steam and wait for the pin to drop before opening.

Move the roast and vegetables to a platter and pull the beef into chunks with two forks. Skim any bits from the broth, set the pot to simmer, and whisk in the cornstarch mixed with the water until the gravy thickens. Season to taste.
Pour the gravy over the beef and vegetables, with fresh thyme or parsley if you have it.

notes: Cooking from frozen works: add roughly 20–30 minutes to the pressure time.
If using baby carrots, add them with the potatoes rather than earlier — they turn to mush otherwise.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 133 kcal · Carbs: 23 g · Protein: 4 g · Fat: 3 g · Sodium: 1087 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Instant Pot Beef Stew
description: Chuck steak browned whole then cubed and pressure-cooked with potatoes, carrots and celery in a sherry-deglazed gravy, deepened with Worcestershire, soy and fish sauce.

items: 2 lb chuck or blade steak, 1½ inch thick

steps: Heat the pot on sauté (high) until it reads HOT. Pat the steak dry, season with salt and pepper, and brown in the oil about 7 minutes per side. Set aside.
Sauté the mushrooms until their moisture cooks off and the edges brown, about 6 minutes. Season lightly and set aside.
Sauté the onions for 3 minutes, then add the garlic, bay leaves and thyme for 1 minute more.
Pour in the sherry and scrape the bottom of the pot completely clean, letting the alcohol cook off briefly.

Switch the pot off. Cut the browned steak into 1½–2 inch cubes and return it with any juices. Stir in the stock, Worcestershire, soy and fish sauce, then add the potatoes, carrots and celery and spread the tomato paste over the top without stirring it in.
Lock the lid, set the valve to sealing, and pressure cook on high for 32 minutes with a 10-minute natural release.
Simmer on sauté, crushing some of the potato and carrot into the liquid to thicken it. Stir in the peas and mushrooms, warm through, and season to taste.

notes: For firmer vegetables, pressure cook them separately for 4 minutes first and stir half back in at the end.
The fish sauce isn't detectable as fish — it just deepens the gravy.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 475 kcal · Carbs: 25 g · Protein: 34 g · Fat: 27 g · Sodium: 666 mg · Fiber: 5 g · Sugar: 7 g

title: Instant Pot Butter Chicken
description: Chicken thighs pressure-cooked on a spiced tomato base, the sauce blended smooth and enriched with butter and cream — a restaurant-style butter chicken in half an hour.

ingredient_groups.name: To finish
items: 4 oz butter, cubed (or coconut oil for dairy-free)
4 oz heavy cream (or full-fat coconut milk)

steps: Add the sauce ingredients to the pot in the order listed and mix well, then lay the chicken on top. (Frozen thighs work — press them into the sauce.)
Lock the lid and pressure cook on high for 10 minutes, followed by a 10-minute natural release, then vent the rest.
Lift the chicken out and set aside.
Blend the sauce smooth, ideally with an immersion blender.
Let the sauce cool slightly, then stir in the butter, cream, cilantro and remaining garam masala until incorporated — adding them to boiling sauce thins it. It should coat the back of a spoon.
This makes double the sauce you need: freeze half, or refrigerate 2–3 days.
Return the chicken, cutting it into smaller pieces if you like (don't shred), and warm through. Serve over rice or zucchini noodles.

notes: The spare sauce reheats into a second meal — simmer with cooked chicken, or with paneer and peas for a quick paneer makhani.
If the sauce goes thin after adding cream, ten minutes in the fridge sets it right.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 336 kcal · Carbs: 11 g · Protein: 25 g · Fat: 22 g · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken Biryani
description: Yogurt-marinated chicken layered under saffron-streaked basmati with whole spices, caramelised onions, and a scatter of ghee-toasted cashews and raisins.

ingredient_groups.name: Marinade
items: 1 lb boneless chicken, in 1½ inch pieces

ingredient_groups.name: Biryani
items: 1 cup basmati rice, washed and soaked 20 minutes

step_groups.name: Marinate
steps: Mix the chicken with all the marinade ingredients and refrigerate for 30 minutes, or up to 8 hours.

step_groups.name: Build the biryani
steps: Soak the saffron in the warm milk and set aside.
Heat the ghee on sauté and toast the cashews and raisins about a minute until lightly golden. Remove and reserve.
Cook the sliced onion 10–12 minutes, stirring often, until caramelised. Set half aside for garnish.
Add the whole spices for 30 seconds, then the marinated chicken. Sauté about 3 minutes, deglazing the base well, then spread the chicken in an even layer.
Spread the rice evenly over the chicken — don't stir. Drizzle the saffron milk over the rice and pour in the water.
Lock the lid, valve to sealing, and pressure cook on high for 4 minutes. Let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then vent the rest.
Serve garnished with the reserved onions, cashews, raisins and cilantro, with raita alongside.

notes: Keeping the rice layered on top, not stirred in, is what keeps the grains distinct.
Soaking the rice for the full 20 minutes matters for even cooking at only 4 minutes under pressure.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 453 kcal · Carbs: 50 g · Protein: 31 g · Fat: 14 g · Sodium: 752 mg · Fiber: 4 g · Sugar: 3 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken Breast
description: Seasoned boneless breasts, optionally seared, pressure-cooked in broth with a knob of butter for reliably juicy chicken — plus a two-minute gravy from the pot juices.

items: 4 boneless skinless chicken breasts (about 8 oz each, 2 lb total)

steps: Mix the Italian seasoning, salt, paprika, garlic powder and pepper and sprinkle over all sides of the chicken.

Optional but worth it: sauté until the pot reads hot, add the oil, and sear the breasts about 2 minutes per side until golden (in batches if needed). Remove, add the broth, and scrape the bottom clean.
Return the chicken to the broth and set the butter on top.
Lock the lid, valve to sealing, and pressure cook on high for 8 minutes for 8 oz breasts — add a minute or two for larger ones. Allow about 10 minutes for the pot to pressurise.
Let the pressure release naturally for at least 10 minutes, then remove the chicken and rest it 5 minutes before slicing.
For gravy: switch to sauté and whisk 1–2 tbsp each of cornstarch and water into the juices until thickened.

notes: The 8-minute timing assumes 8 oz breasts; weigh rather than guess if you can.
Skipping the sear saves 10 minutes with only a small loss of flavour.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 170 kcal · Carbs: 1 g · Protein: 25 g · Fat: 6 g · Sodium: 449 mg

title: Instant Pot Whole Chicken
description: A whole Cajun-rubbed chicken browned on all sides, stuffed with lemon, onion, garlic and rosemary, then pressure-cooked to rotisserie tenderness — with rice cooked in the resulting broth.

items: 2 cups basmati or long grain rice (optional, to follow)

step_groups.name: The chicken
steps: Pat the chicken thoroughly dry and season it all over — cavity included — with the Cajun seasoning, salt and pepper.
Heat the oil on sauté until the pot reads HOT, then brown the chicken breast-side down first, turning to colour all four sides, about 4 minutes each. Switch off sauté.
Squeeze the lemon over the browned chicken — the sear keeps the seasoning in place.
Stuff the cavity with the onion wedges, garlic, the spent lemon and the rosemary, securing with toothpicks if needed.
Pour in the water — no trivet needed.
Pressure cook on high for 25 minutes, then quick-release the steam.
Rest the chicken 15 minutes before carving, or broil it 5 minutes first for crisp skin — watch it closely.

step_groups.name: Rice in the broth (optional)
steps: Rinse the rice until the water runs clear and soak it 15 minutes.
Add the rice to the broth left in the pot — it should sit an inch under the liquid. Pressure cook on high for 4 minutes with a 15-minute natural release.
Fluff with a fork and serve alongside the chicken.

notes: The pot broth doubles as the rice cooking liquid — don't discard it.
Any all-purpose rub works in place of Cajun seasoning.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 200 kcal · Carbs: 6 g · Protein: 26 g · Fat: 8 g · Sodium: 130 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Instant Pot Chili
description: A thick ground-beef chili on a fire-roasted tomato base with two kinds of beans, built in one pot with the tomatoes layered on top to dodge the burn warning.

items: 14.5 oz can (1¾ cups) low-sodium beef or chicken broth
15 oz can kidney beans, rinsed and drained
15 oz can black beans, rinsed and drained

steps: Heat the oil on sauté and cook the onion 2 minutes until it starts to soften.
Add the beef and cook about 5 minutes, crumbling it as it browns. Drain excess fat if needed.
Stir in the garlic, then cancel sauté.
Add the bell pepper, all the spices, brown sugar, salt, pepper, tomato paste and broth, stirring and scraping the bottom clean.
Pour the crushed tomatoes and beans on top and leave them unstirred — layering them keeps the burn warning away.
Lock the lid, valve to sealing, and pressure cook on high for 15 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then vent the rest and open once the pin drops.
Stir, and simmer on sauté a few minutes if you'd like it thicker. Serve with your usual toppings.

notes: Leaner beef (90%) means no grease slick to skim.
Like most chilis, it's better the next day; keeps 4 days refrigerated.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 369 kcal · Carbs: 42 g · Protein: 28 g · Fat: 12 g · Sodium: 1324 mg · Fiber: 14 g · Sugar: 12 g

title: Instant Pot White Rice
description: Fluffy long grain rice from a 1:1 ratio and four minutes at pressure — the fully hands-off method.

items: 2 cups long grain white rice (basmati or jasmine)

steps: Rinse the rice in a fine sieve under cold water, drain well, and tip into the pot.
Add the water and salt, stir, and make sure no grains are stranded on the sides above the waterline.
Lock the lid and set the valve to sealing.
Pressure cook on high for 4 minutes — the pot takes about 10 minutes to pressurise first.
Leave it alone for a natural release of at least 15 minutes, then vent anything remaining.
Fluff with a fork and serve.

notes: The 1:1 rice-to-water ratio is the point — pressure cooking loses almost no liquid.
The natural release finishes the cooking; venting early gives wet rice.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 225 kcal · Carbs: 49 g · Protein: 4 g · Fat: 1 g · Sodium: 201 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Mashed Potatoes
description: Steamed russets mashed back in the warm pot with staged additions of soft butter and hot milk — a restaurant-technique mash without the stovetop juggling.

items: 4 lb russet potatoes, peeled and quartered
¼ cup–1 lb unsalted butter, room temperature (to taste — more is richer)

steps: Take the butter out of the fridge a couple of hours ahead. Peel, wash and quarter the potatoes.
Add the water and a steamer rack to the pot, pile the potatoes on the rack, and pressure cook on high for 8 minutes with a quick release.
While it cooks, heat the milk to a simmer on the stove and cut the softened butter into cubes.
Lift the potatoes out, empty and dry the inner pot, and return it on keep-warm. Mash the potatoes back into the pot (a food mill gives the finest result).
On the gentlest heat setting, fold in the butter in two stages, stirring each addition until melted in. Then stir in the hot milk, let it absorb, and salt to taste.
Serve immediately.

notes: Butter quantity is a dial from sensible (¼ cup) to indulgent (a full pound) — the technique works across the range.
Hot milk and room-temperature butter are what keep the mash silky rather than gluey.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 191 kcal · Carbs: 33 g · Protein: 4 g · Fat: 5 g · Sodium: 16 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Steel Cut Oats
description: Creamy steel cut oats cooked pot-in-pot in a bowl set over water — no sticking, no scorching, no stirring.

items: 1 cup steel cut oats (not rolled or quick)
1 cup water (plus 1½ cups for the pot)

steps: Pour 1½ cups of cold water into the inner pot and set the trivet in.
In a 7-cup heat-safe stainless bowl or 7×4 inch cake pan, stir together the oats, 1 cup water, the milk, salt and flaxseed if using. (Pyrex is no longer rated for pressure cooking — use metal.)
Set the bowl on the trivet, lock the lid, valve to sealing, and pressure cook on high for 8 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then vent the rest.
Lift the bowl out and serve with whatever toppings you like.

notes: The pot-in-pot method is what prevents the milk scorching against the base.
Leftovers keep 5 days refrigerated and reheat with a splash of milk.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 271 kcal · Carbs: 41 g · Protein: 12 g · Fat: 6 g · Sodium: 40 mg · Fiber: 7 g · Sugar: 4 g

title: Easy Instant Pot Cheesecake
description: A 7-inch vanilla cheesecake on a baked graham crust, steamed under pressure on a foil sling for a crack-free, extra-creamy set.

ingredient_groups.name: Filling
items: 500 g (16 oz) full-fat cream cheese, room temperature
½ cup full-fat sour cream or plain Greek yogurt, room temperature

steps: Heat the oven to 350°F. Stir the graham crumbs into the melted butter and press over the base and an inch up the sides of a 7-inch springform or push pan.
Bake the crust 10 minutes until firm, then set aside.
Beat the cream cheese on medium until smooth, scraping down once. Add the sugar and beat on low — low speed throughout keeps air bubbles out.
Beat in the sour cream on low until just smooth, then the eggs and vanilla the same way.
Pour the filling over the crust. Tap the pan on the counter to raise any air bubbles and pop them.
Fold a long strip of foil into a narrow sling. Add the water to the pot (1½ cups for an 8-quart), set the trivet in, and lay the sling across it with the ends up the sides.
Sit the pan on the sling, fold the ends over the top, and lock the lid.
Pressure cook on high for 32 minutes, then let the pressure release fully naturally — about 15 minutes — before opening.
Lift the pan out by the sling and cool to room temperature on a rack, then refrigerate 8 hours or overnight before slicing.

notes: Room-temperature ingredients are non-negotiable; cold cream cheese never beats smooth.
The overnight chill is where the texture happens — don't shortcut it.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 338 kcal · Carbs: 27 g · Protein: 5 g · Fat: 24 g · Sodium: 269 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 21 g

title: Instant Pot New York Cheesecake
description: A dense, rich New York-style cheesecake — cornstarch-stabilised filling on a salted graham crust, pressure-steamed and chilled overnight. The famously over-tested "#17".

ingredient_groups.name: Crust
items: 2 tsp–1½ tbsp brown sugar, to taste
¼ cup all-purpose flour (optional, for a blind-baked crisper crust)

ingredient_groups.name: Filling
items: 16 oz (454 g) cream cheese, room temperature
½ cup sour cream, room temperature

step_groups.name: Crust
steps: Bring the cream cheese, eggs and sour cream fully to room temperature first — cold ingredients are the main cause of a lumpy top.
Mix the ground graham crackers with the salt and brown sugar (and the flour, if blind-baking), then work in the melted butter until the crumbs hold together.
Line a 7-inch cheesecake pan with parchment on the base and sides, tip in the crumbs, and press into an even layer with the base of a jar.
Firm the crust: freeze it while you make the filling, or bake at 325°F for 15 minutes for a crisper base.

step_groups.name: Filling
steps: Stir the cornstarch, salt and sugar together in a small bowl.
Break up the cream cheese with a hand mixer on low for about 10 seconds — a hand mixer, not a stand mixer, to avoid whipping in air.
Beat in the sugar mixture in two additions on low, 20–30 seconds each, scraping the bowl between additions.
Beat in the sour cream and vanilla on low until just combined.
Blend in the eggs one at a time, 15–20 seconds each, stopping as soon as they're incorporated — overmixing here puffs the cake.
Pour the batter over the crust, tap the pan on the counter to surface the air bubbles, and pop them with a toothpick.

step_groups.name: Cook & chill
steps: Pour 1 cup of cold water into the pot and set the pan on a steamer rack above it. Pressure cook on high for 26 minutes with a full natural release (roughly 7 minutes), then blot any condensation off the surface with a paper towel.
Cool toward room temperature; after 10–15 minutes, run a thin knife between the parchment and the pan wall so the cake can contract without cracking.
Once fully cool, refrigerate at least 4–8 hours, ideally overnight.
To serve, briefly warm the base of the pan to release the cake, peel away the parchment, and slice.

notes: For a smooth or denser texture, err to the shorter or longer end of the mixing times — minimal mixing is the whole philosophy.
The overnight chill sets the signature dense New York texture.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 288 kcal · Carbs: 25 g · Protein: 4 g · Fat: 19 g · Sodium: 271 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 16 g

title: Instant Pot Vegetable Soup
description: A hearty pressure-cooker soup of potatoes, carrots, celery, beans and corn in a tomato and herb broth, with spinach stirred in at the end.

items: 6 cups low-sodium chicken or vegetable broth
1½ cups fire-roasted diced tomatoes (398 ml / 13 oz)
1 cup fresh green beans, cut in thirds
½ cup corn (canned, frozen or fresh)

steps: Set the Instant Pot to sauté and warm the oil. Cook the onion, stirring, until soft.
Switch the pot off. Stir in the tomato paste, garlic, Italian seasoning, salt and pepper and let the residual heat cook them for 1 minute.
Pour in the broth while the pot is still hot and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom.
Add the potatoes, carrots, celery, tomatoes, green beans, corn and bay leaf. Stir.
Lock the lid, set the valve to sealing, and pressure cook on high for 2 minutes. Expect about 15 minutes for the pot to come to pressure first.
When the timer ends, let the pressure release naturally for 8–10 minutes before opening the valve — a quick release can splatter hot soup.
Remove the bay leaf, stir in the spinach and taste. A teaspoon of sugar rounds out the tomato paste if it reads sharp. Serve hot.

notes: Keeps 3–4 days refrigerated in an airtight container.
Freezes well for 4–6 months; cool fully first, thaw overnight in the fridge.
Makes roughly 9½–10 cups; a serving is about 2 cups.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 125 kcal · Carbs: 23 g · Protein: 6 g · Fat: 2 g · Sodium: 757 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Instant Pot Lamb Shanks
description: Lamb shanks marinated in a spiced tomato paste, seared, then pressure-braised with red wine, aromatics and herbs until falling off the bone, finished with a silky strained gravy.

ingredient_groups.name: Shanks & braise
items: 3 lamb shanks, trimmed of excess fat
1 cup dry red wine (inexpensive is fine)

step_groups.name: Marinate
steps: Whisk the marinade ingredients into a paste in a large bowl, then turn the shanks through it until thoroughly coated.

step_groups.name: Cook the shanks
steps: Press sauté twice for the medium setting and heat the oil until the display reads HOT. Sear the shanks meat-side down, turning until browned all over — work in batches with extra oil if the pot is crowded.
Move the shanks to a plate and tent with foil.
Sauté the onion, carrot, celery, chilli (if using) and garlic for 5–7 minutes, stirring often, until softened. Pour in the wine and let it reduce briefly.
Return the shanks to the pot with the cinnamon stick, rosemary, thyme and bay leaves.
Scrape in any leftover marinade, then pour the broth down the side of the pot so it doesn't rinse the seasoning off the meat.
Lock the lid, set the valve to sealing, and pressure cook on high for 60 minutes. Allow 10–15 minutes to come to pressure, and let the steam release naturally at the end.
Open the lid carefully and move the shanks to a plate; tent to keep warm.

step_groups.name: Finish the gravy
steps: Strain the braising liquid through a mesh sieve, pressing on the vegetables, and return it to the pot. On sauté, bring to a simmer, whisk in the cornstarch slurry until glossy and thickened, then season to taste.
Serve the shanks over mashed potatoes with plenty of gravy and a scatter of fresh herbs.

notes: A full natural pressure release keeps the meat tender — don't quick-release.
Leftovers keep 3–4 days refrigerated; reheat gently in the gravy.
nutrition: Per serving — Calories: 531 kcal · Carbs: 19 g · Protein: 42 g · Fat: 26 g · Sodium: 1633 mg · Fiber: 4 g · Sugar: 6 g

title: Best Damn Instant Pot Pork Tenderloin
description: A seasoned pork tenderloin cooked under pressure and finished with a savory gravy. Ready in under 15 minutes.

steps: Remove tenderloin from package and let sit unrefrigerated for 15–20 minutes. Rinse and pat completely dry. Trim visible fat and halve lengthwise into two equal pieces.
Lightly coat each piece with about 1 tsp olive oil. Whisk together brown sugar, black pepper, salt, paprika, onion powder, and garlic powder. Rub the mixture generously all over the meat.

Activate Sauté mode on the Instant Pot. Once hot, place meat inside and brown all sides, about 2 minutes per side. Cancel Sauté, transfer meat to a plate. Pour chicken broth into the pot and scrape the bottom with a wooden spoon to dislodge browned bits. Stir in Worcestershire sauce and liquid smoke.

Return meat to the pot directly in the liquid. Lock the lid and switch vent to sealing. Cook on high pressure for 3 minutes. Let pressure fall naturally for 15 minutes, then open the pot. Transfer meat to a cutting board and rest for 5 minutes. Slice into ¼-inch-thick pieces.

Cancel any mode, then start Sauté again. In a small cup, whisk cornstarch with 3 tbsp of the cooking liquid until smooth. Pour this slurry slowly into the simmering pot while stirring. Once the liquid reaches a boil, press Cancel. Stir until the sauce reaches desired thickness.

notes: Adjust black pepper to your heat preference; start with 1 tsp if you prefer milder seasoning.
Pork tenderloin is best sliced thin and served immediately with the warm gravy spooned over top.

title: Beef Stroganoff
description: A creamy beef and egg noodle dish with mushrooms and sour cream sauce. Ready in under an hour with the pressure cooker.

items: 2 tbsp cornstarch + ¼ cup beef broth or water (optional, for thickening)

steps: Switch to sauté mode. Melt butter, add garlic and mushrooms, and cook until the mushrooms are slightly softened. Dust the flour over and stir until coated.
Pour in 3 cups broth and Worcestershire sauce, then add the stew meat. Lock the lid, set the vent to sealed, and pressure cook on high for 15 minutes.
Release pressure quickly by switching the vent to venting and opening the lid once the float valve drops. Stir in the egg noodles and pressure cook on high for 3 more minutes. Quick release again.

Stir in the sour cream and season with salt and pepper to taste. For a thicker sauce, switch to soup mode, whisk the cornstarch with ¼ cup broth, stir it in, and let the mixture come to a boil before serving.

notes: For a thicker stroganoff, make the cornstarch slurry; skip it if you prefer a thinner sauce.
Sour cream can curdle under high heat, so stir it in after pressure cooking is complete.
Egg noodles cook quickly; avoid overshooting the second pressure stage or they'll turn mushy.
nutrition: Calories: 700 kcal · Carbs: 60 g · Protein: 65 g · Fat: 21 g · Sodium: 1015 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Instant Pot Turkey Breast
description: A seasoned turkey breast that emerges juicy inside with a crispy exterior, cooked under pressure and finished under the broiler.

items: 2 teaspoons Herbs de Provence (or Italian herb blend)
½ teaspoon coarsely cracked black pepper

steps: Switch the cooker to SEAR mode. Coat the turkey breast with olive oil and add a little oil to the pot bottom.
Mix the Herbs de Provence, garlic powder, chili powder, salt, smoked paprika, and black pepper together, then coat the entire turkey with this blend, pressing gently with your fingertips.
Place the turkey in the pot and brown on all sides, turning as needed, for 4–5 minutes total.
Pour 2 cups water around the turkey (not directly on top) to avoid washing away the seasoning.
Secure the lid and move the vent valve to SEALED. Set to HIGH pressure for 30 minutes.
When the timer sounds, move the vent to RELEASE for an immediate quick release. Start preheating your oven to BROIL while steam vents.
Once the float valve drops, open the lid. Using two forks (not tongs, which can scrape the seasoning), lift the turkey onto a small sheet pan or roasting dish.
Add roughly ½ cup of the cooking liquid to the pan with the turkey and slide into the broiler for 3–5 minutes until the surface becomes crisp; monitor to prevent charring.
Transfer the turkey to a cutting board using two forks, drizzle the orange juice over it, and let it rest 10–15 minutes before slicing to preserve juiciness.

step_groups.name: Make the Turkey Gravy
steps: While the turkey rests, set the cooker to SOUP mode. Whisk the corn starch with ⅓ cup cold water until smooth.
Once the liquid in the pot reaches a rolling boil, stir in the corn starch mixture and let it bubble until it thickens.
Turn off the cooker, taste, and adjust salt and pepper as needed. Pour the gravy over the sliced turkey to serve.

notes: The resting period after cooking is essential—it redistributes juices and keeps the meat tender.
If your turkey breast is under 5 pounds, reduce pressure cooking time to 25 minutes; if over 7 pounds, add 5 minutes.
A corn starch slurry thickens the gravy smoothly; whisk it well before adding to avoid lumps.
nutrition: Calories: 417 kcal · Carbs: 3 g · Protein: 82 g · Fat: 9 g · Sodium: 1177 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Brown Basmati Rice
description: Fluffy brown basmati rice cooked under pressure. A simple, nutritious side dish.

steps: Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear.
Pour rice and water into the Instant Pot insert. Stir in ghee and salt if using.
Lock the lid, set to high pressure for 22 minutes with the vent in sealing position.
When the cooker beeps, let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then manually release any remaining pressure.
Open the lid and fluff the rice gently with a fork or ladle. Serve alongside curry or other dishes.

notes: Natural pressure release is key to avoiding mushy rice; do not skip it.
Rinsing removes excess starch and helps achieve separate, fluffy grains.
nutrition: Calories: 407 kcal · Carbs: 71 g · Protein: 7 g · Fat: 10 g · Sodium: 595 mg · Fiber: 3 g

title: Vegan Instant Pot Cauliflower Soup
description: A creamy vegan soup made with cauliflower, cashews, turmeric, and cinnamon. Ready in under 30 minutes.

items: 1 head cauliflower (about 5 cups or 1.6 lbs), cut into 2-inch florets

steps: Switch the pot to sauté mode. Heat the oil, then add the diced onion and garlic. Cook for 3 minutes until softened.
Add the cauliflower florets, cashews, turmeric, cinnamon, broth, and salt. Stir to combine. Press cancel, then seal the lid with the vent in sealing position.
Set to high pressure for 3 minutes.
When the pot beeps, allow pressure to release naturally for 10 minutes, then manually release any remaining pressure.
Stir in the lemon juice if using. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup until creamy. (Alternatively, carefully transfer to a stand blender to purée, working in batches to avoid hot splatters.)
Ladle into bowls and top with fresh parsley and chili flakes if desired.

notes: Cashews thicken the soup naturally; raw ones blend more smoothly than roasted.
Store in the refrigerator for up to 4 days or freeze for up to 3 months.
For a brothier consistency, reduce cashews to ⅓ cup or add extra broth after blending.
nutrition: Calories: 183 kcal · Carbs: 18 g · Protein: 6 g · Fat: 11 g · Sodium: 1333 mg · Fiber: 4 g · Sugar: 6 g

title: Mom's Chicken Curry
description: An authentic North Indian chicken curry made with a tomato-onion base and aromatic whole spices.

items: 1 pound boneless chicken, cut into 1–2 inch pieces
½ tsp Kashmiri red chili powder

steps: Cut chicken into 1–2 inch pieces.
Set pot to sauté mode, add ghee, then toast whole spices for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in chili, onion, ginger, and garlic; sauté for 4 minutes, stirring often, until onions turn golden brown.
Add tomatoes and dry spices (coriander powder, red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, salt); sauté for 4 minutes more, stirring constantly.
Add chicken pieces and sauté for 3–4 minutes to brown and develop flavor.
Pour in water, stir well, and scrape the bottom of the pot to release any browned bits.
Switch to manual or pressure cook mode on high; set timer for 5 minutes.
When cooking finishes, manually release pressure, then stir in lemon juice.
Top with cilantro and serve.

notes: Sautéing the spices and chicken separately before pressure cooking builds deeper flavor.
Quick-release prevents mushy chicken; ensure the pot is fully depressurized before opening.
Substitute boneless thighs for darker, more forgiving meat that stays tender.
nutrition: Calories: 268 kcal · Carbs: 8 g · Protein: 25 g · Fat: 14 g · Sodium: 765 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 3 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken Taco Chili
description: A hearty chili combining chicken, beans, corn, and tomatoes with taco seasonings. Ready in about 30 minutes.

items: 2 (10 oz) cans diced tomatoes with chilies
1 (4 oz) can chopped green chili peppers

steps: Select sauté mode, spray the pot with oil, add onion, and cook until soft and golden, about 4–5 minutes.
Cancel sauté mode, deglaze the pot with ¼ cup water to prevent a burn notice.
Place chicken in the pot and season both sides with half the taco seasoning.
Add the black beans, kidney beans, corn, green chili peppers, diced tomatoes with chilies, and tomato sauce.
Pour in ½ cup water and stir in the remaining taco seasonings gently, without moving the chicken.
Lock the lid and cook at high pressure for 25 minutes.
Allow pressure to release naturally, then remove the chicken and shred it with two forks. Return the shredded chicken to the pot and stir to combine.
Top with cilantro and serve.

notes: Makes about 11 cups total; stores well refrigerated for up to 4 days or frozen for up to 3 months.
Substitute ground chicken or turkey if you prefer a finer texture.
nutrition: Calories: 211 kcal · Carbs: 26 g · Protein: 23 g · Fat: 3 g · Sodium: 623 mg · Fiber: 8 g · Sugar: 4.5 g

title: Chunky Beef, Cabbage and Tomato Soup
description: A hearty soup made with ground beef, cabbage, and tomatoes. Simple to prepare and adaptable to whatever vegetables you have on hand.

items: 28 oz can diced or crushed tomatoes

step_groups.name: Instant Pot
steps: Select sauté mode and heat until very hot. Lightly oil the bottom, then add beef and salt. Cook 3–4 minutes, breaking the meat into small pieces as it browns.
Stir in onion, celery, and carrot. Sauté 4–5 minutes until softened.
Add tomatoes, cabbage, stock, and bay leaves. Close the lid and pressurize on high for 20 minutes.
Allow pressure to release naturally. Remove bay leaves and ladle into bowls.

step_groups.name: Stovetop
steps: In a large pot or Dutch oven, brown the beef with salt over medium-high heat, breaking it into small pieces, about 3–4 minutes.
Add onion, celery, and carrot. Cook 4–5 minutes until softened.
Stir in tomatoes, cabbage, stock, and bay leaves. Cover and simmer on low for 40 minutes.
Remove bay leaves and serve.

notes: Bay leaves should be discarded before eating.
Soup keeps refrigerated up to 4 days or freezes well for up to 3 months.
Substitute or add any firm vegetables (zucchini, green beans, diced potatoes) in place of or alongside the celery and carrot.
nutrition: Calories: 181 kcal · Carbs: 14 g · Protein: 15.5 g · Fat: 6 g · Sodium: 592 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 4.5 g

title: Instant Pot Coconut Chicken Curry
description: A fragrant Indian curry featuring chicken thighs in a creamy coconut and tomato sauce, spiced with warming aromatics and finished with lime juice.

items: ½ tbsp ginger paste or grated
½ tbsp garlic paste or minced
1 cup tomato puree (canned or blended fresh)
1 lb boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1–1½ inch pieces
1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder (or paprika, adjust to taste)

steps: Set the pot to Sauté mode and allow it to preheat. Pour in ghee (or oil), add cumin seeds, and let them toast for 30 seconds.
Add onions, ginger, and garlic; sauté for about 3 minutes until onions turn golden.

Stir in tomato puree and all spices (cumin, turmeric, chili powder, coriander, salt). Sauté for 2–3 minutes. If your pot tends to scorch, skip this step and add tomato puree after searing the chicken instead.
Add chicken pieces and stir to coat. Sear for 3–4 minutes to develop flavor.
Pour in water and stir. Cancel Sauté mode, seal the lid with the vent in sealing position.
Cook on high pressure for 4 minutes.
Once the timer sounds, manually release pressure and open the lid.
Stir in coconut milk and garam masala. Switch to Sauté mode and simmer for 4–5 minutes until sauce thickens to your liking.
Finish with lime juice and top with cilantro. Serve over basmati rice or with naan.

notes: If you prefer a thicker curry, mix ½ tsp cornstarch with 1 tbsp water, stir it in during the final simmer, and cook 1 minute longer.
Bone-in, skin-on thighs work too; increase pressure-cook time to 6 minutes and remove skin before serving if desired.
Leftovers keep refrigerated for up to 3 days and taste even better the next day.
nutrition: Calories: 497 kcal · Carbs: 14 g · Protein: 22 g · Fat: 41 g · Sodium: 714 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Frozen Chicken Breasts in the Instant Pot
description: Boneless skinless chicken breasts cooked from frozen in high-pressure steam. No thawing needed; they emerge tender and ready to serve plain or with sauces.

items: 4 frozen boneless skinless chicken breasts (7–8 oz or 200 g each)

steps: Arrange frozen chicken breasts in the Instant Pot base (trivet not needed); stack is fine as long as breasts aren't clumped together.
Pour in water or broth and season with salt and pepper.
Seal the lid and set valve to sealing. Select Manual/Pressure Cook at high pressure for 10 minutes. The pot will spend about 10 minutes reaching pressure before the countdown begins.
Once the timer ends, let pressure drop naturally for 5–10 minutes, then manually release any remaining pressure. Verify internal temperature reaches 165 °F.
Serve whole, chop, or top with sauce.

notes: Natural pressure release for 5–10 minutes before venting keeps breasts moist.
At 10 minutes high pressure, breasts typically reach 180 °F; 12 minutes ensures a higher safety margin if your pot runs cool.
Swap water or broth for pineapple juice, apple juice, or other liquids for subtle flavor variation.
nutrition: Calories: 128 kcal · Protein: 23 g · Fat: 2 g · Sodium: 134 mg

title: Creamy Instant Pot Chicken Noodle Soup
description: A simple, quick creamy chicken noodle soup loaded with vegetables. Easily adaptable to dairy-free or vegetarian.

items: 1 boneless skinless chicken breast (about 300 g), cut into ½" pieces
1 cup short pasta such as ditalini or orzo (about 130 g)
½ cup heavy cream or other cream

steps: Switch to sauté mode and heat the oil until the display reads "hot." Add carrots, celery, and onion, stirring occasionally for 3–4 minutes until the onion softens.
Add the cubed chicken and cook while stirring for 3–4 minutes until the outside turns white.
Mix in parsley, salt, garlic, thyme, and pepper. Stir and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the broth and use a wooden spoon to deglaze the pot bottom. Add the dry pasta and stir to combine.
Lock the lid and switch the valve to sealing. Select Manual/High Pressure and set the timer for 2 minutes. The pot will take about 10 minutes to reach pressure before counting down.
When finished, turn off the pot. Allow natural pressure release for 5 minutes, then open the valve to release any remaining pressure.
Whisk the cream with corn starch until smooth. Stir this mixture and the spinach into the pot.
Cook and stir for 2–3 minutes until the soup reaches desired thickness. Adjust seasoning and serve.

notes: Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days; thaw overnight before reheating.
For dairy-free, replace heavy cream with full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream in the same amount.
Do not slice carrots too thin or they will disintegrate during cooking.
nutrition: Calories: 272 kcal · Carbs: 35 g · Protein: 16 g · Fat: 8 g · Sodium: 742 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Creamy Italian Chicken Breasts
description: Boneless chicken breasts cooked under pressure in a creamy sauce with roasted red peppers, herbs, and pesto. Ready in under 20 minutes.

items: ⅓ cup roasted red peppers, chopped

steps: Whisk together broth, red peppers, pesto, garlic, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
Optional: Switch to sauté mode. Once hot, add oil and sear chicken breasts 2 minutes per side until lightly browned. Avoid overcooking.
Place chicken on trivet (or skip trivet and cook directly in sauce) and pour the broth mixture around it.

Seal the lid and valve. Cook on high pressure for 6 minutes (add 2–3 minutes if chicken wasn't seared; add 4–5 minutes if frozen). The pot will take about 10 minutes to reach pressure before the timer starts.
When done, turn off heat and let pressure drop naturally for 10 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure and carefully open the lid.
Transfer chicken to a plate. Switch to sauté mode. If desired, strain broth to remove excess fat.
Whisk cream and cornstarch together, add to the pot, and stir constantly for 3–4 minutes until sauce thickens. Return chicken to pot or serve with sauce spooned over.

notes: Searing the chicken adds flavor but is optional; cooking time does not include sear time.
For a lighter version, substitute Greek yogurt for some or all of the heavy cream, stirring it in after pressure cooking to avoid curdling.
Refrigerate leftovers up to 3 days; reheat gently to keep sauce smooth.
nutrition: Calories: 237 kcal · Carbs: 6 g · Protein: 26 g · Fat: 12 g · Sodium: 495 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Dal Tadka with Basmati Rice
description: A simple everyday lentil curry made with split pigeon peas and tempered with cumin, curry leaves, and warm spices. Served alongside basmati rice.

ingredient_groups.name: Dal
items: 1 green chili pepper, sliced or chopped
1 cup split pigeon peas (toor dal), washed

steps: Switch the Instant Pot to sauté mode and warm the oil. Toast the cumin seeds until fragrant, then add the green chili, curry leaves, garlic, and asafoetida.
When the garlic is light brown, stir in the onion and cook for 2 minutes until golden.
Add the tomato and all spices (turmeric, coriander powder, red chili powder, and salt). Cook for 2 minutes.
Pour in the washed lentils and water, stirring to combine.

Set the trivet inside the pot. In a separate bowl, combine the basmati rice, water, ghee or oil, and salt (if using), then place this bowl on the trivet. Seal the lid with the vent in sealing position.
Cook at high pressure for 8 minutes. When done, allow the pressure to drop naturally for 10 minutes, then manually release any remaining pressure.
Using tongs, carefully remove the rice bowl, then lift out the trivet.
Stir the dal, garnish with cilantro, and serve alongside the rice.

notes: The dal and rice cook simultaneously using the trivet method; the rice bowl sits above the lentils.
Asafoetida is traditional but optional; omit it if keeping the dish gluten-free or if you dislike the ingredient.
For extra brightness, squeeze fresh lemon juice over the dal just before serving.
nutrition: Calories: 222 kcal · Carbs: 32 g · Protein: 13 g · Fat: 4 g · Sodium: 602 mg · Fiber: 15 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Garlic Herb Instant Pot Potatoes
description: Tender creamer potatoes cooked under pressure and finished with butter and dried herbs. A simple, flavorful side dish.

steps: Halve any potatoes larger than 1½ inches; leave smaller ones whole. Place all potatoes in the pressure cooker.
Add broth and 1 tsp salt.
Close the lid and cook on high pressure for 2 minutes. The pot will take about 10 minutes to pressurize before the timer starts.
When cooking finishes, allow natural pressure release for 5 minutes, then manually vent any remaining pressure.
Drain the cooking liquid. Stir in butter, remaining ½ tsp salt, parsley, garlic powder, thyme, and black pepper until the butter melts.
Taste and adjust salt and pepper as needed. Serve.

notes: Potatoes can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days; reheat gently on the stovetop with a splash of broth.
Fresh herbs (1 tbsp parsley, ½ tsp fresh thyme) can replace dried herbs if you prefer.
The 5-minute natural release ensures potatoes remain creamy rather than mushy.
nutrition: Calories: 112 kcal · Carbs: 18 g · Protein: 3 g · Fat: 3 g · Sodium: 494 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Green Moong Dal
description: A protein-rich mung bean curry simmered in onion and tomato gravy. Serve as a lentil soup alongside rice.

items: 1 cup green gram lentils (whole mung beans), rinsed
½ tablespoon ginger, grated or paste
½ tablespoon garlic, minced or paste

step_groups.name: Instant Pot Method
steps: Select sauté mode and heat ghee. Toast cumin seeds and green chili for 30 seconds.
Add onion, ginger, and garlic; sauté 3 minutes.
Stir in tomato and all spices.
Add lentils and water; stir well. Cancel sauté and seal the lid.
Pressure cook on high for 15 minutes. Allow pressure to release naturally.
Open lid, stir in lime juice and cilantro.

step_groups.name: Stovetop Pressure Cooker Method
steps: Heat ghee in the pressure cooker over medium-high heat. Toast cumin seeds and green chili for 30 seconds.
Add onion, ginger, and garlic; sauté 3 minutes.
Stir in tomato and all spices.
Add lentils and water; stir well. Cover and increase heat to high.
When two whistles sound, reduce heat to medium and cook 8 minutes more. Allow pressure to release naturally.
Open lid, stir in lime juice and cilantro.

notes: Rinse lentils thoroughly before cooking to remove debris.
For creamier texture, mash some lentils after cooking or blend partially.
Keeps refrigerated up to 4 days; reheat with extra water if needed.
nutrition: Calories: 245 kcal · Carbs: 40 g · Protein: 13 g · Fat: 5 g · Sodium: 644 mg · Fiber: 10 g · Sugar: 7 g

title: Honey Garlic Instant Pot Chicken Breasts
description: Tender chicken breasts cooked under pressure in a savory-sweet honey garlic sauce. Serve over rice or with vegetables for an easy weeknight meal.

items: ⅓ cup water or chicken broth
¼ cup low sodium soy sauce

steps: Whisk together the water (or broth), soy sauce, honey, minced garlic, and black pepper in the Instant Pot until the honey dissolves completely.
Place the chicken breasts into the pot with the sauce.
Lock the lid in place and set the steam valve to sealing.

Cook on high pressure for 8 minutes (small 7–8 oz breasts), 10 minutes (medium 10 oz breasts), or 11 minutes (large 12 oz breasts). For frozen chicken, add 3 minutes to the time above; ensure breasts are not clumped together.

Allow the cooker about 10 minutes to reach pressure, then the timer will count down. When done, quick-release the pressure by carefully moving the valve to venting with a long spoon handle (or use a 10-minute natural release for frozen chicken before venting). Wait for steam to clear and the pin to drop, then open the lid.
Check the chicken's internal temperature with a thermometer—it must reach 165°F. If below this, use the sauté function to finish cooking.
Transfer the chicken to a plate or cutting board.

Stir together the cornstarch and 1 tbsp water in a small bowl. Switch the Instant Pot to sauté mode, add the cornstarch slurry, and stir constantly until the sauce thickens, 2–3 minutes. Turn off the cooker and remove the inner pot to halt cooking.
Slice the chicken and serve with the sauce, rice, and vegetables as desired.

notes: Frozen chicken requires a natural release rather than quick release to ensure even cooking.
The internal temperature must reach 165°F for food safety; use a meat thermometer to verify.
Thickening the sauce at the end concentrates the honey garlic flavor and prevents a watery plate.
nutrition: Calories: 285 kcal · Carbs: 20 g · Protein: 40 g · Fat: 5 g · Sodium: 651 mg · Sugar: 18 g

title: Honey Sesame Instant Pot Chicken Thighs
description: Tender chicken thighs cooked in a sweet and savory honey sesame garlic sauce. Serve over rice for a complete meal.

steps: Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper on both sides.
Set pot to sauté and heat olive oil and 1 tsp sesame oil until hot.
Add chicken and sear 2–3 minutes per side until golden brown, then transfer to a plate.
Pour broth into pot and scrape up browned bits with a wooden spoon. Turn off heat.
In a bowl, whisk together remaining 1 tsp sesame oil, garlic, soy sauce, honey, ketchup, red pepper flakes, and ½ cup water.
Return chicken to pot and pour sauce over it.
Seal pot and cook on manual: 8 minutes for boneless thighs, 10 minutes for bone-in. Use quick release when done.
Transfer chicken to a plate and tent with foil to keep warm.
Mix cornstarch and cold water in a small bowl until smooth.
Set pot to sauté, add cornstarch slurry to sauce while whisking until thickened, then turn off heat.
Serve sauce over chicken with sesame seeds and green onion. Serve with rice.

notes: Boneless thighs cook in 8 minutes; bone-in thighs need 10 minutes—adjust accordingly.
The cornstarch slurry thickens the sauce to a glaze; whisk constantly to avoid lumps.
Leftovers keep refrigerated for 3–4 days and reheat well on the stovetop.

title: Instant Pot Applesauce
description: Unsweetened applesauce made with a blend of Granny Smith and Honeycrisp apples, ready in minutes under pressure.

items: 4 large Granny Smith apples, peeled, cored, and roughly chopped
4 large Honeycrisp apples, peeled, cored, and roughly chopped

steps: Peel, core, and chop all apples into rough pieces.
Pour water into the Instant Pot and add lemon juice and cinnamon. Stir to combine, then add apples and toss to coat.
Lock the lid and cook on high pressure for 8 minutes.
Perform a controlled quick release or let pressure release naturally.
Mash the cooked apples with a potato masher to your desired texture, or pulse in a blender for a smoother consistency.
Transfer to a bowl and cool completely. Taste and stir in additional cinnamon if desired.
Serve warm or cold. Refrigerate up to 7–10 days.

notes: For chunky applesauce, mash lightly; for smooth, use a blender.
Lemon juice prevents browning and brightens the flavor.
nutrition: Calories: 58 kcal · Carbs: 15 g · Sodium: 2 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 11 g

title: Instant Pot Baked Beans
description: Tender beans cooked from scratch with bacon, onion, and a tangy-sweet barbecue sauce. No soaking required.

items: 16 oz dry navy or pinto beans
½ red or green bell pepper, cored, seeded, and finely chopped

steps: Add beans, water, and salt to the pot. Cook on high pressure for 25 minutes, then let pressure release naturally.
Drain beans in a colander and rinse under cold water.
Switch to sauté mode. Cook bacon until starting to brown, breaking it apart and scraping the bottom frequently. Pour off excess grease.
Add bell pepper and onion to the pot and cook until softened.
Turn off sauté mode. Stir in barbecue sauce, ketchup, mustard, vinegar, and liquid smoke.
Add brown sugar, water, and drained beans; stir to combine.
Seal the lid and close the steam valve. Cook on high pressure for 15 minutes, then allow pressure to release naturally.
Open the lid carefully and gently stir to blend the sauce.

notes: A 6-quart Instant Pot Duo is the tested size; other models may trigger a burn notice with this recipe.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to five days.
Substitute the barbecue sauce with your preferred brand or homemade version to adjust the sauce profile.
nutrition: Calories: 195 kcal · Carbs: 29 g · Protein: 5 g · Fat: 6 g · Sodium: 585 mg · Fiber: 4 g · Sugar: 17 g

title: Instant Pot Baked Potatoes
description: Fluffy, tender potatoes cooked under pressure in the Instant Pot.

items: 1 cup cold water (or 1.5 cups for an 8-quart Instant Pot)

steps: Add 1 cup cold water to the inner pot and place the trivet inside. (Use 1.5 cups of water if using an 8-quart Instant Pot.)
Scrub the potatoes thoroughly and poke each one 5–6 times with a fork to allow steam to escape during cooking.
Carefully arrange potatoes on the trivet, stacking them if needed. Seal the lid and lock the valve.
Select HIGH pressure and set the cook time to 12 minutes for medium potatoes, 16 minutes for average to large, or 22 minutes for very large potatoes.
When cooking is complete, let pressure release naturally for at least 10 minutes, then perform a quick release. Remove potatoes and serve with desired toppings.
For crispy skin, toss cooled potatoes with olive oil and salt, spread on a baking sheet, and bake at 400°F for 10 minutes or broil 2–3 minutes per side until golden.

notes: Piercing potatoes prevents them from bursting under pressure.
Natural release time is essential for even cooking throughout.
Adjust cook time based on potato size to avoid undercooked or overcooked centers.
nutrition: Calories: 123 kcal · Carbs: 26 g · Protein: 5 g · Sodium: 21 mg · Fiber: 5 g

title: Instant Pot Baked Ziti
description: A one-pot whole wheat pasta and spinach dinner with marinara sauce and melted cheese. Vegetarian and ready in under 25 minutes.

items: 10 oz whole wheat pasta (ziti or cavatappi, about 3 cups)

steps: Select sauté mode. Once hot, add oil and garlic; cook 1 minute until golden.
Pour in water and salt, scraping the pot bottom to loosen any stuck garlic.
Stir in spinach and pasta.
Spread marinara sauce evenly over the pasta without stirring.
Cover and pressure cook on high for 7 minutes.
Quick release the pressure. Open the lid and stir. Dollop ricotta over the pasta, then sprinkle Pecorino and mozzarella on top.
Cover and let sit 3–4 minutes until cheese melts.

notes: Do not stir after adding marinara—this allows the pasta to cook properly under pressure.
For creamier results, stir the ricotta into the pasta before topping with hard cheeses.
Leftovers store covered in the refrigerator up to 3 days; reheat gently with a splash of water.
nutrition: Calories: 452 kcal · Carbs: 64 g · Protein: 24 g · Fat: 12.5 g · Sodium: 855.5 mg · Fiber: 10 g · Sugar: 6.5 g

title: Instant Pot Balsamic Pork Tenderloin
description: Tender pork loin cooked under pressure and finished with a rich balsamic glaze.

steps: Rub tenderloins with olive oil and season all sides with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and Italian herbs.
Switch cooker to sauté mode. Brown tenderloins on all sides, turning every 1–2 minutes, until well colored.
Whisk soy sauce, brown sugar, balsamic vinegar, and water together. Pour into pot around the tenderloins. Lock lid and close steam valve. Cook at high pressure for 15 minutes.
When done, allow natural release for 5 minutes, then vent steam and open lid when float valve drops. Transfer tenderloins to a cutting board.
Switch cooker to soup mode. Whisk cornstarch into 4 tbsp cold water until smooth, then stir into the cooking liquid. Continue stirring until glaze thickens.
Slice tenderloins and serve with glaze spooned over top.

notes: Extra glaze keeps refrigerated in a jar for up to 2 weeks.
Use 1 large tenderloin or 2 smaller ones depending on thickness and fit.
Dried oregano or thyme work if Italian blend is unavailable.
nutrition: Calories: 856 kcal · Carbs: 61 g · Protein: 96 g · Fat: 23 g · Sodium: 1268 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 58 g

title: Basmati Rice
description: Fluffy basmati rice cooked under pressure. Optionally soak beforehand for tender grains.

steps: Rinse rice under cold water until the water runs clear. If soaking, submerge in excess water for 1 hour, then drain and rinse.
Place rice and water in the pot. Stir in ghee and salt if using. Lock the lid, set to high pressure for 4 minutes with the valve in sealing position.
When the timer beeps, allow the pressure to release naturally for 5 minutes, then manually release any remaining pressure.
Gently fluff the rice with a fork or ladle. Let rest uncovered for 5 minutes before serving.

notes: Soaking is optional but yields softer, more tender grains.
Do not stir during cooking; this keeps the rice from turning mushy.
Store leftover rice in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 4 days.
nutrition: Calories: 359 kcal · Carbs: 73 g · Protein: 6 g · Fat: 3 g · Sodium: 592 mg · Fiber: 1 g

title: BBQ Pulled Pork
description: Tender, shredded pork shoulder cooked with cola and barbecue sauce until it falls apart easily. Serve on buns for a classic sandwich.

items: 3 lb pork shoulder, cut into 4 large chunks
1 can (12 oz) cola (Dr Pepper or root beer work too)

step_groups.name: Instant Pot
steps: Place pork chunks in a lightly greased Instant Pot and pour cola over them.
In a bowl, mix 2 cups barbecue sauce, brown sugar, vinegar, garlic powder, and onion powder. Pour this mixture over the pork.
Seal the lid and set to high pressure for 45 minutes.
Allow natural pressure release for 10 minutes, then move the vent to release remaining steam. Remove the lid once the float valve drops.
Using two forks, shred the pork into bite-sized pieces. Transfer shredded pork to a clean bowl using a slotted spoon, discarding the cooking liquid. Stir in the remaining ½ cup barbecue sauce.
Serve on hamburger buns with optional toppings like mayonnaise, extra sauce, or coleslaw.

step_groups.name: Slow Cooker
steps: Prepare ingredients as above. Place pork in a slow cooker, add cola, then pour the barbecue sauce mixture over top.
Cover and cook on low for 6 hours.
Shred, finish, and serve as directed in the Instant Pot method.

notes: For deeper flavor, brown the pork chunks in a skillet before adding to the pot.
Leftover pulled pork keeps refrigerated for up to 4 days and reheats well in the microwave or on the stovetop.
Any cola variety works; choose based on your preferred level of sweetness and flavor intensity.
nutrition: Calories: 605 kcal · Carbs: 89 g · Protein: 33 g · Fat: 12 g · Sodium: 1551 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 60 g

title: Instant Pot Dried Beans
description: Tender cooked dried beans without overnight soaking. Choose your bean type and cook on high pressure, then allow natural pressure release.

items: 1 pound dried beans, rinsed and sorted

steps: Add rinsed and sorted beans, water, onion, and bay leaf to the inner pot.
Secure the lid and set the valve to seal.

Cook on high pressure according to bean type: black beans 30 minutes, chickpeas 40 minutes, kidney beans 35 minutes, pinto beans 25 minutes, navy beans 25 minutes, butter beans 40 minutes, great northern beans 35 minutes, cannellini beans 35 minutes, large lima beans 22 minutes, baby lima beans 18 minutes, or red beans 30 minutes.
Let pressure release naturally for at least 20 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure.
Add salt and vinegar to the cooked beans. Refrigerate beans in their cooking liquid to maintain tenderness, or drain as preferred.

notes: Cooking times vary by bean size and age; older dried beans may need extra time.
Store in cooking liquid in the refrigerator for up to five days, or freeze for longer storage.
Rinsing and sorting remove debris and beans that float to the surface during soaking.
nutrition: Calories: 63 kcal · Carbs: 11 g · Protein: 4 g · Sodium: 200 mg · Fiber: 2 g

title: Instant Pot Beef Bourguignon
description: A French-style braise of tender beef chunks with mushrooms, potatoes, and root vegetables in a rich wine and beef broth sauce.

items: 3 pounds chuck beef, cut into 2-inch pieces
1 cup red cooking wine (or chicken broth as substitute)
2 tablespoons fresh thyme, chopped (or 3 teaspoons dried thyme)

steps: Turn on the sauté setting. Heat oil and cook chopped bacon for 3–4 minutes until crispy. Transfer to a paper towel with a slotted spoon.
Add beef pieces to the pot and sear for 3–4 minutes, turning with tongs to brown all surfaces.

Pour in red wine and use a wooden spoon to scrape up the browned bits stuck to the pot bottom and sides. Simmer for about 5 minutes, then add beef broth, tomato sauce, and crushed bouillon cube. Whisk in the flour gradually.
Add the cooked bacon back to the pot along with carrots, onion, garlic, thyme, potatoes, mushrooms, salt, and pepper. Lock the lid and set to high pressure for 45 minutes.

When cooking is done, allow the pressure to release naturally for 10 minutes, then carefully turn the valve to vent for a quick release. Once the float valve drops, remove the lid. Switch back to sauté mode and simmer uncovered for 5–10 minutes to thicken the sauce.
Stir well and taste, adjusting salt and pepper as needed. Serve garnished with parsley.

notes: The sauce should coat the back of a spoon; if it's too thin after the sauté step, continue cooking for another few minutes.
You can substitute chicken broth for the wine if you prefer, maintaining the same total liquid amount.
nutrition: Calories: 581 kcal · Carbs: 24 g · Protein: 51 g · Fat: 32 g · Sodium: 1135 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 4 g

title: Instant Pot Beef Tips
description: Tender beef chunks braised in a savory mushroom and Worcestershire gravy. A hearty main dish ready in under an hour.

items: 2 pounds beef stew meat or top sirloin, cut into 1-inch pieces
½ teaspoon cracked black pepper (or ¼ teaspoon ground pepper)
Rice, mashed potatoes, or egg noodles for serving

steps: Select sauté mode. Heat oil, then cook onion and garlic until the onion softens and garlic becomes fragrant, about 2–3 minutes.
Add beef pieces and sear for about 1 minute. Stir in broth, Worcestershire sauce, mushrooms, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper.
Lock the lid, set valve to seal, and cook on high pressure for 35 minutes.
After cooking, allow pressure to release naturally for 10 minutes, then turn valve to vent for quick release. Once the float valve drops, remove the lid and switch to sauté mode.
Bring the liquid to a boil. Whisk cornstarch and cold water together until smooth, then stir the slurry into the pot.
Simmer until the gravy thickens. Taste and adjust salt and pepper as needed. Serve over rice, mashed potatoes, or egg noodles.

notes: For a thicker gravy, increase cornstarch to 4 tablespoons; for thinner, reduce to 2 tablespoons.
Stew meat or chuck roast will yield more tender results than sirloin.
This dish reheats well; refrigerate up to 4 days or freeze up to 3 months.
nutrition: Calories: 566 kcal · Carbs: 40 g · Protein: 60 g · Fat: 17 g · Sodium: 2362 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 4 g

title: Instant Pot Brisket
description: A savory braised brisket cooked under pressure with onions and a tangy sauce. The meat becomes tender and absorbs deep, complex flavors without heating up your kitchen.

items: 2 pounds beef brisket, cut into 4 pieces against the grain
1 tbsp prepared mustard, plus more to taste
½ tsp xanthan gum (or cornstarch slurry for thickening)

step_groups.name: Pressure Cooker
steps: Season the brisket pieces generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
In a small bowl, whisk together the water, tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, liquid smoke, and mustard.
Scatter the sliced onions across the bottom of the Instant Pot liner, then arrange the seasoned brisket pieces on top.
Pour the sauce mixture evenly over the brisket.
Close the lid, set to high pressure, and cook for 35 minutes for medium tenderness, 40–45 minutes for very tender meat, or 60 minutes if you plan to shred it.
When done, carefully open the lid. Remove the brisket with tongs and set aside on a cutting board.
Using an immersion blender, puree the onions and cooking liquid in the pot until smooth. Stir in additional mustard to taste.
Switch the pot to sauté mode. Whisk in the xanthan gum (or a cornstarch slurry made with 1 tbsp cornstarch and 2 tbsp cold water) and simmer, stirring often, until the sauce thickens.
Slice the brisket against the grain and serve with the sauce spooned over top.

step_groups.name: Slow Cooker Alternative
steps: Follow all steps above, but transfer the seasoned brisket, onions, and sauce to a slow cooker. Cook on low for 8–9 hours, then proceed with pureeing and thickening the sauce as directed.

notes: Cook time varies by desired tenderness: 35 min for some chew, 40–45 min for tender slices, or 60 min for shreddable meat.
Make a cornstarch slurry by mixing 1 tbsp cornstarch with 2 tbsp cold water if you don't have xanthan gum.
Leftover brisket and sauce keeps covered in the refrigerator for up to 4 days.
nutrition: Calories: 200 kcal · Carbs: 5 g · Protein: 24 g · Fat: 8 g · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Instant Pot Broccoli Cheddar Soup
description: A creamy broccoli and cheddar soup made with broccoli, carrots, and cheese. Ready in under an hour with tender vegetables and a smooth, rich texture.

items: 3 cups broccoli, cut into small pieces

steps: Switch the Instant Pot to sauté mode and melt 2 tbsp butter. Cook the diced onion and minced garlic for 2 minutes.
Add the broccoli, carrot, black pepper, red chili flakes, salt, and broth. Stir to combine. Press Cancel and seal the lid with the vent in sealing position.
Cook on high pressure for 5 minutes.
When the timer beeps, allow pressure to release naturally. Open the lid.

step_groups.name: Stovetop roux
steps: While pressure releases, melt 2 tbsp butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in the flour and cook for 1 minute until no raw flour taste remains. Gradually whisk in the milk until you reach a smooth, thick consistency.

step_groups.name: Finish
steps: Pour the roux into the Instant Pot and stir continuously. Gradually add the grated cheddar cheese, stirring until fully melted.
Switch to sauté mode and simmer for a couple of minutes.
Ladle into bowls and serve with crusty bread, garnished with extra cheese, black pepper, or red chili flakes as desired.

notes: The roux can be made while the pot depressurizes naturally, saving active time.
Sharp cheddar provides more robust flavor than mild; adjust cheese amount to taste preference.
Soup keeps refrigerated up to 4 days; reheat gently on stovetop with a splash of milk if needed.
nutrition: Calories: 446 kcal · Carbs: 18 g · Protein: 21 g · Fat: 33 g · Sodium: 560 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 7 g

title: Instant Pot Brown Rice
description: Perfectly cooked brown rice made in the pressure cooker without guesswork. Ready in about 20 minutes with a natural release.

items: 1 cup (200 g) long grain brown rice

steps: Combine 200 g brown rice and 250 ml cold water in the Instant Pot. Ensure the venting knob is in the venting position, then seal the lid and switch the knob to sealing position. Set to high pressure for 15 minutes.
When the timer ends, let pressure release naturally for 5 minutes, then manually release remaining pressure by turning the venting knob to venting position. Carefully open the lid.
Use a fork or rice paddle to fluff the grains. If moisture remains on the surface, allow it to sit briefly before serving.

notes: Adjust cooking time to 12–18 minutes depending on your rice brand and doneness preference.
If rice seems wet after cooking, leave uncovered for a few minutes to let steam escape.
nutrition: Calories: 344 kcal · Carbs: 72.4 g · Protein: 7.1 g · Fat: 2.5 g · Sodium: 7 mg · Fiber: 3.2 g

title: Instant Pot Carnitas
description: Tender, juicy pork shoulder cooked under pressure, then crisped until browned. Serve in tortillas with salsa for authentic Mexican-style pulled pork.

items: 3¼ pounds pork shoulder, cut into 2–2¼ inch cubes
½–1 tsp cinnamon powder (or 1 cinnamon stick)

steps: Remove the rind and bone from the pork shoulder, then cut the meat into 2–2¼ inch cubes as evenly as you can. Reserve any excess fat.

Place the pork cubes and reserved fat into the Instant Pot. Season generously with kosher salt and black pepper. Add the 4 crushed garlic cloves, chopped onion, 1 tsp ground cumin, ½–1 tsp cinnamon powder, 2 bay leaves, 1 tbsp soy sauce, and 55 ml orange juice. Stir to combine and let sit for 20 minutes.

Pour 200 ml chicken stock into the pot. Seal the lid and set the venting knob to Sealing. Cook on high pressure for 30 minutes, then allow natural release for 15 minutes before venting remaining pressure manually.

Taste the pork and adjust the salt in the cooking liquid as needed. Spread the pork on a baking tray and brush each piece with some of the cooking liquid. If the liquid lacks fat, whisk together 63 ml of the cooking liquid with 2 tbsp vegetable oil and brush that mixture over the pork instead to prevent drying and aid crisping.

Position the tray under a broiler or on the top rack of a 450°F toaster oven. Broil until the surface browns, about 8–10 minutes. Turn the pieces over, brush with more cooking liquid, and return to the oven until the second side crisps.
While the pork browns, warm the corn tortillas if desired.
Serve the hot carnitas on warm tortillas with salsa and lime.

notes: Keep the fat on the pork cubes during cooking—it renders into flavor and aids crisping.
The cooking liquid should be mostly fat; if it isn't, augment it with vegetable oil before basting.
Naturally releasing pressure for 15 minutes prevents splattering and helps the meat stay juicy.
nutrition: Calories: 373 kcal · Carbs: 3 g · Protein: 29.3 g · Fat: 26.4 g · Sodium: 165 mg · Fiber: 0.3 g · Sugar: 1.4 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken and Dumplings
description: Tender bone-in chicken thighs and fluffy homemade dumplings simmered in a savory broth with potatoes, carrots, celery, and peas. A classic comfort dish ready in about an hour.

ingredient_groups.name: For Browning and Cooking
items: 1 tablespoon (14g) unsalted butter or olive oil

ingredient_groups.name: Vegetables
items: ½ to 1 cup (60–120g) frozen green peas

ingredient_groups.name: Seasonings and Aromatics
items: Kosher salt and ground black pepper
Fish sauce or salt for final seasoning

ingredient_groups.name: Dumpling Dough
items: Fresh parsley or chives (optional), finely chopped

step_groups.name: Prepare Dumplings and Brown Chicken
steps: While preheating the Instant Pot on Sauté More until it displays HOT, prepare the dumpling dough in a bowl by whisking together 2 cups (250g) flour and 1 tablespoon (12g) baking powder. In a separate container, combine ¾ cup (188ml) warm milk, 1 teaspoon (5g) salt, 1 tablespoon (15ml) olive oil, and 2 tablespoons (28g) melted butter. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until just combined without overworking.

Pat the chicken thighs completely dry. Season the skin side generously with kosher salt and black pepper. Add 1 tablespoon (14g) butter to the hot pot, coating the entire bottom. Place 4 thighs skin-side down and sear for 3.5 minutes until golden. Season the exposed side with salt and pepper, flip, and cook for 2 minutes. Transfer to a plate and repeat with the remaining 4 thighs.

step_groups.name: Build the Base
steps: Add the chopped onion to the pot with a pinch of salt and pepper, sautéing for about 2.5 minutes until softened. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add the chopped carrots and celery, cooking for 2 minutes. Sprinkle in the bay leaves and dried thyme, cooking 1 minute more to release the aroma.
Pour in 1 cup (250ml) of the chicken stock and scrape the bottom and sides of the pot vigorously with a wooden spoon to lift all the browned bits. Stir once to combine.

step_groups.name: Cook Under Pressure
steps: Add the potato chunks, the remaining 4 cups (1000ml) of chicken stock, and the browned chicken thighs along with all their accumulated juices. Using a teaspoon, carefully drop spoonfuls of dumpling batter onto the surface of the liquid; they will expand to roughly double their size during cooking. Ensure the lid is securely closed with the vent knob in the Sealing position. Set to High Pressure for 3 minutes, then allow natural pressure release for 15 minutes. After 15 minutes, slowly release any remaining pressure by gradually turning the vent knob until the float valve drops and no steam escapes.

step_groups.name: Finish and Serve
steps: Carefully transfer the cooked dumplings to a bowl and set aside. Working in the pot, shred the chicken meat into bite-sized pieces, discarding the skin and bones.

Return the pot to Sauté mode and bring the soup to a gentle simmer. Add ½ to 1 cup (60–120g) frozen peas and stir gently. Using a spoon, partially mash some potato pieces against the pot wall to thicken the broth. In a separate bowl, whisk together ½ cup (62.5g) flour with several ladles of hot soup until the flour dissolves completely. Slowly stir this mixture back into the pot in thirds, stirring well between each addition until you reach the desired thickness.

Return the shredded chicken and dumplings to the pot and mix thoroughly. Adjust the seasoning with fish sauce or additional salt to taste. Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh parsley; serve with heavy cream drizzled in if desired.

notes: The dumpling batter should be thick and slightly sticky—do not overmix or they will become tough.
Natural release for 15 minutes is key to keeping the dumplings intact; skip the quick-release step.
Refrigerate leftovers up to 3 days; reheat gently on Sauté mode, adding water or broth to restore consistency.
nutrition: Calories: 578 kcal · Carbs: 51 g · Protein: 29 g · Fat: 28 g · Sodium: 496 mg · Fiber: 4 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken and Potatoes
description: Seasoned chicken breasts and baby potatoes cooked together under pressure. Ranch and herb flavors combine with parmesan for a simple, one-pot dinner.

items: 2 pounds baby red or gold potatoes

steps: Coat chicken and potatoes with olive oil in a large bowl. In a small cup, mix garlic powder, thyme, basil, oregano, and 2 tablespoons Ranch seasoning. Sprinkle over chicken and potatoes and toss until evenly coated.
Pour chicken broth into the Instant Pot. Arrange chicken in the pot, then pile potatoes on top. Lock the lid and set the vent to sealed.
Cook on high pressure for 10–15 minutes.

Once cooking ends, quick-release the pressure by moving the vent to venting. After the float valve drops, carefully remove the lid. Transfer chicken and potatoes to a serving platter, draining excess liquid if desired.
Sprinkle with remaining 2 teaspoons Ranch seasoning and parmesan cheese. Garnish with fresh thyme or parsley if desired.

notes: Thicker chicken breasts may need the full 15 minutes; thinner ones cook in 10 minutes.
For less sodium, use homemade seasoning blend instead of packaged Ranch mix.
Leftovers keep refrigerated for up to three days and reheat well in the microwave.
nutrition: Calories: 419 kcal · Carbs: 41 g · Protein: 30 g · Fat: 15 g · Sodium: 1290 mg · Fiber: 5 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken and Sausage Jambalaya
description: A one-pot Cajun rice dish with andouille sausage, chicken, and vegetables. Cooked under pressure for an easy weeknight meal.

items: 12 oz andouille sausages, cut into ¼ in. thick slices
1 boneless skinless chicken breast, cut into small pieces
1 green bell pepper, seeded and chopped

steps: Set the pot to sauté mode. Heat 1 tbsp oil until shimmering. Add sausage slices and brown on both sides, about 2–3 minutes per side. Remove to a paper towel–lined plate.
Add the remaining 1 tbsp oil to the pot.
Add chicken and cook for 1 minute, scraping up any browned sausage residue. Switch off the sauté function. Stir in onion, green pepper, celery, and garlic; cook 1 minute more.
Mix in Cajun seasoning, basil, thyme, sugar, and rice until combined.
Pour in diced tomatoes with their juices, broth, and salt.
Lock the lid and move the pressure valve to sealing.
Cook on high pressure for 5 minutes. Allow natural release for 5 minutes, then quick release any remaining pressure.
Open the lid carefully and fluff rice with a fork. Return sausages to the pot on top of the rice. Replace the lid and let rest 5 minutes. Stir gently before serving.

notes: For spicier jambalaya, increase Cajun seasoning to 2½ tsp or add a pinch of cayenne.
Leftovers keep refrigerated for up to 4 days; reheat on the stove with a splash of broth to restore texture.
nutrition: Calories: 588 kcal · Carbs: 54 g · Protein: 30 g · Fat: 27 g · Sodium: 1062 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 4 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken Breast and Rice
description: Boneless chicken breasts and long-grain white rice cooked together under pressure with Italian seasonings, garlic, and paprika, finished with lemon juice.

ingredient_groups.name: Rice and broth
items: ½ teaspoon dried Italian blend seasoning

steps: Switch pot to sauté mode with 1 tablespoon oil. Mix the dried Italian seasoning, garlic powder, onion powder, and paprika in a small bowl. Coat chicken breasts with remaining 1 tablespoon oil, season with salt and pepper, then rub all over with the spice mixture. Brown chicken in the pot for 1–2 minutes per side, then set aside on a plate.
Add butter to the pot and let melt. Stir in minced garlic, then add the rinsed rice along with the remaining ½ teaspoon Italian seasoning and salt and pepper. Pour in the broth and stir to combine.

Place the steam rack in the pot with handles up. Arrange chicken breasts on the rack. Cover, lock the lid, and cook at high pressure for 5 minutes. Allow a natural pressure release for 5 minutes, then carefully remove the lid. Transfer chicken to a plate.
Use a fork to fluff the rice, taste, and adjust salt and pepper as needed. Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the chicken and rice before serving.

notes: Rinsing the rice removes excess starch and helps prevent mushiness.
Natural release lets the rice finish absorbing liquid without overcooking; quick release is acceptable but may leave rice slightly wetter.
For smoked paprika, use the same amount for a deeper flavor.
nutrition: Calories: 502 kcal · Carbs: 39 g · Protein: 52 g · Fat: 14 g · Sodium: 596 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken Breasts
