description: Tender boneless skinless chicken breasts cooked under pressure in 8–12 minutes, ready to serve whole or shred.

items: 3 lbs boneless skinless chicken breasts (fresh or frozen)
1 cup liquid (water, chicken broth, apple juice, or pineapple juice)

steps: Set the inner pot into the pressure cooker base and place the trivet on the bottom.
Pour 1 cup of your chosen liquid into the pot.
Arrange chicken breasts in a single layer on the trivet and season with salt, pepper, and any other seasonings you prefer.
Lock the lid and move the steam valve to the sealed position.
Cook on high pressure for 8–10 minutes if using fresh chicken, or 10–12 minutes if starting with frozen (adjust based on thickness; cooking time remains constant regardless of quantity).
Let pressure drop naturally for 5 minutes once the timer sounds, then switch the valve to venting to clear remaining steam.

Check internal temperature with a meat thermometer—it should read 165°F. If below that, replace the lid and rest for 5 minutes; if still below 160°F, seal and cook 1–3 minutes longer on high pressure.
Allow chicken to rest 5 minutes before slicing or shredding.
To make shredded chicken: take out the trivet and keep about ½ cup liquid in the pot; return the rested chicken to the pot with the liquid and use a handheld mixer to shred quickly.

notes: Fresh and frozen chicken cook at the same time; the pot just takes longer to pressurize with frozen.
Resting after cooking helps retain moisture whether you slice or shred.
For shredded chicken, a handheld mixer makes the job fast and effortless.
nutrition: Calories: 388 kcal · Carbs: 1 g · Protein: 72 g · Fat: 9 g · Sodium: 979 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken Congee
description: A comforting rice porridge made with chicken drumsticks, ginger, and minimal ingredients. Perfect for cold days or when you need something soothing.

steps: Place 173 g rice in the Instant Pot and wash under cold running water, rubbing the grains gently between your fingertips in circular motions. Drain and repeat until the rinse water runs clear.

Add the ginger strips, 5 to 6 drumsticks, and 6.5 to 7 cups cold water to the pot. Ensure the venting knob is in the venting position, then seal the lid and switch the knob to sealing position. Cook at high pressure for 30 minutes, then allow natural pressure release. Carefully open the lid. Do not add salt before cooking.
Switch to sauté mode to heat the congee. Stir continuously until you reach your preferred thickness, then season with salt to taste.
Using tongs and a fork, pull the chicken meat from the bones and discard the bones and skin if desired. Shred the meat into bite-sized pieces if preferred.
Turn off the heat, top with green onions, and serve at once.

notes: Rice-to-water ratio is approximately 1 cup rice to 9 to 9.75 cups water; this recipe uses ¾ cup rice with 6.5 to 7 cups water.
Omit salt during pressure cooking; add it only after sautéing to control the final flavor.
Shredding the chicken is optional; leave it whole or partially separated based on your preference.
nutrition: Calories: 186 kcal · Carbs: 19 g · Protein: 13 g · Fat: 6 g · Sodium: 80 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken Marsala
description: Tender chicken breasts cooked with mushrooms in a marsala wine and cream sauce. Ready in under 30 minutes.

items: 1½ tsp Italian seasoning or Herbs de Provence
¼ tsp pepper, or to taste

steps: Season chicken breasts on both sides with salt, pepper, and 1 tsp Italian seasoning. Switch pot to sauté mode and melt 2 tbsp butter with 2 tsp oil.
Sear chicken 2–3 minutes per side, then transfer to a plate.
Melt remaining 1 tbsp butter in pot, add garlic and mushrooms, sauté 1–2 minutes until fragrant, then stir in remaining ½ tsp Italian seasoning.
Pour in chicken broth and marsala wine, then return chicken to pot.
Lock lid in sealed position with vent turned to seal.
Cook on high pressure for 8 minutes.
Quick release pressure by moving vent to venting position. Once float valve drops, remove lid. Transfer chicken to a plate and cover.

Switch pot to soup mode. Season with 1 tsp salt and ¼ tsp pepper. Whisk cold water and cornstarch together until smooth, then whisk into pot. Turn off heat once sauce thickens. Stir in heavy cream if using.
Pour mushroom sauce over chicken and serve.

notes: Pound chicken breasts to even thickness for consistent cooking.
Use low-sodium broth to control salt level; marsala wine reduces during cooking.
Serve over rice, pasta, or mashed potatoes to catch the sauce.
nutrition: Calories: 491 kcal · Carbs: 19 g · Protein: 50 g · Fat: 16 g · Sodium: 1086 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 6 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken Stock
description: A quick chicken stock made from bones and kitchen scraps, enriched with vegetables and herbs.

items: 1 chicken carcass or roasted chicken bones

steps: If using a strainer insert, place it in the inner pot.
Add chicken bones, carrot, onions, celery, thyme, salt, peppercorns, and bay leaves to the strainer insert or directly into the pot.
Pour water over everything, filling to no more than two-thirds of the pot's capacity.
Seal the lid and ensure the vent knob is set to sealed.
Set the cooker to high pressure for 45 minutes.
After cooking, naturally release pressure for at least 30 minutes.
Strain the stock through a colander and let cool slightly before storing.
Once chilled, remove any solidified fat layer from the surface with a spoon and discard.

notes: For bone broth, increase pressure time to 120 minutes.
Store in airtight containers; stock keeps refrigerated for up to 5 days or frozen for several months.
Do not fill the pot beyond two-thirds capacity to prevent foaming and ensure safe pressure release.
nutrition: Calories: 1 kcal · Carbs: 1 g · Protein: 1 g · Fat: 1 g · Sodium: 591 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken Taco Bowls
description: A one-pot dinner combining chicken breasts, rice, black beans, corn, and salsa cooked together under pressure, then shredded and served in bowls with fresh toppings.

items: 15 oz can black beans, rinsed and drained
1¼ cups long-grain white rice, rinsed and drained

steps: Coat the pot with non-stick cooking spray.
Pour ½ cup broth into the bottom.
Lay chicken breasts in the pot.
Season the chicken evenly with taco seasoning.
Add the rinsed beans and corn.
Stir in the salsa.
Add the rinsed rice and remaining 1 cup broth.
Use a spoon to submerge the rice completely in liquid.
Lock the lid and set to Manual (high pressure) for 8 minutes.
Let pressure drop naturally for 12 minutes, then move the valve to quick release.
Open the lid and fluff the rice gently with a fork without stirring.
Replace the lid (unplugged) for 5 minutes while you prepare toppings.
Push the rice aside to locate the chicken, remove it, and shred with two forks.
Divide the rice mixture among bowls, top each with shredded chicken and desired toppings, and serve with tortillas if desired.

notes: Don't stir the rice after cooking—fluff only with a fork to keep grains separate.
You can substitute brown rice, but increase cook time to 15 minutes and allow natural pressure release for 15 minutes.
Leftovers keep refrigerated for up to 3 days; reheat gently with a splash of water.
nutrition: Calories: 299 kcal · Carbs: 52 g · Protein: 17 g · Fat: 2 g · Sodium: 804 mg · Fiber: 7 g · Sugar: 3 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken Thighs
description: Tender, juicy chicken thighs cooked under pressure in under 30 minutes. Works with fresh or frozen meat.

items: 2–3 lbs chicken thighs (skin on, fresh or frozen)
1 cup liquid (water, chicken broth, apple juice, or pineapple juice)

steps: If using fresh thighs, set the pot to sauté, add oil, season the chicken, and brown 3–4 minutes per side until the skin is crispy. Remove and set aside. Skip this step for frozen thighs.
Pour 1 cup of your chosen liquid into the pot and scrape the bottom to loosen any browned bits.
Place the trivet in the pot and arrange the chicken on top. Lock the lid and set the vent to sealed.
For boneless thighs, cook on high pressure for 8 minutes (fresh) or 12 minutes (frozen). For bone-in thighs, cook on high pressure for 10 minutes (fresh) or 15 minutes (frozen).

Release pressure naturally for 5 minutes, then open the vent to release remaining steam. Check that the chicken reaches 165°F. If it hasn't, reseal the lid and let it rest 5 minutes; if still undercooked, seal and cook 1–3 minutes more on high pressure.
Let the chicken rest 5 minutes before serving.

notes: Store leftovers in the refrigerator for 3–4 days or freeze for up to 3 months.
Use any liquid—water, broth, or juice—to flavor the chicken and create a flavorful cooking environment.
Always verify doneness with a meat thermometer; 165°F is the safe internal temperature.
nutrition: Calories: 266 kcal · Carbs: 1 g · Protein: 35 g · Fat: 13 g · Sodium: 629 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken Tikka Masala
description: A creamy Indian curry with tender chicken in a tomato-coconut sauce, finished with Greek yogurt and peas. Made quickly under pressure.

items: 2 pounds boneless skinless chicken breasts, cut into bite-size pieces
½ cup plain nonfat Greek yogurt
Prepared brown rice or naan, for serving

steps: Rub the chicken pieces with 1 tsp of the salt and reserve.

Set the Instant Pot to sauté mode and melt the butter. Add the onion, garlic, ginger, garam masala, chili powder, cumin, turmeric, and cayenne; stir continuously for about 5 minutes until the onion becomes tender and the spice mixture releases its aroma.

Add the chicken pieces and fold to incorporate the spice-onion mixture, then sauté for about 4 minutes until the surfaces begin to brown. Pour in the tomato sauce and the remaining ½ tsp salt; mix well. Seal the lid and pressurize on high for 8 minutes, then immediately vent to release pressure.

Open the pot and stir in the coconut milk. Switch back to sauté mode, bring to a gentle simmer while stirring from time to time, and allow to reduce for 10 to 15 minutes until the sauce becomes thicker. Disable the sauté function and fold in the peas. Wait 3 to 4 minutes for the mixture to cool slightly, then swirl in the yogurt carefully to avoid curdling. Serve warm over rice or naan topped with cilantro.

notes: Do not add Greek yogurt while the sauce is actively hot; cool slightly first to prevent curdling.
For a spicier dish, increase the cayenne to taste.
Keeps well refrigerated for 3 days and freezes up to 3 months (add yogurt when reheating if frozen).
nutrition: Calories: 355 kcal · Carbs: 32 g · Protein: 36 g · Fat: 10 g · Fiber: 4 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken Tortilla Soup
description: A quick Mexican-style chicken tortilla soup made by blending fresh vegetables into a rich base, then pressure cooking chicken until tender. Top with cheese, avocado, tortilla strips, and your choice of garnishes.

items: 1 chipotle chile in adobo sauce with 1 tsp sauce
¼ cup chopped cilantro or parsley
2 corn tortillas, cut into small pieces (optional)

steps: Blend onion, tomatoes, garlic, chipotle chile with sauce, jalapeño, salt, and cilantro until smooth.
Set the Instant Pot to sauté mode and heat the oil until shimmering.
Pour the blended vegetable mixture into the pot and stir frequently for 10 minutes until noticeably thickened.
Add 4 cups water and the corn tortillas if using them for body.
Cut the chicken breasts into large pieces and add to the pot.
Lock the lid, set to high pressure for 20 minutes, then allow pressure to release naturally for 10 minutes before fully venting any remaining pressure.
Remove the chicken, shred it, return it to the pot, and ladle into bowls. Serve with cheese, avocado slices, tortilla strips, and other toppings.

notes: Shredding the chicken after cooking lets you control texture and remove it easily from the hot broth.
The blended vegetable base thickens significantly during sautéing, creating authentic depth of flavor.
Garnish generously—the toppings (cheese, avocado, sour cream, crispy tortilla strips) are essential to the dish.
nutrition: Calories: 208 kcal · Carbs: 10 g · Protein: 25 g · Fat: 7 g · Sodium: 782 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Instant Pot Cold Start Yogurt
description: Homemade yogurt made with just milk and a yogurt starter culture, incubated in the Instant Pot without heating. Requires minimal hands-on time.

items: 2 tablespoons prepared yogurt with live cultures (or yogurt starter)

steps: Combine 1 cup of the milk with the 2 tablespoons of yogurt starter and whisk until smooth.
Pour all 52 ounces of milk into the Instant Pot inner pot and stir in the yogurt-milk mixture.
Set the lid on the pot without sealing it.
Press the yogurt button, then adjust to reach 8:00 on the display, confirming the pressure setting shows normal (not low); press yogurt again if it defaults to low.
Let the mixture incubate undisturbed for 8 hours.
Remove the inner pot, cover tightly with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for 8 hours.
Transfer the finished yogurt to glass containers and refrigerate for up to 10–14 days.

notes: Ultra-filtered milk produces the thickest yogurt; standard ultra-pasteurized milk works but may be thinner.
Do not seal the lid during incubation; the pot must remain open to maintain proper temperature.
Longer refrigeration allows yogurt to thicken further as it cools.
nutrition: Calories: 70 kcal · Carbs: 4 g · Protein: 8 g · Fat: 3 g · Sodium: 69 mg · Sugar: 3 g

title: Instant Pot Corned Beef and Cabbage
description: A tender corned beef brisket cooked with cabbage, potatoes, and carrots in one pot. A classic Irish-American dinner.

items: 6 carrots, peeled and cut into 2½-inch lengths

steps: Rinse the corned beef thoroughly under cold water.

Place the rinsed corned beef, quartered onion, crushed garlic, mustard seeds, peppercorns, coriander seeds, allspice, cloves, bay leaves, optional ginger, and 4 cups cold water into the Instant Pot. Lock the lid and set the valve to sealing. Pressure cook on high for 70 minutes, then allow pressure to release naturally for about 15 minutes. If the float valve hasn't dropped after 15 minutes, manually release any remaining pressure by switching the valve to venting. Carefully open the lid.

While the pot is releasing pressure, prepare the vegetables. Remove the cooked corned beef and reserve 750 ml (about 1½ to 2 cups) of the cooking liquid in a serving bowl. Leave the remaining liquid in the pot and add the quartered potatoes, carrots, and cabbage wedges. Lock the lid and set the valve to sealing. Pressure cook on high for 2–3 minutes, then quick-release by switching the valve to venting. Carefully open the lid.
Slice the corned beef against the grain into ⅛-inch-thick slices. Serve with the cabbage wedges, potatoes, and carrots alongside the reserved cooking liquid.

notes: Rinsing the corned beef removes excess salt and the curing spices that come in the package.
If you prefer more tender vegetables, extend the vegetable pressure-cook time to 4 minutes.
Leftover corned beef reheats well; store sliced meat with cooking liquid for up to 4 days.
nutrition: Calories: 452 kcal · Carbs: 35 g · Protein: 28 g · Fat: 23 g · Sodium: 1948 mg · Fiber: 9 g · Sugar: 11 g

title: Instant Pot Country Style Ribs
description: Tender pork country-style ribs rubbed with warm spices, braised in broth and barbecue sauce under pressure.

ingredient_groups.name: Ribs & Cooking Liquid
items: ¾ cup beef broth (water can be substituted)
½ cup barbecue sauce, plus more for serving

steps: Combine brown sugar, salt, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, pepper, cumin, and cayenne in a small bowl. Rub the mixture evenly onto both sides of the ribs.
Pour beef broth into the Instant Pot. Arrange ribs inside, then pour barbecue sauce over them.
Seal the lid and cook on high pressure for 45 minutes. Allow natural pressure release for 15–20 minutes.
Transfer ribs to a serving platter and serve with additional barbecue sauce on the side.

notes: The ribs can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 4 days, or frozen for up to 3 months.
For extra tenderness, allow a full 20-minute natural release; quick release will yield slightly firmer meat.
nutrition: Calories: 273 kcal

title: Instant Pot Egg Bites
description: Light and fluffy egg bites made with cottage cheese and your choice of add-ins. They're protein-rich, quick to prepare, and easily customizable.

items: ⅓ cup cottage cheese (full-fat or reduced-fat, not fat-free)
¼ cup shredded cheese (cheddar, feta, or similar)
⅓ cup fillings of choice (cooked meat, vegetables, etc.)

steps: Combine 4 eggs, ⅓ cup cottage cheese, ¼ cup cheese, salt, and ¼ tsp pepper in a blender. Process on low speed until smooth, then tap the blender on the counter to release trapped air.
Coat egg molds with nonstick spray. If using fillings, distribute ⅓ cup among the molds, then pour blended mixture over them, filling each cavity to about ¾ full. Seal the mold tightly with foil.
Add 1½ cups cold water to the inner pot and insert the metal rack. Position the mold(s) on the rack, stacking if necessary.
Seal the lid and ensure the vent is closed. Set to high pressure for 9 minutes using the manual or pressure cook button.
When the timer beeps, allow pressure to drop naturally for at least 10 minutes without intervention.
Carefully remove the mold from the pot and let it sit for 2 to 5 minutes, then turn out onto a plate.

notes: Use full-fat or reduced-fat cottage cheese; fat-free versions won't yield the same creamy texture.
The egg bites can be made ahead and reheated in the microwave for quick weekday breakfasts.
Cooked bacon, diced peppers, spinach, and mushrooms are all reliable mix-in options.
nutrition: Calories: 51 kcal · Carbs: 1 g · Protein: 5 g · Fat: 3 g · Sodium: 83 mg · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Ground Beef Pasta
description: A one-pot creamy pasta with seasoned ground beef and marinara sauce. Ready in half the time of stovetop cooking.

items: 3 tsp Italian blend seasoning (or Herbes de Provence)
1 pound short uncooked pasta (shells, macaroni, penne, or bowties)
½ tsp crushed red pepper flakes (optional)

steps: Switch pot to SAUTÉ mode and heat oil. Brown the ground beef for 4–5 minutes, breaking it apart as it cooks until no pink remains.
Stir in Italian seasoning and garlic, cooking for 1 minute more. Transfer meat to a separate bowl and cover.
Pour marinara sauce, water, and beef broth into the pot. Add uncooked pasta, onion powder, and red pepper flakes if using. Stir well to combine.
Seal the lid and move the vent valve to SEALED.
Cook at high pressure (PRESSURE COOK or MANUAL) for 8 minutes.
Perform a quick release by flipping the vent valve to VENT immediately. Wait for the float valve to drop, then open the lid.
Return the reserved beef to the pot along with mozzarella cheese. Stir until cheese melts throughout, then season with salt and pepper.
Top with fresh parsley, basil, or thyme and serve.

notes: Choose any short pasta shape; cooking time remains the same.
For extra creaminess, stir in a splash of heavy cream or cream cheese after adding the mozzarella.
Leftovers keep refrigerated for up to 4 days.
nutrition: Calories: 312 kcal · Carbs: 9 g · Protein: 20 g · Fat: 22 g · Sodium: 918 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Beef Shawarma and Rice
description: Ground beef cooked with shawarma spices, cabbage, and basmati rice in one pot. A quick Lebanese-inspired weeknight dinner.

items: 1½ cups basmati rice, rinsed and drained
¼ cup chopped cilantro (optional, for garnish)

steps: Set the Instant Pot to Sauté mode on high heat. Once the display shows HOT, pour in the oil. When it shimmers, stir in the garlic for 30 seconds.

Add the onions and ground beef, stirring constantly to break apart the meat into small pieces. Do not fully cook the beef at this stage. If anything is sticking to the pot bottom, pour ¼ cup water in and scrape thoroughly to deglaze.
Stir in the rinsed rice, water, shredded cabbage, salt, and shawarma spice until combined.
Seal the lid and cook at high pressure for 5 minutes. Let pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then manually release any remaining pressure.

notes: Deglazing prevents the burn notice from interrupting cooking.
Cilantro and yogurt on the side brighten the finished dish.
Leftovers store well in the refrigerator for up to 4 days.
nutrition: Calories: 348 kcal · Carbs: 43 g · Protein: 19 g · Fat: 10 g · Sodium: 335 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 3 g

title: Instant Pot Hard Boiled Eggs
description: Hard boiled eggs cooked under pressure, using the 5-5-5 method for consistent results and easy peeling.

steps: Pour 1 cup cold tap water into the inner pot for a 3 or 6-quart model, or 1½ cups for an 8-quart model.
Set an egg tray or metal trivet in the pot and arrange eggs on it, stacking as needed.
Lock the lid with the vent knob sealed. Set the pressure cooker to high pressure for 5 minutes using the manual or pressure cook setting.
While cooking, prepare an ice bath by filling a large bowl halfway with ice cubes, then adding enough cold water to cover the eggs.

When the timer beeps, allow pressure to release naturally for 5 minutes, then perform a quick release by carefully moving the vent knob to the venting position with a long utensil handle; stand back to avoid steam.
Using tongs or a pot holder, transfer the hot eggs to the ice bath.
After 5 minutes in the ice bath, remove the eggs, pat dry, peel, and serve.

notes: Room temperature eggs cook more evenly; take them out of the refrigerator 10 minutes before cooking.
The ice bath stops the cooking process and makes peeling easier by contracting the membrane under the shell.
Stack eggs vertically to fit more in smaller cookers without affecting cooking time.
nutrition: Calories: 62 kcal · Protein: 5 g · Fat: 4 g · Sodium: 63 mg

title: Instant Pot Hibachi Shrimp
description: A quick shrimp dish with soy-ginger sauce, zucchini, onion, and mushrooms cooked under pressure. Served over rice with sesame seeds and fresh herbs.

items: ¼ cup low sodium soy sauce
1 zucchini, sliced ¼ inch thick and halved
1 yellow onion, quartered into wedges and halved
green onion, chives, or parsley, chopped

steps: Combine soy sauce, water, rice wine vinegar, ginger, minced garlic, sugar, sesame oil, and white pepper in the Instant Pot.
Add the thawed shrimp, onion, zucchini, and mushrooms to the pot.

Seal the lid with the valve in sealing position. Select manual or pressure cook and set to LOW for 0 minutes. Once finished, use quick release to vent steam and carefully open the lid. If the sauce needs thickening, remove vegetables and whisk together cornstarch and water, stir into sauce, then sauté for 1 minute until thickened; return vegetables if desired.
Ladle over cooked rice and top with sesame seeds and fresh herbs.

notes: For a thicker, more flavorful sauce, double the sauce ingredients as the cooking time is very short.
Rice vinegar, apple cider vinegar, or plain vinegar work interchangeably.
Thaw shrimp completely before cooking to ensure even doneness.
nutrition: Calories: 244 kcal · Carbs: 12 g · Protein: 42 g · Fat: 3 g · Sodium: 3008 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 8 g

title: Instant Pot Honey Mustard Chicken
description: Chicken thighs braised with honey, mustard, potatoes, and carrots in a simple one-pot meal.

items: 3 tbsp stone ground or whole grain mustard (not ground mustard powder)
3 carrots, peeled and cut into 1–2 inch pieces

steps: Select sauté mode and heat the oil. Season the chicken thighs with salt and pepper, then brown them skin-side down until well colored.
Pour in the water and scrape the pot bottom with a wooden spoon to loosen any stuck bits. Turn off sauté mode.
Stir in the garlic, honey, Dijon mustard, and whole grain mustard. Add the potatoes and carrots, then season with additional salt and pepper.
Cover the pot and move the valve to sealing position.
Press manual or pressure cook on high for 8 minutes.
When done, quick-release the steam and open the lid carefully. Serve, garnishing with chopped parsley if desired.

notes: Use whole grain or stone ground mustard, not dry powder—it dissolves and flavors unevenly.
Bone-in, skin-on thighs stay moist and brown better than breasts for this dish.
Chicken releases liquid as it cooks; the sauce will thin—simmer on sauté mode for a few minutes after cooking if you prefer it thicker.
nutrition: Calories: 258 kcal · Carbs: 27 g · Protein: 22 g · Fat: 7 g · Sodium: 231 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 14 g

title: Instant Pot Honey Teriyaki Chicken
description: Tender shredded chicken coated in a sweet and savory honey teriyaki glaze. Simple enough for weeknight dinners, best served over rice.

items: 4 boneless skinless chicken breasts (or 6 chicken thighs)
½ cup soy sauce (low sodium preferred)
¼–½ tsp crushed red pepper flakes

steps: Add chicken to the pot. Combine soy sauce, water, honey, garlic, rice vinegar, ginger, and red pepper flakes in a bowl, then pour the mixture over the chicken.
Seal the lid and set the vent to sealing. Cook at high pressure for 10 minutes if using thawed chicken, or 20 minutes if frozen.
When done, let the pressure release naturally without venting until the float valve drops.
Remove chicken and skim off any visible fat from the cooking liquid. Shred the chicken into bite-sized pieces.
Switch to sauté mode. Once the liquid bubbles, mix corn starch with cold water and whisk the slurry into the sauce until it thickens. Stir in the sesame seeds.
Return the shredded chicken to the pot and toss until evenly coated.
Turn off the heat, transfer to a serving dish, top with green onions, and serve over rice.

notes: Frozen chicken requires 10 extra minutes of pressure cooking; thawed breasts cook in 10 minutes.
Thighs work equally well and stay moist; use 15 minutes for thawed or 25 minutes for frozen.
The sauce thickens as it cools slightly, so don't over-reduce it on the stove.
nutrition: Calories: 350 kcal · Carbs: 51 g · Protein: 31 g · Fat: 4 g · Sodium: 3378 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 47 g

title: Instant Pot Iced Tea
description: A quick method for brewing strong, smooth iced tea using the Instant Pot. Ready in under 20 minutes with minimal hands-on work.

items: ¼ cup Splenda or ½ cup sugar (adjust to taste)

steps: Add teabags, water, and sweetener to the Instant Pot. Lock the lid and set to high pressure for 4 minutes.
After cooking ends, let pressure drop naturally for 15 minutes minimum. Carefully release any remaining pressure.
Cool slightly, then pour over ice and serve.

notes: Adjust sweetener to taste before serving.
Store cooled tea in the refrigerator for up to one week.
nutrition: Calories: 1 kcal · Sodium: 9 mg

title: Instant Pot Lamb Biryani
description: Tender lamb marinated in yogurt and spices, cooked together with basmati rice in a single pot for an authentic dum biryani.

ingredient_groups.name: Lamb and Marinade
items: 1 pound lamb cubes (shoulder or leg, visible fat trimmed)
¼–1 tsp cayenne pepper (adjust to taste)

step_groups.name: Prepare Ingredients
steps: Rinse the basmati rice under cold water and set aside to soften slightly.
Combine yogurt, minced onion, cilantro, mint, ginger, garlic, garam masala, salt, turmeric, cayenne, cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, and serrano pepper in a bowl.
Add lamb cubes to the marinade and stir to coat evenly. Let sit for 30 minutes.

While the lamb marinates, preheat the oven to broil. Line a baking sheet with foil, spread the sliced onions over it, sprinkle with salt and oil, and toss to coat. Broil for about 15 minutes, stirring once or twice, until deeply browned. Set aside.

step_groups.name: Cook in Instant Pot
steps: Spread the marinated lamb with all its yogurt mixture across the bottom of the Instant Pot inner liner in an even layer.
Distribute the rinsed rice evenly over the lamb without stirring the two together.
Pour the 1 cup water over the rice and gently press it down until submerged. Keep the lamb and rice layers separate.
Seal the lid and set to high pressure for 6 minutes. When done, allow pressure to release naturally (do not quick-release). This slow steam release mimics traditional dum cooking.
Open the pot and top with the reserved browned onions and fresh chopped cilantro before serving.

notes: Use aged Indian basmati rice rather than American varieties for the best texture and fragrance in this dish.
Natural pressure release is crucial—it allows the biryani to finish cooking gently and the rice to absorb the lamb's flavors.
Make the browned onions ahead of time and store at room temperature to streamline prep.
nutrition: Calories: 241 kcal · Carbs: 29 g · Protein: 18 g · Fat: 5 g · Sodium: 734 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Kimchi Jjigae
description: A Korean beef stew with kimchi, mushrooms, and aromatic seasonings. Spicy, umami-rich, and ready in under an hour.

items: 1 pound beef cubes (2-inch pieces, preferably fatty cut)
1 Tbsp gochugaru (or ½ tsp cayenne pepper)
¼ tsp Splenda (or ½ tsp sugar)

steps: Add kimchi, beef cubes, onion, dried mushrooms, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, dark soy sauce, gochugaru, gochujang, Splenda, and water to the pot.
Close the lid and cook at high pressure for 15 minutes, then allow natural pressure release for 5 minutes.
Stir in green scallions and tofu (if using).
Taste and adjust salt or gochugaru as needed.
Serve hot.

notes: Use a fattier beef cut like chuck or brisket so it becomes tender and adds richness to the broth.
Kimchi varies in saltiness and spice; start with less gochugaru and adjust after tasting.
Tofu can be added at any point; if you prefer it firmer, add it during natural release rather than at the end.
nutrition: Calories: 200 kcal · Carbs: 10 g · Protein: 22 g · Fat: 8 g · Sodium: 242 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Instant Pot Lentil Curry
description: A mild, vegan lentil curry with warming Indian spices, coconut milk, and fresh ginger. Ready in under an hour with minimal prep.

items: ½ tbsp coconut sugar or brown sugar
⅛ to ¼ tsp cayenne pepper

steps: Rinse and drain the lentils. Set the pot to sauté mode, add coconut oil, then add 1 tablespoon water along with the shallot, ginger, and garlic. Cook, stirring, until fragrant and the shallot softens, about 2 minutes. Add curry powder, coconut sugar, salt, turmeric, and cayenne; stir vigorously. Stir in the lentils, coconut milk, and 1 cup water until the lentils are fully submerged.

Press cancel to stop sauté. Seal the lid and set to high pressure for 15 minutes. After the timer beeps, let pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then vent fully. Stir in lemon juice, taste, and adjust seasonings. If too thick, add water to reach desired consistency. Serve over brown rice with cilantro.

notes: Reduce cayenne to ⅛ tsp if you prefer mild heat, or omit entirely for sensitive palates.
Natural pressure release is key to tender lentils; avoid quick release.
Leftovers keep refrigerated for up to 4 days and freeze well.
nutrition: Calories: 315 kcal · Carbs: 59 g · Protein: 7 g · Fat: 12 g · Fiber: 6 g · Sugar: 8 g

title: Instant Pot Lentil Soup
description: A hearty French lentil soup made in the pressure cooker with carrots, celery, tomatoes, and aromatic herbs. Naturally gluten-free and vegan.

items: Freshly grated Parmesan cheese for serving (optional)

steps: Heat oil in the pot using the sauté function. Once hot, add the onion and stir frequently until very soft and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.

While the onion cooks, cut the carrots and celery into small dice. Add them to the pot along with the thyme sprigs, salt, paprika, and pepper. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring, until just beginning to soften.
Stir in the lentils and garlic, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant. Pour in the diced tomatoes, crushed tomatoes, and broth.
Lock the lid and set to high pressure for 15 minutes. Allow the pressure to release naturally for 10 to 15 minutes.

Open the lid and stir. Remove the thyme sprigs, leaving loose leaves behind. If too thick, thin with water or additional broth to your preference. Taste and adjust seasonings. Serve with parsley, Parmesan if desired, and bread.

notes: Recipe yields a thick soup; add more broth or water to reach your desired consistency.
Works with green, red, or brown lentils; cook time may vary slightly.
Freezes well for up to 3 months.
nutrition: Calories: 236 kcal · Carbs: 36 g · Protein: 12 g · Fat: 5 g · Sodium: 516 mg · Fiber: 16 g · Sugar: 10 g

title: Instant Pot Lentil Tacos
description: Seasoned lentils cooked under pressure make a hearty, protein-rich taco filling. Serve with your favorite toppings for a quick vegetarian meal.

items: 4 cups low-sodium chicken or vegetable broth
corn or flour tortillas, for serving
sour cream or plain Greek yogurt

steps: Combine lentils, broth, salsa, onion powder, garlic powder, chili powder, cumin, and diced tomatoes in the Instant Pot; stir well.
Lock the lid and set the valve to sealing.
Pressure-cook on high for 15 minutes.
When the timer sounds, place a towel over the valve and move it to quick-release position to vent all pressure.
Open the lid and gently stir the lentils.
Let cool slightly while you arrange toppings.
Spoon the warm lentil mixture into tortillas and top with cheese, avocado, cilantro, green onion, olives, sour cream, and salsa as desired.

notes: Green or brown lentils hold their shape better than red varieties; do not substitute without checking texture after cooking.
For extra heat, use spicy salsa or hot sauce; adjust seasonings to taste before serving.
nutrition: Calories: 97 kcal · Carbs: 15 g · Protein: 7 g · Sodium: 76 mg · Fiber: 7 g

title: Instant Pot Low Country Boil
description: A one-pot seafood and sausage boil with corn, potatoes, and shrimp finished in a spiced garlic butter sauce. Ready in under 30 minutes.

items: ½ lb smoked sausages, cut into 4 pieces
1 tbsp Louisiana Shrimp and Crab Boil seasoning

steps: Add sausage, corn, and potatoes to the pot and cover with water. Stir in the Louisiana Shrimp and Crab Boil seasoning.
Lock the lid and set to high pressure for 4 minutes.
While the pot pressurizes, melt butter in a small skillet over medium-high heat. Add the garlic and stir constantly until the butter foams and absorbs the garlic flavor.
Mix in the Cajun seasoning, Old Bay, hot sauce, and lemon pepper. Taste and adjust—start sparingly as salt content is high. Keep warm on low heat.
When the timer sounds, perform a quick pressure release. Carefully open the lid and verify potatoes are tender but not mushy.
Add the shrimp and stir gently. Once they turn pink (1–2 minutes), immediately transfer them to a serving pot.
Remove corn, potatoes, and sausage from the cooker and add to the serving pot.
Pour the warm garlic butter sauce over everything and stir to coat evenly.
Serve immediately. For less spice-tolerant eaters, omit the hot sauce and rely on the butter, garlic, lemon juice, and lemon pepper for flavor.

step_groups.name: Stovetop Alternative
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil.
Add sausage and potatoes; cook 10 minutes.
Add corn and cook 5 minutes more.
Add shrimp and cook until pink, about 3 minutes.
Drain the pot.
Melt butter with garlic and all spices, then toss with the cooked ingredients.
Finish with fresh lemon juice and serve.

notes: Shrimp cooks quickly—remove immediately when pink to avoid rubbery texture.
The Louisiana Shrimp and Crab Boil seasoning is salty, so taste the spiced butter before adding extra salt.
Leftovers can be refrigerated up to 2 days; reheat gently with additional butter to restore moisture.
nutrition: Calories: 459 kcal · Carbs: 18 g · Protein: 22 g · Fat: 33 g · Sodium: 990 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Meatloaf
description: A one-pot dinner of seasoned ground beef and turkey meatloaf served alongside creamy mashed Yukon Gold potatoes.

ingredient_groups.name: Meatloaf
items: 2 pounds ground beef (or ground turkey, ground pork, or a combination)
4 cloves garlic, minced (or 1½ tsp garlic powder)
½ tsp freshly ground black pepper
½ cup sweet onion, diced (or 3 tsp dried minced onion)

steps: Combine all meatloaf ingredients in a large bowl, stirring gently until just mixed without compacting the meat.
Shape the mixture into a round loaf sized to fit in the pot.
Wrap the loaf in aluminum foil, folding the edges upward to form a containment vessel that prevents drippings from seeping onto the potatoes.
Pour the quartered potatoes, water, and salt into the pot bottom.
Set the trivet over the potatoes and position the foil-wrapped meatloaf on the trivet.
Lock the lid and close the steam valve. Cook on high pressure for 25 minutes. Allow pressure to drop naturally for 10 minutes after the timer signals.
While cooking, whisk together the ketchup, brown sugar, mustard, and nutmeg in a small bowl to prepare the glaze.
Lift the meatloaf from the pot, unwrap it, discard the foil and any accumulated liquid, then spoon the glaze over the surface and slice.

Drain excess water from the pot. Stir in the milk, sour cream, butter, and bouillon paste with the potatoes, mashing until the desired consistency is reached (avoid overworking them). Season to taste.

notes: Natural pressure release for 10 minutes is followed by manual venting; do not skip the natural release phase.
Use a meat thermometer to verify doneness: meatloaf interior should reach 160°F.
Potatoes can be made creamy or chunky depending on your mashing preference; taste and adjust salt and pepper at the end.
nutrition: Calories: 546 kcal · Carbs: 43 g · Protein: 27 g · Fat: 29 g · Sodium: 845 mg · Fiber: 4 g · Sugar: 15 g

title: Instant Pot Mexican Casserole
description: A one-pot meal combining ground chicken, rice, beans, corn, and Mexican spices. Fresh bell peppers and salsa create a flavorful, filling dish.

items: 1 lb 93% lean ground chicken or turkey
1 large red bell pepper, cut into ¾-inch wide strips and halved if long
1 large green bell pepper, cut into ¾-inch wide strips and halved if long
1 (15 oz) can low-sodium black beans, drained and rinsed
1 cup corn kernels (fresh, frozen and thawed, or canned and drained)
¼ cup water (plus up to 1 additional cup if you have a newer Instant Pot model)
1 (15 oz) can fire-roasted diced tomatoes with juices
2 cups salsa, chunky medium or your preference (1 (16 oz) jar)
1 cup shredded Mexican blend cheese, divided
For serving: chopped fresh cilantro, diced avocado, chopped green onion, sour cream, or plain Greek yogurt

steps: Switch the Instant Pot to SAUTÉ mode and heat the oil until shimmering. Add chicken, onion, and salt; cook and stir 7 minutes, breaking up the meat until no pink remains. Fold in both bell peppers, black beans, corn, chili powder, cumin, and garlic powder.

Pour in the ¼ cup water and use a spoon to scrape the pot bottom and sides thoroughly, removing all browned bits to prevent a burn warning. Add the diced tomatoes with their juices and the salsa without stirring. Rinse the rice and drain it well, then add to the pot. Use the back of a spoon to gently submerge the rice in the liquid and spread it in an even layer; do not stir.

Seal the lid and cook on high pressure for 20 minutes. Let pressure release naturally for 20 minutes, then manually vent any remaining steam. Open the lid and stir the mixture; some liquid may remain at the bottom but will absorb as the casserole rests.

Stir ½ cup cheese into the casserole, then scatter the remaining ½ cup over the top. Replace the lid without sealing. Allow to sit 10 minutes for the cheese to melt and additional liquid absorption. Serve with your choice of toppings.

notes: Rinse rice before adding and clear any stuck food from the pot bottom to minimize burn warnings, especially with newer Instant Pot models.
If your Instant Pot registers burn warnings easily, increase water to 1¼ cups total.
The rice will continue absorbing liquid after cooking; some visible liquid immediately after pressure release is normal.
nutrition: Calories: 437 kcal · Carbs: 47 g · Protein: 27 g · Fat: 14 g · Fiber: 9 g · Sugar: 9 g

title: Instant Pot Authentic Mexican Rice
description: A traditional Mexican rice side dish with tomatoes, carrots, and peas, cooked in the Instant Pot. Perfectly seasoned with tomato bouillon and finished with a natural pressure release.

items: ¼ cup vegetable or canola oil
¼ cup tomato sauce (or 2 pureed tomatoes)
2 tomato bouillon cubes, finely chopped (or 2 tsp granulated)

steps: Rinse rice under cold water until the water runs clear, then drain thoroughly.
Switch Instant Pot to sauté mode and wait until it is hot, then add oil.
Pour in rice and sauté while stirring frequently until it turns light golden, about 5 minutes.
Stir in garlic, onion, tomato sauce, salt, tomato bouillon, and carrots.
Add water and stir well until bouillon cubes dissolve completely.
Turn off sauté mode, close the lid, and move the valve to sealed.
Set to high pressure for 3 minutes (for tender grains) or 4 minutes (for softer rice).
When the timer sounds, let pressure release naturally for 10 minutes.
Carefully remove the lid and fold in the frozen peas, then gently fluff the rice with a fork.
Cover the pot with the lid again and let the rice rest for a few minutes before serving.

notes: Rinsing the rice removes excess starch and prevents stickiness.
The 3-minute cook time yields rice with slight bite; use 4 minutes if you prefer softer texture.
Fluff gently with a fork rather than stirring to keep grains intact.
nutrition: Calories: 323 kcal · Carbs: 50 g · Protein: 5 g · Fat: 10 g · Sodium: 371 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Mushroom Wild Rice Soup
description: A creamy vegetarian soup combining wild rice, mushrooms, and vegetables in a rich broth base. Hearty and warming, finished with a butter-flour roux and fresh kale.

items: 2 large handfuls kale (roughly chopped, thick stems removed)
½ tsp freshly cracked black pepper

step_groups.name: Instant Pot
steps: Combine wild rice, carrots, celery, onion, garlic, mushrooms, broth, thyme, bay leaves, rosemary, salt, and pepper in the Instant Pot. Stir to combine, then seal the lid with the vent in sealing position.
Cook on high pressure for 30 minutes. Allow pressure to release naturally.
Open the lid and remove the bay leaves. Stir in the kale.

step_groups.name: Stovetop roux
steps: While the pot cooks, melt butter in a skillet over medium heat.
Whisk in flour and cook for 1–2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the raw flour smell disappears.
Gradually whisk in milk until the mixture is smooth and thickened.

step_groups.name: Finish
steps: Stir the roux into the Instant Pot until fully combined.
Ladle into bowls and serve.

notes: Refrigerate leftovers up to 4 days; the soup thickens as it cools and can be thinned with additional broth or milk when reheating.
For a lighter version, substitute half-and-half or heavy cream for the milk, or omit the roux and use a blend option to thicken naturally.
Dice carrots and celery into similar sizes so they cook evenly.
nutrition: Calories: 423 kcal · Carbs: 56 g · Protein: 17 g · Fat: 17 g · Sodium: 1146 mg · Fiber: 6 g · Sugar: 11 g

title: Instant Pot Oatmeal
description: A quick, hands-off breakfast porridge made with rolled oats, milk, and water cooked together in a heat-safe bowl set on a trivet inside the pressure cooker.

steps: Pour 1½ cups cold water into the inner pot and place a metal trivet inside.
In a 7-cup heat-safe bowl or 7×4-inch cake pan, combine the rolled oats, 1 cup water, 2 cups milk, and flaxseed if using. Set this bowl on the trivet inside the pot.
Close the lid and set the pressure valve to sealed. Cook on high pressure for 3 minutes for rolled oats or 1 minute for quick-cooking oats.
When cooking finishes, let pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then carefully switch the valve to vent to release remaining steam.
Carefully remove the bowl from the pot and serve with your chosen toppings, or let cool and refrigerate for reheating later.

notes: Use rolled oats for 3 minutes of cooking time or quick-cooking oats for 1 minute.
Cooked oatmeal keeps refrigerated for up to 5 days and can be reheated with a splash of milk.
The pot-in-pot method prevents the oatmeal from sticking and scorching on the pot bottom.
nutrition: Calories: 160 kcal · Carbs: 22 g · Protein: 6 g · Fat: 5 g · Sodium: 41 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 4 g

title: Instant Pot Spaghetti and Meat Sauce
description: A one-pot spaghetti dinner made with lean ground turkey and marinara sauce, delivering over 30 grams of protein per serving. Ready in under 30 minutes with minimal cleanup.

items: 1 lb 93% ground turkey or lean ground beef

steps: Press sauté mode. Once hot, add the ground turkey with salt and cook, breaking it apart with a wooden spoon, until no longer pink, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the onion and garlic, cooking until softened, 3 to 4 minutes.
Cancel sauté mode. Pour in the water and scrape the bottom of the pot with a wooden spoon to release any browned bits and prevent a burn warning.
Break the spaghetti in half and add it to the pot. Pour the marinara sauce over the pasta without stirring, ensuring it is covered.
Lock the lid and cook on high pressure for 9 minutes.
When the timer sounds, immediately turn the pressure release valve to venting for a quick release. Once pressure is fully released, remove the lid, stir, and serve with parmesan cheese if desired.

notes: Breaking the spaghetti in half prevents it from standing above the sauce; the pasta will soften fully during pressure cooking.
Do not stir before cooking; the pasta will cook unevenly if mixed with the sauce beforehand.
For storage, refrigerate leftovers in an airtight container for up to 3 days, or freeze for up to 2 months.
nutrition: Calories: 485 kcal · Carbs: 60.5 g · Protein: 30.5 g · Fat: 12 g · Sodium: 637.5 mg · Fiber: 5.5 g · Sugar: 9 g

title: Instant Pot Pasta e Fagioli Soup
description: A hearty Italian soup combining ground Italian sausage, two types of beans, tomatoes, and vegetables in a savory broth, finished with ditalini pasta.

items: ⅔ cup dried white great northern or navy beans
⅔ cup dried red kidney beans
1 pound ground Italian sausage (mild or spicy)
½ yellow or white onion, diced
1 15 oz can diced tomatoes, not drained
½ cup carrots, chopped or sliced
1 cup ditalini pasta, boiled until tender
1½ teaspoons dried Italian blend seasoning (or ½ teaspoon each dried basil, thyme, and oregano)
¼ teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes
Fresh basil, cracked black pepper, grated Parmesan cheese for topping, to taste

steps: Soak the dried beans overnight or for 6 to 8 hours in cold water, then drain and rinse.
In a large pot, cover the beans with fresh water, bring to a boil, reduce heat, and cook gently for 1 hour, stirring occasionally, until the beans reach full tenderness. Drain the cooked beans.
Set the Instant Pot to sauté mode. Add the sausage and onion, cooking for 3 to 5 minutes until the meat loses its pink color. Stir in the garlic and cook for another 1 to 2 minutes until aromatic.
Switch to soup mode. Pour in the broth, tomato sauce, and canned tomatoes. Add the celery, carrots, beans, salt, Italian seasoning, and red pepper flakes. Stir well to combine.
Seal the lid, set the valve to sealing, and cook at high pressure for 10 minutes. Let the pot rest for 3 to 4 minutes before opening the valve for quick release.
Stir in the cooked ditalini pasta. Top each serving with black pepper, fresh basil, and Parmesan cheese as desired.

notes: If using canned beans instead of dried, skip the soaking and precooking steps and add about 3 cups drained canned beans in step 4.
The soup can be made in a slow cooker: combine all ingredients after precooking the beans, cover, and cook on low for 6 to 8 hours or high for 3 to 4 hours; stir in pasta just before serving.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 4 days; pasta may absorb more broth overnight.
nutrition: Calories: 666 kcal · Carbs: 61 g · Protein: 33 g · Fat: 32 g · Sodium: 2185 mg · Fiber: 9 g · Sugar: 3 g

title: Instant Pot Pork and Sauerkraut
description: Tender pork loin chunks braised with sauerkraut, apples, and onions in apple juice. A German-style one-pot meal that cooks under pressure in minutes.

items: 2–3 pounds pork loin roast, cut into 2–3 inch chunks
1 cup apple juice or chicken stock

steps: Cut the pork loin into 2–3 inch chunks and season generously with salt and pepper on all sides.
Add the pork pieces to the pot with 1 cup apple juice. Layer in the sliced apple, sliced onion, and garlic. Top with the drained sauerkraut.
Set to high pressure for 15 minutes using the pressure cook button and +/− to adjust the time.
When cooking is done, allow pressure to release naturally for at least 15 minutes.
Serve the pork and sauerkraut with the cooking liquid spooned over noodles or mashed potatoes.

notes: Let pressure release naturally for the full 15 minutes to keep the meat tender.
If your sauerkraut is very salty, rinse it before draining to adjust the seasoning.
Serve over egg noodles or buttered mashed potatoes to catch the flavorful braising liquid.
nutrition: Calories: 194 kcal · Carbs: 11 g · Protein: 26 g · Fat: 5 g · Sodium: 433 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 7 g

title: Instant Pot Pork Chops with Gravy
description: Tender pork chops cooked under pressure with a savory gravy made from cream of chicken soup, beef broth, and seasonings.

items: 1 (10.5 oz) can cream of chicken soup

steps: Coat both sides of the pork chops with salt, garlic powder, and pepper.
Heat olive oil in the pot on sauté mode until shimmering, then brown the chops for 2–3 minutes per side until golden; transfer to a plate.
Pour ¼ cup beef broth into the pot and scrape up the browned bits with a wooden spoon to deglaze.
Turn off sauté mode and return the pork chops to the pot, then add the ranch dressing mix, brown gravy mix, cream of chicken soup, and the remaining beef broth.
Seal the lid and set the valve to sealing, then cook on manual (high pressure) for 8 minutes.
Once cooking ends, let pressure drop naturally for 10 minutes, then manually release any remaining pressure and open the lid.
Transfer the cooked chops to a serving dish.
Mix cornstarch and water in a small bowl until smooth, then activate sauté mode and stir the slurry into the gravy, whisking continuously until thickened; turn off heat.
Plate the pork chops and spoon the gravy over them, serving with your choice of sides.

notes: Natural pressure release takes 10 minutes; allow an additional 10 minutes for the pot to pressurize before cooking begins.
Use ¼ cup of the beef broth for deglazing—measurement need not be exact.
Cornstarch slurry thickens quickly once added to hot gravy; stir constantly to avoid lumps.
nutrition: Calories: 316 kcal · Carbs: 4 g · Protein: 37 g · Fat: 15 g · Sodium: 1128 mg

title: Instant Pot Pot Roast with Gravy
description: A tender beef chuck roast with potatoes and carrots in a rich, thickened gravy. Everything cooks in one pot for an easy weeknight dinner.

ingredient_groups.name: main ingredients
items: 3 lb beef chuck roast, cut into 1 lb chunks
1½ lb creamer or baby potatoes, whole
3 large carrots, peeled and cut into thick slices

step_groups.name: prep and sear
steps: Mix seasoning salt, garlic powder, parsley, onion powder, and black pepper in a small bowl. Coat the beef chunks with this mixture.

Set the Instant Pot to sauté mode and wait for it to read hot. Add oil, then sear beef pieces in batches, allowing each side to brown without moving for 2–3 minutes before flipping. Transfer browned meat to a plate.
In the same pot, cook the chopped onion with occasional stirring until soft, adding more oil if needed. Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.

step_groups.name: pressure cook roast
steps: Pour in beef broth, balsamic vinegar, tomato paste, and Worcestershire sauce. Scrape up any browned bits stuck to the pot bottom with a wooden spoon. Stir to combine.
Return the seared beef to the pot. Lock the lid in place and set the valve to sealing. Cook on high pressure for 65 minutes (90–100 minutes if frozen).
When done, turn off the pot and let pressure release naturally for 10–15 minutes. Carefully move the valve to venting and open the lid.

step_groups.name: finish vegetables and gravy
steps: Transfer the roast to a plate, cover, and rest for 10 minutes. Add potatoes and carrots to the pot, lock the lid, set to sealing, and cook on high pressure for 3 minutes.
While vegetables cook, shred the beef and set aside to keep warm.
When the vegetable timer ends, let pressure release naturally for 5 minutes, then carefully move the valve to venting and release remaining pressure. Open the lid.
Remove potatoes and carrots and arrange on a serving platter with the shredded beef.
Stir ketchup into the remaining liquid in the pot. Whisk cornstarch and water together to form a slurry and stir into the gravy.
Switch the pot to sauté mode and cook, stirring frequently, until the gravy thickens. Pour over the roast and vegetables and serve.

notes: For a frozen roast, extend the high-pressure cook time to 90–100 minutes.
Natural pressure release is key to keeping the meat tender; avoid quick release.
Ketchup brightens the savory gravy but is optional if you prefer a deeper flavor.
nutrition: Calories: 447 kcal · Carbs: 24 g · Protein: 36 g · Fat: 23 g · Sodium: 943 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Instant Pot Pot Roast with Mashed Potatoes and Gravy
description: A tender braised chuck roast cooked with onion soup mix and potatoes, served with creamy mashed potatoes and savory pan gravy.

items: 2–3 pounds chuck or rump roast, cut into 4-inch chunks
6–8 red potatoes, quartered with skins on
2 teaspoons minced garlic (or 1 teaspoon garlic powder)

steps: Switch to sauté mode. Season meat generously with salt and pepper. Heat oil in pot and brown meat in batches, 2–3 minutes per side until color develops. Add onion soup mix, potatoes, and beef broth.

Lock lid with vent set to sealed. Pressure cook on high for 60 minutes. Allow natural pressure release for 15–20 minutes, then quick release by turning vent to open. Remove lid once float valve drops.
Transfer meat to a platter and shred using two forks. Strain cooking liquid to remove fat and solids. Switch to soup setting to bring liquid back to a boil.
Place potatoes in a large bowl. Stir in sour cream, salt, pepper, and garlic. Mash by hand or with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy.

Whisk cold water and corn starch together to form a slurry. Pour into boiling cooking liquid and stir until thickened. Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt and pepper as needed. Serve over mashed potatoes and shredded meat.

step_groups.name: From Frozen
steps: Follow main instructions but do not cut meat into sections (too hard when frozen) and skip the searing step. Pressure cook on high for 90 minutes, then proceed with natural and quick release as directed.

notes: Make your own onion soup mix by combining 3 tablespoons dried minced onion, 2 tablespoons beef bouillon powder, 1 tablespoon onion powder, and 1 teaspoon each of garlic powder and salt.
Shred or cube the cooked meat to your preference; shredding gives a more rustic texture.
Refrigerate leftovers for up to 3 days; reheat gently to avoid breaking down the gravy.
nutrition: Calories: 383 kcal · Carbs: 32 g · Protein: 30 g · Fat: 15 g · Sodium: 940 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 3 g

title: Instant Pot Potato Salad
description: A classic American potato salad with hard-boiled eggs cooked simultaneously with the potatoes under pressure, dressed in a creamy mayo-based sauce with mustard, celery, pickles, and red onion.

items: 2 tbsp yellow mustard (or half Dijon mustard)
2 pickles, minced, plus splash of pickle juice

steps: Add 1 cup water and chopped potatoes to the Instant Pot. Place eggs on top of the potatoes.
Lock the lid, set valve to sealing, and cook on high pressure for 4 minutes.
When timer sounds, quick-release pressure. Transfer eggs to a bowl of ice water and drain liquid from the pot.
Separate egg yolks from whites. Place yolks in a bowl, mash with a fork, then whisk in mayonnaise, buttermilk, mustard, pickle juice splash, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing over warm potatoes. Chop egg whites and stir into potatoes along with celery, onion, and pickles until combined.
Cover and refrigerate for several hours before serving to allow flavors to meld.

notes: Chilling the salad for a few hours helps the potatoes absorb the dressing and develop better flavor.
Substitute Dijon mustard entirely if you prefer a sharper tang.
This salad holds well in the refrigerator for up to three days.
nutrition: Calories: 166 kcal · Carbs: 21 g · Protein: 6 g · Fat: 7 g · Sodium: 360 mg · Fiber: 4 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Instant Pot Potato Soup
description: A creamy potato soup loaded with root vegetables and aromatics. Quick to prepare and deeply comforting.

items: 2 lbs red potatoes, cut into 1 cm cubes

steps: Switch to sauté mode and heat the oil. Add onions and cook, stirring frequently, until softened and lightly browned.
Mix in the salt, garlic, parsley, thyme, and pepper; cook for 1 minute. Turn off the pot and pour in the broth. Scrape the bottom with a wooden spoon to dislodge any browned bits.
Add the cubed potatoes, carrots, and celery. Seal the lid and set to high pressure for 3 minutes.

Allow the pot to pressurize naturally (about 20 minutes total including heat-up). After the timer sounds, gradually open the pressure valve slowly to release steam in a controlled manner to prevent spattering.
Once the lid is removed, return to sauté mode.
Whisk together the evaporated milk and cornstarch until smooth, then add to the soup. Stir constantly until the soup thickens.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed. Serve garnished with crumbled bacon, shredded cheese, and chopped green onions.

notes: Reduce salt to 1½ tsp if using salted broth to avoid oversalting.
A gradual release prevents foaming and sputtering; open the valve partway and let pressure escape slowly.
Leftovers keep refrigerated for up to 4 days and can be reheated gently on the stovetop.
nutrition: Calories: 347 kcal · Carbs: 55 g · Protein: 13 g · Fat: 10 g · Sodium: 1065 mg · Fiber: 5 g · Sugar: 12 g

title: Chicken and Lentil Soup
description: A hearty soup combining chicken thighs and lentils simmered together with aromatic spices. Finished in about 30 minutes under pressure, it yields a nourishing one-pot meal.

items: 12 oz boneless skinless chicken thighs, trimmed of fat
½ tsp kosher salt, plus more to taste

steps: Add all ingredients to the Instant Pot and stir well. Lock the lid and set to high pressure using the Soup function for 30 minutes.
Allow pressure to release naturally. Remove the chicken thighs and shred them with two forks, then return to the pot and stir. The finished soup yields about 11 cups. Adjust salt to taste.

notes: Stores well in the refrigerator for up to 5 days or freezes for up to 3 months.
Homemade sazon spice blend works best, but paprika is a fine substitute if unavailable.
nutrition: Calories: 263 kcal · Carbs: 40 g · Protein: 23.5 g · Fat: 3 g · Sodium: 503.5 mg · Fiber: 6.5 g · Sugar: 1.5 g

title: Instant Pot Ranch Chicken Pasta
description: Creamy one-pot pasta with tender chicken, bacon, and a savory ranch-cheese sauce. A comforting weeknight dinner that's ready in under 30 minutes.

items: ½ pound boneless skinless chicken breasts, cut into chunks
salt and freshly ground black pepper
1 package Hidden Valley Original Ranch dressing mix

steps: Season the chicken pieces with salt and pepper, then add to the pot.
Pour in the chicken broth and milk. Stir in the ranch seasoning mix until combined. Add the penne and stir well, making sure the noodles are submerged.
Close the lid and move the valve to sealing position.
Set to high pressure for 4 minutes.
Switch the valve to quick release and carefully open the pot.
Stir in the cream cheese, cheddar cheese, and cooked bacon until melted and incorporated.
Let the pasta sit for 5–10 minutes off heat; the sauce will become thicker as it rests.

notes: Cut chicken into uniform chunks so it cooks evenly in the short cook time.
Cook the bacon ahead of time to save active cooking minutes.
If the sauce seems too thin after cooking, let it rest undisturbed; residual heat will thicken it naturally.
nutrition: Calories: 881 kcal · Carbs: 76 g · Protein: 43 g · Fat: 44 g · Sodium: 700 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 9 g

title: Red Beans and Rice
description: A New Orleans–style one-pot meal of dried red beans simmered with andouille sausage, ham, and aromatic vegetables until tender, then served over rice.

items: 1 lb andouille sausage, sliced ½-inch thick
Cooked white long-grain rice, for serving

steps: Rinse beans thoroughly and pick out any debris or shriveled beans.
Switch pot to sauté mode. Brown sausage slices in 1 tbsp oil on both sides, then transfer to a plate.
Add remaining oil to pot, then sauté garlic and onion over medium heat until onion turns translucent. Stir in celery, bell pepper, and half the green onions; cook 5 minutes.
Add beans, black pepper, Cajun seasoning, thyme, oregano, and bay leaves. Pour in water and chicken broth.
Lock lid and cook on high pressure for 55 minutes. Allow pressure to release naturally for 15 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure.
Remove bay leaves. Transfer 1½ cups of bean mixture to a bowl and mash until smooth, then stir back into pot. Add salsa, parsley, sausages, and ham.
Switch to sauté mode and simmer for 15 minutes until desired thickness is reached.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Thin with up to 1 cup additional water or broth if needed.
Serve over white rice, garnished with remaining green onions and hot sauce to taste.

notes: Do not soak beans; they cook directly from dry.
Mashing a portion of beans thickens the cooking liquid naturally without added starch.
Make ahead: refrigerate finished dish up to 4 days; reheat gently with added broth if needed.
nutrition: Calories: 639 kcal · Carbs: 56 g · Protein: 34 g · Fat: 32 g · Sodium: 1457 mg · Fiber: 14 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Instant Pot Refried Beans
description: Creamy refried beans made from dried pinto beans cooked under pressure with aromatic vegetables and spices. Serve as a side dish with Mexican meals.

items: 1 small yellow onion, chopped into ¼-inch dice
1 jalapeño, cored, seeded, and finely chopped
4 cups low-sodium chicken or vegetable stock, divided
⅛ to ¼ teaspoon cayenne pepper, optional
Queso fresco or shredded Monterey Jack cheese for serving

steps: Rinse the pinto beans in a colander and pick through them, discarding any damaged or misshapen ones.

Set the Instant Pot to sauté mode and heat the oil. Add the onion and jalapeño; sauté for 2 minutes. Stir in the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Pour in a splash of stock and scrape the bottom of the pot to release stuck bits. Add the remaining stock, water, bay leaves, salt, cumin, oregano, cayenne, and drained beans; stir gently to combine.
Seal the pot and cook on high pressure for 45 minutes. After cooking, allow the pressure to release naturally for 25 minutes, then manually vent any remaining pressure and carefully open the lid.
Remove and discard the bay leaves. Transfer 2 cups of cooking liquid to a separate bowl, then drain the remaining liquid from the beans.

Blend the beans using an immersion blender until you reach your preferred consistency, adding reserved liquid as needed. Alternatively, transfer beans to a blender in batches (after they cool slightly) and purée until smooth.
Taste and adjust seasonings. Top with cheese and serve.

notes: The beans will be very liquidy after pressure cooking; draining and reserving the liquid gives you control over the final consistency.
If using a regular blender, let the beans cool for a few minutes first to prevent splattering from hot steam.
Leftovers keep refrigerated for up to 5 days and reheat well with a splash of water or stock.
nutrition: Calories: 134 kcal · Carbs: 20 g · Protein: 9 g · Fat: 3 g · Fiber: 6 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Instant Pot Salisbury Steak
description: Juicy beef patties cooked under pressure with a rich mushroom and onion gravy. A classic comfort dish that comes together in 45 minutes.

ingredient_groups.name: Beef Patties
items: ½ medium onion (140g), finely minced, or 1 teaspoon onion powder
2 cloves (7g) garlic, minced, or ¼ teaspoon garlic powder
Salt and ground black pepper to taste

ingredient_groups.name: Gravy
items: 3 tablespoons (42g) unsalted butter or olive oil
½ medium onion (140g), sliced, or 1 teaspoon onion powder
4 cloves (14g) garlic, minced, or ½ teaspoon garlic powder
1 cup (250ml) unsalted chicken or beef stock
1 tablespoon (15g) Dijon mustard, or 1 teaspoon (2.4g) yellow mustard powder
Salt and ground black pepper to taste

step_groups.name: Prepare & Season Beef Mixture
steps: Combine 1 pound (454g) ground beef with ½ cup (30g) panko breadcrumbs, minced onion, 2 minced garlic cloves, 1 beaten egg, 1 tablespoon (15ml) soy sauce, ½ teaspoon (2.5ml) Worcestershire sauce, ½ teaspoon (2.5ml) sesame oil (optional), and 1 tablespoon (12g) cornstarch (optional) in a large bowl. Season lightly with salt and pepper. Knead and fold until thoroughly integrated, then slap the mixture against the bowl 4–5 times to develop texture.
Fry a small portion in oil to taste the seasoning, adjusting salt as needed. Shape the remaining mixture into 4 firm patties.

step_groups.name: Brown & Cook Patties
steps: Select Sauté More on your Instant Pot and allow it to reach HOT (about 8 minutes). Pour in 3 tablespoons (42g) butter or oil and coat the bottom evenly. Season patties on one side with salt and pepper, place them seasoned-side down, season the top side, and sear each side for 3 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
Add sliced mushrooms to the pot and cook until lightly browned, roughly 9–10 minutes. Stir in sliced onion and cook 4 minutes, then add minced garlic and sauté 30–45 seconds until fragrant.
Pour 1 cup (250ml) stock into the pot and scrape up all browned bits from the bottom with a wooden spoon. Stir briefly.

Add 1 tablespoon (15ml) soy sauce, 1 tablespoon (15ml) Worcestershire sauce, and 1 tablespoon (15g) Dijon mustard; mix well. Return the browned patties to the pot, nestling them as close to the bottom as possible and partially submerged.

step_groups.name: Pressure Cook & Finish
steps: Seal the lid and turn the venting knob to Sealing. Cook at high pressure for 3 minutes, then allow 10 minutes natural release. Open the lid and check that an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest patty reads above 160°F.

Remove patties to a serving plate. Activate Sauté and bring the gravy to a simmer. Whisk together 2 tablespoons (16g) flour and 3 tablespoons (45ml) water, then stir this slurry into the gravy in thirds until your desired thickness is reached. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Spoon the mushroom gravy over the patties and serve with mashed potatoes or your preferred sides.

notes: Meat thermometer should register 160°F minimum for food safety.
Whisk the flour-water mixture in thirds to avoid lumps and achieve smooth gravy consistency.
Store leftovers covered in the refrigerator for up to 3 days; reheat gently on the stovetop to preserve gravy texture.
nutrition: Calories: 350 kcal · Carbs: 15 g · Protein: 19 g · Fat: 24 g · Sodium: 517 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 4 g

title: Vegetable Sambar
description: A one-pot South Indian lentil and vegetable stew seasoned with sambar masala and tamarind. Comfort food that cooks quickly under pressure.

items: ½ cup split pigeon pea (toor dal), washed
½ medium onion (about ¾ cup), sliced
cilantro leaves, finely chopped, for garnish
1 cup pumpkin (about 120 g), cut into medium pieces
2–3 baby eggplants, cut into 4 pieces each
8 drumstick pieces (2-inch pieces, fresh or frozen)

steps: Set the Instant Pot to sauté mode, heat the oil, then add mustard seeds and curry leaves until seeds begin popping.
Stir in the sliced onion and cook for 2 minutes until softened.
Mix in the chopped tomatoes, sambar masala, and salt, then sauté for another 2 minutes.
Pour in the water, then add the toor dal, pumpkin, carrot, eggplant, and drumstick pieces. Lock the lid, set the vent to sealing, and pressure cook at high for 12 minutes.
After the timer beeps, allow pressure to drop on its own. Open the pot and stir in the tamarind paste, mixing thoroughly.
Top with fresh cilantro and serve.

step_groups.name: Stovetop Pressure Cooker Method
steps: Follow the same tempering and sautéing steps in a stovetop pressure cooker, then add water, lentils, and all vegetables.
Bring to pressure over medium-high heat and maintain for 3 whistles, then remove from heat and allow pressure to release naturally.
Stir in tamarind paste until well combined, garnish with cilantro, and serve.

notes: For a thicker sambar, use less water; for a brothier consistency, add more.
Asafoetida is traditional but optional; omit if following a gluten-free diet.
This keeps refrigerated for up to 4 days and reheats well on the stovetop with a splash of water.
nutrition: Calories: 305 kcal · Carbs: 51 g · Protein: 16 g · Fat: 5 g · Sodium: 1237 mg · Fiber: 23 g · Sugar: 13 g

title: Sesame Chicken
description: Chicken pieces cooked under pressure in a honey-soy glaze with garlic, ginger, and sesame. A quick weeknight main dish with a glossy, thickened sauce.

items: 2 lb boneless skinless chicken breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces
¼ cup honey or brown sugar

steps: Place chicken in the pot with soy sauce, garlic, ginger, ketchup, and hot sauce; stir to combine.
Seal the lid and set valve to sealing position. Cook on high pressure for 3 minutes.
Once pressurized, allow the timer to count down. When done, turn the vent to release position and carefully vent steam away from your face.
Open the lid and switch to sauté mode. Stir in honey and sesame oil.
Mix cornstarch with water to form a slurry. Pour into the pot and stir until the sauce thickens, about 1 minute. Turn off sauté.
If the sauce needs more body, add another tablespoon of cornstarch mixed with water and stir again.
Serve over rice and top with sesame seeds and green onion.

notes: Make the cornstarch slurry just before adding to avoid lumps.
Quick manual release is important here; do not use natural release or the chicken will dry out.
Store leftovers in an airtight container for up to 3 days; reheat gently on the stovetop with a splash of water.
nutrition: Calories: 418 kcal · Carbs: 28 g · Protein: 52 g · Fat: 11 g · Sodium: 1575 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 21 g

title: Instant Pot Shredded Chicken Tacos
description: Quick shredded chicken cooked with spices and tomatoes in the pressure cooker, ready to serve in tortillas with your favorite toppings.

items: 3–4 medium to large boneless skinless chicken breasts
avocado, tomatoes, cheese, cilantro, sour cream (for serving)

steps: Add chicken breasts, broth, salt, cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, and the canned tomatoes with their liquid to the Instant Pot.
Lock the lid and cook on high pressure for 20 minutes (or 30 minutes if using frozen chicken).
After cooking, carefully vent the pressure cooker by switching the vent knob to the vent position and wait for the float valve to drop completely.
Remove the lid and use two forks to shred the cooked chicken directly in the pot.
Transfer the shredded chicken to tortillas and top with cheese, tomatoes, avocado, cilantro, sour cream, or other desired toppings.

notes: For 30 minutes cook time with frozen breasts, allow 10 minutes longer to reach pressure before cooking begins.
Store any leftover shredded chicken in the cooking liquid in an airtight container for up to 4 days.
nutrition: Calories: 202 kcal · Carbs: 1 g · Protein: 37 g · Fat: 5 g · Sodium: 1003 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Sloppy Joes
description: A sweet and savory ground beef sandwich filling made quickly under pressure. The homemade sauce is easy enough for weeknight dinners and popular with families.

items: 1 (200g) green or red bell pepper, diced
Kosher salt and ground black pepper to taste
Spicy version: cayenne pepper to taste

steps: Set the pot to Sauté mode (Sauté More on Instant Pot) and let it preheat until the display shows HOT.

Season the ground beef with salt and pepper. Add 1 tablespoon (15ml) olive oil to coat the pot bottom. Place beef in the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until it begins to brown. At the 5-minute mark, drain and discard accumulated liquid. Continue browning until the meat is crisped, about 5–7 minutes total.
Stir in the diced onion and cook for 2 minutes. Add the diced bell pepper and minced garlic; cook until aromatic, roughly 1 minute.

Pour in 1 tablespoon (12.5g) brown sugar, 1 tablespoon (15ml) soy sauce, 1 tablespoon (15ml) Worcestershire sauce, and ½ cup (125ml) water. Use a wooden spoon to scrub up all browned bits from the pot bottom and stir them into the liquid.

Spread 1 tablespoon (15ml) tomato paste, 1 teaspoon (5ml) Dijon mustard, and ¾ cup (188ml) ketchup over the beef mixture without stirring. Close the lid and cook at high pressure for 5 minutes. Allow natural pressure release for 10 minutes, then switch the venting knob to open and release any remaining pressure. Carefully open the lid.
In a skillet, lightly toast the burger buns over medium heat for a few minutes.

If the sauce appears thin, stir the mixture and switch to Sauté mode to simmer for a few minutes until it thickens. Mix in liquid smoke and cayenne pepper as desired. Adjust seasoning with additional salt and pepper to your preference.
Spoon the sloppy joe mixture onto toasted buns and serve with your choice of side dishes.

notes: Draining the beef after 5 minutes of browning removes excess moisture and ensures better texture.
Do not stir the sauce ingredients before pressure cooking; they layer and cook into the meat.
For a spicier version, add cayenne pepper during the final seasoning step; liquid smoke adds depth if you prefer a smokier flavor.
nutrition: Calories: 244 kcal · Carbs: 16 g · Protein: 25 g · Fat: 8 g · Sodium: 576 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 11 g

title: Instant Pot or Slow Cooker Beef Stroganoff Soup
description: A creamy, savory beef stroganoff transformed into soup form. Tender stew meat and mushrooms simmer in a rich broth thickened with sour cream and cornstarch, then finished with egg noodles.

step_groups.name: Instant Pot Instructions
steps: Add stew meat, beef broth, Worcestershire sauce, Italian seasoning, onion powder, garlic powder, and salt and pepper to the pot. Seal the lid with the vent nozzle in the sealed position. Select pressure cook and set the timer for 1 hour.

Once time is up, switch the vent nozzle to venting and release pressure until the button drops. Open the lid and select soup mode. Whisk sour cream with 1 cup of the hot broth in a separate bowl until smooth, then pour back into the pot. Add the mushrooms.

In a small bowl, mix cold water with cornstarch. When the soup reaches a boil, gradually add the cornstarch slurry while stirring constantly until thickened to your liking. Fold in the cooked noodles. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. Serve topped with cracked pepper, fresh herbs, and Parmesan.

step_groups.name: Slow Cooker Instructions
steps: Combine stew meat, beef broth, Worcestershire sauce, Italian seasoning, onion powder, garlic powder, and salt and pepper in the slow cooker. Put the lid on and cook on high for 3 hours, or on low for 6 hours.

Whisk sour cream together with 1 cup of broth from the slow cooker in a bowl until smooth. Return this mixture to the slow cooker with the mushrooms. In a separate bowl, combine cold water and cornstarch, then stir into the slow cooker.

Replace the lid and continue cooking on high for an additional 1 hour. Check the seasoning and adjust salt and pepper as needed. Stir in the cooked noodles. Ladle into bowls and finish with cracked pepper, fresh herbs, and grated Parmesan.

notes: Use low-sodium broth if preferred; the Worcestershire sauce provides plenty of saltiness.
Egg noodles, rotini, or cellentani all work well; cook them separately and add them at the end to prevent mushiness.
For thicker soup, increase cornstarch by ½ teaspoon at a time until you reach your desired consistency.
nutrition: Calories: 593 kcal · Carbs: 58 g · Protein: 51 g · Fat: 16 g · Sodium: 1635 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 6 g

title: Smoked Sausage and Potatoes with Green Beans
description: A hearty one-pot meal of smoked sausage, potatoes, and green beans cooked together under pressure. Ready in minutes.

items: 1 lb smoked sausage, sliced into ½ inch pieces
1 cup low sodium chicken stock (3 or 6-quart model) or 1½ cups (8-quart model)

steps: Set pot to sauté mode and heat the olive oil for 1–2 minutes.
Add onion and sliced sausage; cook for 2–3 minutes until sausage browns and onion softens. Press cancel to turn off sauté.
Pour in the appropriate amount of chicken stock (1 cup for smaller models, 1½ cups for 8-quart) and scrape the bottom of the pot to dissolve browned bits.
Arrange cubed potatoes on top of sausage, sprinkle with Cajun seasoning, then add drained green beans.
Seal the pot lid and set the vent to sealed.
Select high pressure cook mode and set time to 2 minutes.

After cooking finishes, allow pressure to drop naturally for at least 5 minutes, then carefully switch the vent to quick release mode and move your face away from escaping steam. Alternatively, allow full natural pressure release.
Stir and serve.

notes: Use 1 cup broth for 3 or 6-quart pots, 1½ cups for 8-quart to ensure proper pressure cooking.
For extra tenderness, use potatoes of uniform size so they cook evenly.
nutrition: Calories: 384 kcal · Carbs: 33 g · Protein: 17 g · Fat: 22 g · Sodium: 723 mg · Fiber: 8 g · Sugar: 6 g

title: Instant Pot Spaghetti
description: One-pot spaghetti with ground beef cooked under pressure. Everything cooks together with sauce and noodles for an easy weeknight dinner.

items: 1 lb lean ground beef (or Italian sausage)
1 (14.5 oz) can diced tomatoes (or tomato sauce)

steps: Switch the pot to sauté mode and brown the ground beef, breaking it into small pieces as it cooks. Mix in the seasonings while the meat finishes cooking. Turn off the pot and drain excess fat if needed.
Break the noodles in half and scatter over the meat. Pour the spaghetti sauce, canned tomatoes, and water over the noodles. Use a spoon to submerge the noodles so they are fully covered by liquid.
Lock the lid and set to high pressure for 8 minutes. When done, release pressure manually. Stir well and serve.

notes: Drain excess fat after browning the meat to keep the dish from becoming greasy.
Breaking the noodles ensures they cook evenly and stay submerged in the liquid.
nutrition: Calories: 385 kcal · Carbs: 56 g · Protein: 26 g · Fat: 4 g · Sodium: 63 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Instant Pot Spaghetti Sauce
description: A rich tomato-based pasta sauce made with crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, and aromatics that develops deep flavor under pressure in about 25 minutes.

items: ½ cup dry red wine (or additional vegetable broth)
1½ cups low-sodium vegetable broth (or beef or chicken stock)
¼ tsp crushed red pepper flakes
28 oz crushed tomatoes (or tomato puree or passata)
1 tbsp unsalted butter (or additional olive oil)

steps: Switch the Instant Pot to sauté mode and warm the 1 tbsp olive oil until shimmering. Stir in the minced onion and cook 2–4 minutes until softened.
Pour in the ½ cup wine and scrape the pot bottom to release any stuck-on bits. Let the wine simmer and reduce for about 1 minute, then cancel the sauté function.
