Stir in the 1½ cups broth, 2 tsp oregano, 1 tbsp parsley, 2 bay leaves, 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp pepper, ¼ tsp red pepper flakes, and 3 crushed garlic cloves until combined.
Layer the 28 oz crushed tomatoes on top without stirring, then dollop the 12 oz tomato paste and 1 tbsp butter over the tomatoes. Drizzle the 1 tbsp honey over everything and do not stir.
Close the lid and lock the vent to the sealed position. Set the cooker to high pressure for 25 minutes using the manual or pressure cook button and the +/− controls.

Once the timer sounds, let pressure release naturally for 10–15 minutes before manually venting any remaining pressure. Remove the bay leaves and garlic cloves, then stir thoroughly. Use immediately or cool and refrigerate.

notes: For fresh tomatoes, use about 3 pounds; core and crush them before adding.
Natural pressure release for 10–15 minutes is key to developing flavor without scorching the bottom.
This sauce freezes well for up to 3 months in airtight containers.
nutrition: Calories: 63 kcal · Carbs: 11 g · Protein: 2 g · Fat: 1 g · Sodium: 441 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 7 g

title: Instant Pot Spanish Rice
description: A quick Tex-Mex side dish of long-grain rice cooked with tomato sauce, jalapeño, and warm spices.

items: 1 small jalapeño, seeded and finely minced

steps: Rinse the 2 cups rice in a colander under running water until the water runs clear, then drain completely.

Switch the pot to sauté mode and heat the 1 tablespoon olive oil for about 1 minute. Add the onion and jalapeño, stirring often for 2–3 minutes until the onion turns translucent. Stir in the garlic and cook for 30 seconds more to release its aroma.
Cancel sauté mode. Pour in the 2 cups broth and scrape the pot bottom with a wooden spoon to dissolve any stuck-on bits and prevent a burn warning.

Stir in the 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon paprika, 1 teaspoon chili powder, ½ teaspoon cumin, and ¼ teaspoon oregano. Add the drained rice and gently press it down to submerge it. Spread the ⅔ cup tomato sauce over the top without mixing it in.
Seal the lid and set the vent to sealed. Pressure-cook on HIGH for 3 minutes (white rice) or 22 minutes (brown rice).
After cooking finishes, allow pressure to release naturally for at least 15 minutes, then open the lid.
Stir in the 1 tablespoon butter if using, and fluff the rice with a fork to combine all ingredients. Serve warm.

notes: For brown rice, increase the high-pressure cook time to 22 minutes instead of 3.
Rinsing the rice removes excess starch and helps achieve fluffy, separate grains.
The tomato sauce layer on top prevents the rice from overcooking at the surface.
nutrition: Calories: 196 kcal · Carbs: 40 g · Protein: 4 g · Fat: 2 g · Sodium: 520 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Instant Pot Split Pea Soup
description: A hearty split pea and ham soup made in the Instant Pot. It combines green split peas, smoked pork, vegetables, and broth to create a thick, nourishing meal ready in about an hour.

items: 1 (2 lb) ham bone or smoked pork hock
2 tablespoons (28g) unsalted butter or olive oil

steps: Select the Sauté More function and wait for the pot to display HOT. Pour in 28g butter or olive oil, then immediately add the diced onion with a pinch of salt. Cook the onion until it softens, about 2 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic, dried thyme, and bay leaves, and cook for 1 minute more. Add the chopped carrots and celery and cook for 1 additional minute.
Pour ½ cup (125ml) of chicken stock into the pot and scrape the bottom with a wooden spoon to release all the browned bits.

Add the 454g split peas and the ham bone or pork hock to the pot. Pour in the remaining 5½ cups (1375ml) of chicken stock. Ensure the peas are fully submerged. Close the lid with the venting knob in the sealing position. Set to high pressure for 15 minutes, then allow natural pressure release for 10 minutes.

Remove the ham bone or pork hock and set it aside. Stir the soup several times; it will thicken as it cools. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt as needed (add about 2 large pinches of kosher salt as a reference). If using smoked pork hock, shred any meat and return it to the soup or reserve for sandwiches. Serve warm.

notes: The soup will continue to thicken as it cools; stir occasionally while resting.
Smoked pork hock meat can be shredded and either stirred back into the soup or used in sandwiches.
Taste before seasoning since the pork and stock may already provide saltiness.

title: Easy Instant Pot Stroganoff
description: Ground beef combined with mushrooms and onions, simmered with wide egg noodles in a creamy stroganoff sauce cooked entirely in the Instant Pot.

items: 1 pound ground beef (or ground turkey or ground pork)
salt and freshly ground black pepper
10.5 ounce can cream of mushroom soup (or homemade)
⅓ cup sour cream (or Greek yogurt)

steps: Set pot to sauté mode. Once hot, coat the bottom with cooking spray or oil, then add ground beef. Brown the meat, crumbling it as it cooks, and season with salt and pepper. Drain excess fat.
Add onion, garlic, and mushroom slices; sauté for 1 minute.
Stir in flour, cream of mushroom soup, beef broth, and egg noodles until combined.
Lock the lid and move the valve to sealed. Cook on high pressure for 2 minutes.
When cooking finishes, perform a quick release. If steam sprays from the valve, close it, wait 30 seconds, then release again; alternatively, turn the valve halfway to let steam escape gradually.
Carefully open the lid and stir. Let cool a few minutes, then fold in sour cream and parsley. Taste and adjust salt and pepper.

notes: Greek yogurt works well as a sour cream substitute if you prefer a lighter version.
Homemade cream of mushroom soup can replace the canned version; simply whisk together mushrooms sautéed in butter with flour and broth to make a quick sauce.
nutrition: Calories: 371 kcal · Carbs: 40 g · Protein: 33 g · Fat: 8 g · Sodium: 698 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Stuffed Pepper Soup
description: A savory soup combining ground beef, bell peppers, rice, and tomato sauce in beef broth. Made entirely in the Instant Pot.

steps: Switch the pot to sauté mode and brown the ground beef with garlic powder, Italian seasoning, onion powder, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper until no pink remains.
Turn off sauté mode.
Pour in beef broth and scrape up any browned bits from the pot bottom.
Stir in diced peppers, tomato sauce, and rice.
Seal the lid and set the valve to sealing.
Cook at high pressure for 10 minutes.
Let the pot sit for 5 minutes after the timer ends, then quick-release by opening the valve.
Serve immediately.

notes: Scraping the pot bottom after browning the meat prevents the burn warning and deepens flavor.
For softer rice, allow natural release instead of quick-release.

title: Instant Pot Swedish Meatballs and Noodles
description: A creamy one-pot meal of beef meatballs and egg noodles in a savory sauce, ready in under 30 minutes.

items: 3 cups extra broad egg noodles (140 g)
½ cup half and half or heavy cream

steps: Add meatballs, beef broth, Worcestershire sauce, garlic, parsley, salt, and pepper to the pot. Stir to combine.
Spread noodles over the mixture in an even layer and press down gently—do not stir, and it is fine if they are not fully submerged.
Close the lid, set the valve to sealing, and cook on high pressure for 3 minutes. The pot will take about 10 minutes to reach pressure.

When finished, perform a quick release by opening the valve. (For softer noodles, allow natural release for 5 minutes first.) The noodles may look dry on top—this is normal. Stir them into the meatballs.
Whisk cream and corn starch together, then stir into the pot. Switch to sauté mode and cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens.

notes: Do not stir the noodles into the liquid before cooking; they will cook properly without being submerged and stirring can lead to mushiness.
Frozen meatballs work fine and do not require thawing first.
For a richer sauce, use heavy cream instead of half and half.
nutrition: Calories: 470 kcal · Carbs: 25 g · Protein: 27 g · Fat: 29 g · Sodium: 690 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 1 g

title: Instant Pot Taco Chicken Soup
description: A savory chicken and bean soup flavored with taco seasoning, salsa, and corn. Ready in 30 minutes.

steps: Set pot to sauté mode. Heat olive oil, add onion, and cook 2–3 minutes until softened. Stir in garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Pour in chicken broth and scrape the bottom of the pot with a wooden spoon to release browned bits. Turn off sauté mode.
Add chicken breasts, black beans, pinto beans, corn, tomato sauce, salsa, and taco seasoning. Stir to combine.
Seal the lid and set valve to sealing. Cook on high pressure for 20 minutes.
Use quick release. If liquid sprays from the valve, reseal, wait 5 minutes, then release again.
Shred chicken using two forks and stir soup. Serve with tortilla chips, cheese, and sour cream.

notes: Ensure the pot bottom is fully deglaased to prevent a burn notice during cooking.
Soup can be refrigerated up to 3 days; freeze portions for up to 1 month.
nutrition: Calories: 197 kcal

title: Instant Pot Taco Pasta
description: A one-pot ground beef and pasta dish seasoned with taco flavors, tomato sauce, and salsa, finished with melted cheddar and cream. Mild and family-friendly.

items: salt and ground black pepper to taste

steps: Select sauté mode and brown the ground beef in the pot, breaking it apart as it cooks until no pink remains, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the onion and garlic; cook for 2 minutes more.
Turn off sauté mode. Season the mixture with taco seasoning, salt, and pepper. Pour in the tomato sauce, salsa, and beef stock.
Spread the uncooked pasta over the beef mixture and use a spatula to submerge it in liquid, adding a splash of water if needed to ensure full coverage.
Lock the lid in place, move the vent to sealing, and set the pressure cooker to high for 4 minutes.
Once pressure builds and cooking completes, perform a quick release of steam.
Stir the pasta, then fold in the heavy cream and cheddar cheese until melted. Serve hot.

notes: Use 85-90% lean ground beef to avoid excess grease in the finished dish.
Quick release prevents the pasta from overcooking after the pressure cycle ends.
Cilantro is optional; omit if you prefer, or use fresh parsley as a substitute.
nutrition: Calories: 623 kcal · Carbs: 50 g · Protein: 31 g · Fat: 33 g · Sodium: 802 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Instant Pot Tomato Basil Soup
description: A creamy tomato soup enriched with sautéed vegetables, parmesan cheese, and fresh basil. The soup is puréed until smooth and thickened with a butter-flour roux.

items: ¼ cup fresh basil leaves, chopped (or 1 tbsp dried basil)
¼ tsp freshly ground black pepper

steps: Set pot to sauté mode with oil. Cook chopped carrots, onions, and celery for 2 minutes, then add diced tomatoes, tomato paste, broth, oregano, and basil; stir to combine.
Turn off sauté mode, secure the lid, and set valve to sealing.
Cook on high pressure for 5 minutes. Allow pressure to release naturally for 5 minutes, then open.
While the pot pressurizes, melt butter in a large saucepan over medium-low heat. Stir in flour and cook, stirring constantly, for about 10 minutes until the roux turns golden brown.
Carefully transfer the soup to a blender in 2–3 batches and blend until smooth.
Add 1 cup of puréed soup to the roux and stir until combined into a thick paste. Slowly whisk in 3 cups more soup, then return this mixture to the pot and stir until smooth.
Stir in parmesan cheese, half and half, salt, and pepper. Taste and adjust seasonings with additional oregano and basil as desired.

notes: The roux must be golden brown (about 10 minutes) to cook out the raw flour taste and thicken the soup properly.
Blending hot soup can cause splattering; let it cool slightly or use a blender with a vented lid.
For a lighter version, substitute heavy cream with whole milk or use half the butter and flour.
nutrition: Calories: 297 kcal · Carbs: 14 g · Protein: 10 g · Fat: 22 g · Sodium: 690 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Instant Pot Turkey Meatball and Ditalini Soup
description: A hearty Italian soup combining lean turkey meatballs, ditalini pasta, zucchini, and aromatic vegetables in a tomato broth.

ingredient_groups.name: Soup
items: 1 can (28 oz) diced San Marzano tomatoes

step_groups.name: Meatballs
steps: Mix turkey, breadcrumbs, Pecorino, egg, crushed garlic, parsley, and salt in a bowl until just combined.
Form into 30 meatballs, each about 1 heaping tablespoon.

step_groups.name: Cook
steps: Select sauté mode on the Instant Pot and spray with cooking spray.
Brown meatballs in 2 to 3 batches, spraying between batches. Transfer to a plate.
Wipe out the pot. Add olive oil, then cook onion, celery, carrot, and minced garlic until softened, about 4 minutes.
Stir in tomatoes, Parmesan rind, basil, and bay leaves. Return meatballs to pot.
Add chicken broth. Cover and cook at high pressure for 15 minutes.
Quick release the pressure. Add pasta and zucchini, then cook on high pressure for 4 minutes more. Quick release again to prevent pasta from overcooking.
Serve with grated Parmesan and additional basil if desired.

notes: Gluten-free breadcrumbs and pasta are easy substitutions without adjusting cooking times.
The Parmesan rind adds depth to the broth but is optional; discard before serving.
Leftovers keep well refrigerated for up to 4 days; reheat gently to avoid mushiness.

title: Tuscan Chicken
description: Tender chicken cooked in a creamy garlic sauce with mushrooms and sun-dried tomatoes. A rich, satisfying weeknight dish.

items: 2 tbsp (28g) unsalted butter or olive oil
¼–½ cup (16–32g) freshly grated Parmesan cheese
Salt and black pepper to taste

steps: Activate the Sauté More function on the Instant Pot and allow it to reach HOT status. Pat the chicken dry and season one side with salt and pepper. Add 2 tbsp (28g) butter or oil to the hot pot, then immediately place chicken in to prevent the fat from smoking. While the first side develops color, season the exposed side with salt and pepper. Cook the initial side for 3.5 minutes until browned, flip and cook the second side for 2 minutes. Transfer chicken to a plate. (Tip: For even browning, work in two batches using the same timing.)

To the pot, add the minced garlic and allow it to become fragrant for 30 seconds. Introduce the sliced mushrooms, season them with salt and pepper, and cook for 1 minute. Sprinkle the 2 tsp (2.4g) Italian seasoning over the mushrooms and continue cooking for 1 minute more.

Combine 1 tbsp (15ml) soy sauce with ¾ cup (188ml) chicken stock in a small bowl. Pour this mixture into the pot and scrape the bottom vigorously with a wooden spoon to release all browned bits. Stir briefly.

Return the browned chicken and any accumulated juices to the pot. Set the Instant Pot to high pressure: cook for 5 minutes for thighs or 4 minutes for breasts, then allow natural pressure release for 10 minutes before manually venting any remaining pressure.

Remove the chicken and set aside. Turn on Sauté mode again and bring the liquid to a boil. Stir in the 1 cup (40g) spinach and 60–80g sun-dried tomatoes, cooking for 30 seconds. Pour in ½ cup (125ml) heavy cream and ¼–½ cup (16–32g) Parmesan cheese, stirring to combine.

In a small bowl, whisk 3.5 tbsp (32g) cornstarch with ¼ cup (63ml) cold water until smooth. Add this slurry to the sauce in three additions, stirring after each, until the sauce reaches your desired thickness. Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt, cheese, or soy sauce as needed.
Return the chicken to the pot and stir gently to coat. Serve immediately.

notes: For thighs use high pressure 5 minutes plus 10 minutes natural release; for breasts use high pressure 4 minutes plus 10 minutes natural release.
Add the cornstarch slurry gradually to avoid over-thickening the sauce.
Substitute heavy cream with half-and-half or Greek yogurt if desired, though the sauce will be less rich.
nutrition: Calories: 343 kcal · Carbs: 13 g · Protein: 37 g · Fat: 15 g · Sodium: 399 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 4 g

title: Instant Pot Tuscan Chicken Pasta
description: Creamy garlic pasta with chicken, sun-dried tomatoes, and spinach. A quick one-pot meal with a smooth, rich sauce.

items: 1 pound boneless skinless chicken breasts (about 2 breasts)
salt and freshly ground black pepper
6 ounces cream cheese (softened and cut into small pieces)
salt and freshly ground black pepper

steps: Switch the pot to sauté mode.
Coat chicken breasts with paprika, salt, and pepper on both sides.
Pour the olive oil into the hot pot.
Place chicken in the pot and sear for 2 minutes per side until golden but not fully cooked through. Transfer to a separate plate.
Melt butter in the pot, then cook the onion for 2 minutes.
Return the pot to sauté and scrape the bottom to loosen browned bits. Pour in broth, then add basil, garlic powder, Italian seasoning, milk, and pasta. Nestle the chicken back into the mixture.
Seal the lid and set the valve to sealed. Cook under high pressure for 5 minutes.
When beeping occurs, perform a controlled quick release by slowly opening the valve; if liquid spatters, close it and wait a few seconds before trying again.
Carefully open the lid. Lift the chicken to a separate plate and cover loosely with foil to keep warm while it finishes cooking gently.
Add the cream cheese to the pot and gently stir until fully dissolved and the sauce becomes smooth.
Fold in the parmesan, sun-dried tomatoes, and spinach. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
Dice the chicken and return it to the pot.
The sauce will thicken upon cooling. Ladle into bowls and top with additional parmesan if desired.

notes: The sauce thickens as it cools, so it may look looser immediately after cooking.
Let chicken rest briefly under foil after cooking to ensure it stays tender and moist.
You can substitute farfalle or another short pasta for the penne if preferred.
nutrition: Calories: 868 kcal · Carbs: 91 g · Protein: 52 g · Fat: 35 g · Sodium: 925 mg · Fiber: 9 g · Sugar: 24 g

title: Instant Pot Cold Start Yogurt (No Boil Method)
description: A no-boil method for making thick, creamy homemade yogurt using ultra-filtered milk and a yogurt starter. Ready to eat after 8 to 12 hours of incubation.

items: 2 tbsp (32g) yogurt with active bacterial cultures

steps: Optional sterilization: Add 2 cups cold water and trivet to the Instant Pot. Place a silicone whisk or spatula, glass measuring cup, and measuring spoon inside. Close the lid, set venting knob to sealing, and run on high pressure for 3 minutes with natural release. Air dry the equipment on a clean rack.
Combine 2 tbsp (32g) yogurt starter with 1 cup (250ml) ultra-filtered milk in the inner pot. Whisk until well mixed.

Pour 7 cups (1750ml) ultra-filtered milk into the pot. Whisk to combine thoroughly. Close the lid (venting knob position does not matter). Press the Yogurt button and select Yogurt Normal function. Set incubation time to 8 hours 30 minutes to 12 hours 30 minutes depending on desired tanginess; longer fermentation produces tangier yogurt. Do not open the lid or move the pot during fermentation; yogurt will be set around 6 hours 30 minutes.
Once yogurt reaches your preferred tanginess, transfer the inner pot to the refrigerator for a few hours to stop fermentation and allow further thickening. Add flavorings at this point if desired.

Optional straining for thicker yogurt: Use a yogurt strainer. After 1 hour, approximately 1.5 cups (375ml) of whey separates; after 2 hours, approximately 2.5 cups (625ml) separates; overnight straining yields approximately 4 cups (902ml) of whey and about 1 liter of Greek yogurt.

notes: Do not disturb the pot or open the lid during the first 6 hours 30 minutes of incubation to ensure proper fermentation.
Use only ultra-filtered milk and yogurt with live active cultures for best results.
Strain overnight for Greek yogurt consistency, or skip straining for a thinner yogurt.
nutrition: Calories: 122 kcal · Carbs: 9 g · Protein: 7 g · Fat: 7 g · Sodium: 92 mg · Sugar: 9 g

title: Instant Pot Vegetable Lo Mein
description: A vegetable-based lo mein with noodles and crisp vegetables in a savory sauce, cooked under pressure in about 20 minutes.

items: 8 oz noodles (spaghetti, chow mein, or hakka noodles)
1–2 tsp red chili paste (sambal oelek or similar)
¼ tsp salt, plus more to taste
¼ tsp black pepper, plus more to taste
1 carrot, peeled and cut into matchsticks
½ cup thinly sliced bell pepper
¼ cup scallion or green onion (white and green parts separated)

steps: Whisk together in a bowl: sesame oil, ginger paste, garlic, soy sauce, vinegar, chili paste, brown sugar, and broth. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Break spaghetti in half and place in the Instant Pot.
Pour the sauce mixture over the noodles. Arrange cabbage, carrot, bell pepper, and scallion whites on top (reserve broccoli).
Seal the pot, set to manual high pressure for 4 minutes.
When cooking ends, release pressure immediately by moving the valve to venting.

Carefully open the lid. Stir the noodles thoroughly with tongs to separate any clumps. Add broccoli florets, stir to combine, season with salt and pepper to taste, then close the lid (no pressure) for 5 minutes to soften the broccoli.
Open the lid, top with reserved scallion greens and sesame seeds if desired, and serve.

notes: Cut broccoli into small pieces so it cooks through in the residual heat without pressure.
Spaghetti can be substituted with thicker chow mein or hakka noodles; adjust cook time if using noodles significantly thicker than spaghetti.
Store leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days; reheat gently with a splash of water or broth.

title: Instant Pot Vegetarian Chili
description: A hearty vegetarian chili made with quinoa, sweet potatoes, black beans, and kidney beans seasoned with chili and cumin spices.

items: 2 medium sweet potatoes, peeled and ½-inch diced (about 4 cups)
2 medium red bell peppers, ¾-inch diced
1 (15 oz) can low-sodium black beans, rinsed and drained
1 (15 oz) can low-sodium red kidney beans, rinsed and drained
Nonfat plain Greek yogurt or sour cream

steps: Add olive oil to a 6-quart Instant Pot and switch to sauté mode. Once hot, add diced onion and cook until it begins to soften, about 3 minutes. Stir in sweet potatoes, bell pepper, garlic, chili powder, chipotle chili powder, cumin, and salt. Cook 1 to 2 minutes more until fragrant.
Pour in half the vegetable broth and stir, scraping the pot bottom to prevent a burn warning. Add remaining broth and quinoa, then pour tomato sauce on top without stirring.
Cover and seal the pot. Set to high pressure for 8 minutes. Once done, release pressure immediately and carefully open.
Turn off the pot and stir in black beans, kidney beans, and sugar. Replace the lid and let sit 10 minutes to thicken. Uncover, taste, adjust seasoning as needed, and serve with desired toppings.

notes: Scraping the pot bottom after adding the first broth portion prevents false burn warnings.
The 10-minute rest period allows the chili to thicken without additional cooking.
Customize toppings with avocado, cilantro, cheese, tortilla chips, or Greek yogurt.

title: Instant Pot Yogurt
description: Homemade yogurt made by heating and culturing milk in an Instant Pot. This method produces creamy yogurt in about nine hours total.

items: 2 tablespoons prepared yogurt with active cultures

steps: Sterilize the inner pot using one of two methods: pour boiling water into it and let sit 10 minutes, or add 2 cups tap water, lock the lid, and cook under high pressure for 3 minutes, then quick-release, drain, and dry.

Add milk to the inner pot, place the lid on (vent can be open or sealed), and press the yogurt button twice (or yogurt then adjust if needed) to start the boil cycle. This takes 30–60 minutes depending on your machine.
Once the Instant Pot signals the boil is done, remove the lid carefully and check that the milk reaches 180–190°F (82–88°C). Leave the pot in the unit for 5 minutes, then remove it.
Cool the milk to 105–115°F (41–46°C) on the counter for about 1 hour, or speed this up to 5–10 minutes by nestling the pot in a bowl of ice water.
Remove and discard the skin that forms on top, then whisk in 2 tablespoons starter culture (or 3 tablespoons if using skim milk) until evenly mixed.

Dry the pot if it was in the ice bath, return it to the unit, and lock the lid. Press the yogurt button, then adjust to NORMAL (not low) and set the timer to 8:00 for standard yogurt or 10:00 for tangier yogurt. Press start if your model requires it.
When the yogurt cycle ends, remove the pot from the unit, cover it with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for 8 hours without stirring.
Transfer yogurt to sealed containers and refrigerate for up to two weeks. Reserve 2–3 tablespoons as starter culture for your next batch.

notes: Using skim milk requires 3 tablespoons starter culture instead of 2.
Ice water cooling reduces waiting time from 1 hour to 5–10 minutes after the boil cycle.
Reserve a few tablespoons of finished yogurt to use as the starter culture for future batches.
nutrition: Calories: 89 kcal · Carbs: 5 g · Protein: 3 g · Fat: 2 g · Sodium: 55 mg · Sugar: 5 g

title: Instant Pot Zuppa Toscana
description: A creamy Italian sausage, kale, and potato soup finished with heavy cream and crispy bacon.

items: 1 pound (505g) Italian sausage with basil, cut into ½ inch thick slices
½ to 1 bunch (150–300g) kale, stems removed
Salt and ground black pepper to taste
Optional: ½ teaspoon red pepper flakes for spice

steps: Set the Instant Pot to Sauté Normal and add the chopped bacon. Stir occasionally as the bacon renders and crisps over 11 to 12 minutes. Transfer the cooked bacon bits to a paper towel.

Sauté the sausage slices in the bacon fat for 3 minutes, then flip and cook the other side for another 3 minutes. Stir in the chopped onion and sauté for 3 minutes more. Add the minced garlic, a pinch of dried thyme, and ½ teaspoon red pepper flakes if desired, then sauté for 1 additional minute.
Pour in ½ cup (125ml) of the chicken stock and use a wooden spoon to scrape the browned bits from the pot bottom.

Add the fish sauce (if using), the remaining 3½ cups (875ml) chicken stock, and the 820g potato cubes to the pot and stir to combine. Lock the lid and cook on high pressure for 3 minutes, then allow 10 minutes of natural pressure release.

Switch back to Sauté mode and add the 150–300g kale bunch to the simmering soup. Let it cook for 2 to 3 minutes until wilted. Pour in the 1 cup (250ml) heavy cream, then turn off heat as soon as it reaches a simmer.
Stir in the reserved bacon bits, taste the soup, and add salt and black pepper to your preference. Serve immediately.

notes: The optional fish sauce adds umami depth but can be omitted if not on hand.
Russet potatoes break down slightly during cooking, creating a naturally creamy texture.
Prepare all ingredients before starting since the sauté steps move quickly.
nutrition: Calories: 544 kcal · Carbs: 27 g · Protein: 18 g · Fat: 40 g · Sodium: 661 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Keto Chili
description: A bean-free chili made with ground beef, fire-roasted tomatoes, and chipotle peppers. It cooks under pressure in the Instant Pot.

items: 1 tablespoon chipotle chile in adobo sauce, chopped

steps: Set the Instant Pot to sauté. When hot, add oil, onion, and garlic; stir for 30 seconds. Add ground beef, break it apart, and cook until it begins to brown.
While the meat cooks, blend the canned tomatoes, chipotle chile, and corn tortillas until smooth.
In a small bowl, mix the chili powder, cumin, salt, and oregano. Once the beef is partially browned, add the spice blend and stir for 30 seconds to toast the spices.
Pour the blended tomato mixture into the pot. Rinse the blender with the ½ cup water and add that liquid to the pot as well.
Seal the lid, set to high pressure for 10 minutes. After cooking, allow pressure to release naturally for 10 minutes, then manually release any remaining pressure.
Stir, top with cheese if desired, and serve.

notes: For a lower-carb version, replace the corn tortillas with ¼ teaspoon xanthan gum while bringing the pot to pressure.
Chipotle peppers in adobo add heat and smokiness; adjust the amount to your tolerance.
Leftover chili keeps refrigerated for up to 4 days or can be frozen for up to 3 months.
nutrition: Calories: 302 kcal · Carbs: 15 g · Protein: 25 g · Fat: 16 g · Sodium: 1373 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 3 g

title: Ham Bone Soup
description: A hearty soup made with leftover ham bone, potatoes, and cabbage in a savory broth. Perfect for using up ham scraps.

items: 10 oz Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled and diced small
1 small head cabbage, cored and chopped (13 oz)

step_groups.name: Instant Pot
steps: Set to sauté mode. Spray the pot with cooking spray, then add onion, celery, and garlic. Sauté until softened, 4 to 5 minutes.
Pour in broth and water. Add carrots, potatoes, ham bone, and ham. Lock the lid and cook on high pressure for 20 minutes.
Allow pressure to release naturally. Open the pot, add cabbage, and return to high pressure for 5 minutes. Release pressure naturally or quickly.

step_groups.name: Stovetop
steps: In a large pot or Dutch oven, sauté onion, celery, and garlic in a little oil until softened, 4 to 5 minutes.
Add broth, water, carrots, potatoes, ham bone, and ham. Cover and simmer on low for 1 hour.
During the last 10 to 15 minutes, add cabbage and continue cooking until all vegetables are tender.

notes: Save ham bones from roasted ham to make this soup; they release rich flavor during cooking.
For deeper flavor, sauté the ham bone briefly in step 1 before adding liquid.
Leftover soup keeps refrigerated for 3 days or freezes well for up to 2 months.
nutrition: Calories: 124 kcal · Carbs: 15 g · Protein: 10.5 g · Fat: 2.5 g · Sodium: 437 mg · Fiber: 3.5 g · Sugar: 4.5 g

title: Instant Pot Palak Paneer
description: A North Indian spinach and cottage cheese curry. Creamy, aromatic, and ready in minutes.

items: 2 cups paneer or cottage cheese, cut into bite-sized cubes

steps: Switch to sauté mode and heat oil, then add cumin seeds until they crackle.
Add ginger, garlic, green chili, and onion; sauté for 2 minutes.
Stir in tomato and all spices; cook for 2 minutes.
Pour in water and scrape the pot bottom to deglaze. Add spinach, press cancel, seal the lid with vent in sealing position, and set to high pressure for 2 minutes.
After the beep, wait 5 minutes before manually releasing pressure.
Using an immersion blender, puree the mixture until smooth and creamy.
Fold in paneer and garam masala, then let sit 5 minutes for flavors to meld.
Serve with naan, roti, or paratha.

notes: Deglazing the pot removes stuck bits and adds depth to the sauce.
A natural pressure release preserves the tender texture of the spinach.
Paneer is added after blending to keep it in cubes rather than breaking down into the sauce.
nutrition: Calories: 417 kcal · Carbs: 13 g · Protein: 20 g · Fat: 32 g · Sodium: 747 mg · Fiber: 4 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Paneer Butter Masala
description: A creamy North Indian curry with soft paneer cheese in a rich tomato-based gravy flavored with aromatic spices and finished with cream and honey.

items: 1 lb paneer or cottage cheese, cut into chunks (or firm tofu for vegan)
1 onion, cut into large pieces (about 1½ cups)
1 inch ginger, grated or finely diced
¼ cup heavy cream (or coconut milk for vegan)
1–2 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, or to taste

steps: Bundle the cinnamon, green cardamom, black cardamom, black peppercorns, and cloves in cheesecloth and tie securely so spices cannot escape.

Add to the Instant Pot: onion, ginger, garlic, tomatoes, cashews, fenugreek leaves, water, turmeric, coriander powder, ground cumin, chili powder, butter, cumin seeds, green chili if using, and the spice bundle. Do not add paneer, cream, or honey yet. Seal the lid with the vent in sealing position.
Cook on high pressure for 8 minutes.
Allow pressure to release naturally (or use a 15-minute natural release if preferred).

Remove and discard the spice bundle. Using an immersion blender, puree the contents until completely smooth. If using a regular blender, let cool slightly first, then blend in batches and return to the pot.
Stir in the cream and honey until combined.
Add paneer chunks and stir gently to coat with sauce. Let rest, covered, for 5 minutes.
Serve garnished with crushed fenugreek leaves or fresh cilantro.

step_groups.name: Stovetop Pressure Cooker
steps: Follow the same preparation and ingredient steps above. Cook under pressure for 2 whistles on medium-high flame, then allow pressure to release naturally.

notes: The spice pouch keeps whole spices from getting stuck in the blended sauce; remove it before pureeing.
Toast or soak the cashews briefly to make them blend more smoothly into the gravy.
Paneer absorbs the sauce best when added at the end; do not overcook or it will become rubbery.
nutrition: Calories: 374 kcal · Carbs: 17 g · Protein: 14 g · Fat: 29 g · Sodium: 483 mg · Fiber: 4 g · Sugar: 7 g

title: Instant Pot Chicken and Broccoli
description: A quick Asian-style chicken and broccoli dish cooked under pressure and finished with a savory pan sauce. Ready in 20 minutes.

items: 1 pound chicken breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces
¾ cup water or beef stock

steps: Add chicken, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, water, brown sugar, and sesame oil to the pot. Stir to combine.
Lock the lid in place and set the vent to sealing position.
Select high pressure and cook for 3 minutes. Once pressurized, the pot will cook and beep when time is up. Quick-release the steam and open the lid carefully.

step_groups.name: To thicken the sauce and finish
steps: Switch to sauté mode. Mix cornstarch with a little water to form a slurry, then stir it into the pot until the sauce thickens. Add another tablespoon of cornstarch slurry if needed for desired thickness.
Stir in the broccoli, cover the lid, and let it steam for 2 minutes. Serve over rice and top with sesame seeds if desired.

notes: Quick-release is used here to avoid overcooking the chicken; be careful of steam when opening.
For thicker sauce, prepare the cornstarch slurry before starting sauté mode so it mixes smoothly.
nutrition: Calories: 408 kcal · Carbs: 26 g · Protein: 57 g · Fat: 9 g · Sodium: 1407 mg · Fiber: 6 g · Sugar: 6 g

title: Sticky Rice with Sausage
description: A one-pot glutinous rice dish with Chinese sausage and mushrooms, similar to the dim sum classic lo mai gai. Savory, tender, and aromatic.

steps: Soak the dried mushrooms in hot water until softened, about 5–10 minutes. Drain and chop.
Add the glutinous rice, chopped sausage, water, soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, salt, and rehydrated mushrooms to the pressure cooker inner pot. Stir to combine.
Cook on high pressure for 10 minutes. Let pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then manually release any remaining pressure.
Open the lid, stir gently until the liquid is fully absorbed, and serve.

notes: Soak mushrooms while prepping other ingredients to save time.
Substitute fresh mushrooms or other dried varieties if shiitake is unavailable; adjust soaking time for fresh mushrooms.
For extra richness, use chicken broth instead of water.
nutrition: Calories: 218 kcal · Carbs: 41 g · Protein: 4 g · Fat: 4 g · Sodium: 58 mg · Fiber: 1 g

title: Pressure Cooker Dae Ji Bulgogi Korean Spicy Pork
description: Tender sliced pork shoulder braised in a spicy-sweet gochujang sauce, then seared with onions to caramelize. A savory Korean main that's naturally low-carb.

items: 1 lb boneless pork shoulder, cut into ¼–½ inch slices

steps: Combine pork slices, sliced onion, ginger, garlic, soy sauce, rice wine, sesame oil, Splenda, gochujang, gochugaru, and water in the pressure cooker pot. Marinate at least 1 hour, or up to 24 hours if time allows.
Cook at high pressure for 20 minutes. Allow pressure to release naturally for 10 minutes, then manually release any remaining pressure.
Heat a wide cast iron or heavy skillet over high heat until very hot.
Transfer the pork and sliced onions to the hot pan and add ¼ to ½ cup of the braising sauce from the pot.
Stir and let the sauce sizzle and caramelize, cooking until the liquid evaporates and coats the meat, about 5–7 minutes.
Once the sauce is reduced and onions are soft, transfer to a serving dish and top with sesame seeds and green scallions.
Serve any remaining sauce on the side.

notes: Marinating overnight deepens the flavor; even 1 hour yields good results.
The high-heat pan sear is essential—it caramelizes the sauce and adds textural contrast to the tender pork.
Reserve leftover sauce to drizzle over rice or vegetables.
nutrition: Calories: 189 kcal · Carbs: 9 g · Protein: 15 g · Fat: 9 g · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 3 g

title: Lamb Rogan Josh
description: A classic Kashmiri lamb curry made with yogurt, tomato, and warm spices. Rich and aromatic, ready in under an hour.

items: 1 lb lamb leg, cut into cubes

steps: Combine lamb, onion, garlic, ginger, yogurt, tomato paste, cilantro, garam masala, smoked paprika, salt, turmeric, cinnamon, and cayenne in a bowl. Marinate for 1 hour if time permits, or proceed directly to cooking.
Transfer the mixture to the Instant Pot.
Cook at high pressure for 15 minutes.
Allow pressure to release naturally for 10 minutes, then manually release any remaining pressure.
Serve immediately.

notes: For dairy-free, substitute yogurt with ¼ cup lemon juice plus ¼ cup water.
If using beef or chicken instead of lamb, consult Instant Pot time charts to adjust cook time accordingly.
Adjust cayenne to your heat preference; start with ¼ tsp and add more as needed.
nutrition: Calories: 127 kcal · Carbs: 6 g · Protein: 17 g · Fat: 4 g · Sodium: 665 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Chana Masala / Punjabi Chole Masala
description: A spiced chickpea curry from North India, featuring tender legumes in a tomato and aromatic base. Vegan, gluten-free, and protein-rich.

items: ¾ cup tomatoes, chopped (or 1 tbsp tomato paste)
½ tsp cayenne or red chili powder

step_groups.name: Preparation
steps: Soak washed chickpeas in 3 cups water overnight or for at least 4 hours.

step_groups.name: Instant Pot Method
steps: Switch to sauté mode. Heat oil, then add cumin seeds, bay leaves, peppercorns, cinnamon, ginger, garlic, and green chili.
Once ginger and garlic turn golden, add onions and sauté 3 minutes.
Stir in tomatoes and all spices except the mango powder. Drain chickpea soaking liquid, add drained chickpeas and 1.5 cups cooking water, and mix thoroughly.
Switch to bean/chili mode (high pressure) for 35 minutes with vent sealed.
Allow pressure to release naturally when cooking completes. If the float valve hasn't dropped after 20 minutes, manually release pressure.
For thicker curry, mash some chickpeas with a hand masher or ladle back. Stir in fenugreek leaves and mango powder. Switch to sauté mode and boil 2 minutes.
Top with cilantro and serve with naan or bhatura.

step_groups.name: Stovetop Pressure Cooker Method
steps: Heat oil over medium-high heat. Add cumin seeds, bay leaves, peppercorns, cinnamon, ginger, garlic, and green chili.
Once ginger and garlic turn golden, add onions and sauté 3 minutes.
Stir in tomatoes and all spices except the mango powder. Drain chickpea soaking liquid, add drained chickpeas and 1.5 cups cooking water, and mix thoroughly.
Pressure cook for 6–7 whistles until chickpeas break easily between two fingers. Let pressure release naturally.
For thicker curry, mash some chickpeas with a hand masher or ladle back. Stir in fenugreek leaves and mango powder.
Adjust water if curry is too thick. Taste and adjust salt. Bring to a quick boil if water is added.
Top with cilantro and serve with naan or bhatura.

notes: Soaking chickpeas overnight reduces cooking time and aids digestibility.
For canned chickpeas, skip soaking and reduce pressure cooking to 10 minutes on high.
Mashing a few chickpeas thickens the gravy without changing the texture significantly.
nutrition: Calories: 255 kcal · Carbs: 38 g · Protein: 11 g · Fat: 7 g · Sodium: 1271 mg · Fiber: 10 g · Sugar: 7 g

title: Instant Pot Rajma Masala (Red Kidney Beans Curry)
description: A North Indian curry of red kidney beans simmered with aromatic spices, tomatoes, and onions. Cooked with brown rice using the pot-in-pot method for a complete one-pot meal.

ingredient_groups.name: Curry
items: 1 cup red kidney beans, soaked 4 hours or overnight and drained
½ tsp red chili powder, or to taste

steps: Set the Instant Pot to sauté on high heat and wait for the display to show HOT. Pour in oil, add cumin seeds and green chili, then let them bloom for about 30 seconds.
Stir in onions, ginger, and garlic. Sauté for 3 minutes until fragrant.
Add tomatoes and all the spices (turmeric, ground cumin, coriander, garam masala, chili powder, and salt). Sauté for 2 minutes.
Add the drained beans and 1½ cups water; stir well to combine.
If making rice: place the trivet in the pot. Combine the rice, 1¼ cups water, ghee, and salt in a bowl, then carefully set the bowl on the trivet.
Seal the lid and switch to Bean/Chili mode, which runs at high pressure for 30 minutes. When done, allow pressure to release naturally before opening.
If rice was cooked, use tongs to remove the rice bowl, then lift out the trivet.
Stir in the lime juice and taste for seasoning. Top with cilantro and serve alongside the rice or with roti.

step_groups.name: Stovetop Pressure Cooker Alternative
steps: Follow the same sautéing and ingredient steps. Seal the cooker and bring to full pressure over high heat, then cook at pressure for 1–2 whistles. Reduce to medium heat and continue for 15–20 minutes until beans are tender.

notes: Rajma keeps refrigerated up to 4 days; freeze for longer storage.
Use canned beans if short on time: add one 15 oz can (drained) instead and reduce cooking time to 5–8 minutes after sealing.
Adjust chili powder and salt at the end to suit your heat and salt preference.
nutrition: Calories: 227 kcal · Carbs: 37.1 g · Protein: 11.89 g · Fat: 4.53 g · Sodium: 594 mg · Fiber: 8.9 g · Sugar: 4.56 g

title: Instant Pot Kheer (Payasam)
description: A creamy Indian rice pudding made with basmati rice and milk, scented with cardamom and saffron, studded with nuts and raisins.

items: ¼ cup mixed nuts, chopped or sliced (cashews, almonds, walnuts, pistachios) (optional)

steps: Clean the Instant Pot insert, lid, and sealing ring. If available, use a dedicated sweet-foods sealing ring.
Rinse the rice 2–4 times under water until the water runs clear.
Set the pot to Sauté mode. Warm ½ tbsp ghee until melted, then add rice and cook for 30 seconds, stirring (this step is optional but builds flavor; skip it if preferred).
Pour in the water, milk, sugar, cardamom powder, and saffron. Stir well to combine.
Add nuts now if desired, or roast them separately and fold in after cooking (see roasting section).
Seal the lid with the vent set to sealing. Run the Porridge program on high pressure for 20 minutes. When finished, allow pressure to release naturally.
Open the pot and stir. The kheer may look thin but will thicken as it cools for about 30 minutes. For thicker texture, run Sauté mode for 2–3 minutes while stirring.

step_groups.name: Roasting Nuts & Raisins
steps: Heat a small pan over medium-low heat. Add ½ tbsp ghee and warm until melted.
Add nuts and toast for 1 minute until lightly golden, then add raisins and toast for another minute.
Transfer to a plate immediately to prevent burning. Stir into the finished kheer.

notes: The pudding thickens significantly as it cools; don't over-reduce it on Sauté mode.
Adjust sugar to taste before pressure cooking, as sweetness cannot be easily modified afterward.
Store covered in the refrigerator for up to 4 days; reheat gently with a splash of milk.
nutrition: Calories: 286 kcal · Carbs: 42 g · Protein: 9 g · Fat: 10 g · Sodium: 80 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 26 g

title: Salisbury Steak Meatballs
description: Ground beef and turkey meatballs braised in a savory mushroom gravy. A lighter take on the classic comfort dish.

items: ⅓ cup whole wheat seasoned breadcrumbs

step_groups.name: Prep
steps: Finely mince 1 oz of the mushrooms and set aside. Heat oil in a large pot on medium-high heat, add onions, and cook until golden brown, 4 to 5 minutes. Transfer half the onions to a separate bowl.

In a large bowl, combine the reserved onions, both ground meats, minced mushrooms, breadcrumbs, egg, 1 tbsp tomato paste, ¼ cup broth, ¾ tsp salt, and pepper. Mix gently and form into 20 small meatballs.
In a small bowl, whisk flour and 1 cup broth until smooth. Stir in the remaining onions, remaining 1 tbsp tomato paste, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, and mustard powder. Set sauce aside.

step_groups.name: Stovetop
steps: Heat the remaining 1 tsp oil in a large deep skillet over medium heat. Brown meatballs in batches, about 2 minutes per side, until they release easily from the pan.
Add sliced mushrooms, ⅛ tsp salt, and pepper. Pour sauce over meatballs, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer 25 minutes until cooked through. Garnish with parsley.

step_groups.name: Slow Cooker
steps: Brown meatballs in a skillet with the remaining 1 tsp oil, then transfer to slow cooker.
Add mushrooms, ⅛ tsp salt, pepper, and sauce. Cover and cook on low 6 to 8 hours until tender. Garnish with parsley.

step_groups.name: Instant Pot
steps: Switch to sauté mode and heat the remaining 1 tsp oil. Brown meatballs in batches, about 2 minutes per side, until they release easily from the pot.
Add mushrooms, ⅛ tsp salt, pepper, and pour sauce over meatballs. Close lid and cook on high pressure 10 minutes.
Use quick or natural release. Garnish with parsley before serving.

notes: Meatballs are fully browned when they release easily from the pan without sticking; do not rush the searing step.
This recipe yields approximately 271 calories and 28 g protein per serving, making it a lean protein option.
Refrigerate leftovers up to 3 days; reheat gently on stovetop or microwave.
nutrition: Calories: 271 kcal · Carbs: 12 g · Protein: 28 g · Fat: 12 g · Sodium: 741 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 3 g

title: Steamed Broccoli
description: Fresh broccoli florets steamed under pressure until tender-crisp. Ready in under 15 minutes with minimal prep.

steps: Wash and cut broccoli into large, even-sized florets.
Pour water into the pressure cooker insert and place the steamer basket inside. Arrange broccoli florets on the basket in a single layer.
Lock the lid and set the pressure cooker to manual or pressure cook mode at high pressure for 0 minutes with the vent sealed. (For crunchier broccoli, use low pressure for 0 minutes.)
When the cycle finishes and the cooker beeps, manually quick-release the pressure immediately. Carefully lift out the steamer basket—it will be hot.
Optionally run the basket under cold water for about 1 minute to stop cooking, or serve right away.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.

notes: Quick release immediately after the beep to prevent overcooking; timing is critical for the right texture.
For softer broccoli, increase cooking time to 2 minutes; for very tender, use 3–4 minutes.
Works with 1 to 2 broccoli heads; adjust water to 1 cup per head if cooking more.
nutrition: Calories: 103 kcal · Carbs: 20 g · Protein: 8 g · Fat: 1 g · Sodium: 106 mg · Fiber: 7 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Unstuffed Cabbage Rolls
description: A one-pot dinner combining ground beef, cabbage, rice, and tomatoes with the flavors of traditional cabbage rolls but without the rolling work. High in protein and budget-friendly.

items: 1 medium head cabbage, cored and chopped (9 cups)

step_groups.name: Instant Pot Method
steps: Engage the sauté function. Coat the insert with cooking spray, add beef and salt, then cook 5 minutes while breaking the meat apart until no pink remains. Stir in onion, garlic, marjoram, and pepper. Pour in tomato sauce, paprika, broth, and raisins. Cover and pressurize on high for 15 minutes.
Release pressure quickly. Mix in rice and cabbage, seal again, and cook on high pressure for 3 minutes. Quick release and serve.

step_groups.name: Stovetop Method
steps: Set a Dutch oven or large covered pot to medium-high heat and coat with cooking spray. Brown the beef with salt for about 5 minutes, breaking it into small pieces. Mix in onion, garlic, marjoram, and pepper. Stir in tomato sauce, ¼ cup water, paprika, broth, and raisins. Reduce to medium-low, cover, and simmer 25 minutes.
Fold in the cooked rice and chopped cabbage, then cook covered for 8 to 10 minutes until cabbage softens. Serve with extra rice on the side if desired.

notes: Brown the meat thoroughly before adding other ingredients to ensure even cooking and flavor.
For stovetop, the ¼ cup water addition helps maintain moisture during the longer cooking time.
This dish reheats well and keeps refrigerated for up to 4 days.
nutrition: Calories: 338 kcal · Carbs: 36 g · Protein: 30.5 g · Fat: 8 g · Sodium: 808 mg · Fiber: 8 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Instant Pot Vegetable Rice Pulao
description: A one-pot Indian rice dish combining basmati rice with mixed vegetables and warm spices for a mild, balanced flavor.

items: 2 cups mixed vegetables (carrots, green beans, peas, corn, edamame; frozen or fresh)
½ tsp cayenne or red chili powder

steps: Turn the Instant Pot to sauté mode and heat. Add ghee, then toast the cumin seeds, cinnamon stick, bay leaf, star anise, and cloves for 30 seconds until the seeds begin to pop and release their aroma.
Add the green chili, sliced onion, ginger, and garlic. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the onion becomes translucent.
Stir in the chopped tomato, turmeric, cayenne powder, and garam masala. Mix until combined.
Add the diced potato and mixed vegetables. Stir to incorporate.
Pour in the rice and water. Stir well to distribute the rice evenly and break up any clumps. If any mixture sticks to the pot bottom, scrape it free. Seal the lid with the vent in sealing position.
Switch to manual or high-pressure mode and set for 4 minutes at high pressure.
When the beep sounds, allow pressure to drop naturally for 10 minutes, then manually release any remaining pressure.
Gently fluff the rice with a fork.
Remove the inner pot from the Instant Pot base immediately to prevent the bottom layer from overcooking.

notes: Lift out the cooking vessel from the heated base right after cooking stops to avoid the rice on the bottom from becoming overcooked.
Frozen vegetables work fine; add them directly without thawing.
Serve alongside yogurt-based raita or cucumber condiment for balance.
nutrition: Calories: 286 kcal · Carbs: 55.9 g · Protein: 6.3 g · Fat: 4.1 g · Sodium: 609 mg · Fiber: 4.9 g · Sugar: 3.2 g

title: Instant Pot Short Ribs
description: Beef short ribs braised with red wine, stock and aromatics until tender, then served with a glossy pan gravy.

items: 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil (+ more if needed)
½ cup dry red wine (see note)

step_groups.name: Method
steps: Select sauté on the Instant Pot. Add 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil and warm it briefly while seasoning the ribs.

Apply a generous salt-and-pepper coating to every face of each rib. Working with half at a time, sear the ribs in the hot oil for about 4-5 minutes on each side. Transfer the browned batch to a plate, then repeat with the rest, adding oil only if the pot is dry.
Put the onion, carrots and celery into the pot and cook for 3-4 minutes, until softened. Stir in 2 tablespoons minced garlic and cook for about 30 seconds.
Add ½ cup dry red wine, or the same amount of extra stock, and scrape the base thoroughly. Simmer for 1 minute, then cancel sauté.
Stir in 1 cup beef broth, 2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar, 1 tablespoon brown sugar, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, 2 thyme sprigs and 1 bay leaf. Return the ribs and settle them evenly into the liquid.
Lock the lid, set the valve to sealing and cook on high pressure for 45 minutes.
Leave the pressure to release naturally for at least 15 minutes before opening.

Move the ribs to a plate and cover loosely with foil. Bring the liquid to a lively simmer on sauté. Whisk together 2 tablespoons cornstarch and 3 tablespoons cold water, then gradually whisk this slurry into the pot. Continue whisking for 2-4 minutes, until thickened. For a richer finish, melt in 1-2 tablespoons unsalted butter, then cancel sauté.
Spoon the gravy over the short ribs to serve.

notes: A dry red wine gives the gravy depth; use extra beef broth for an alcohol-free version.
nutrition: Calories: 529 kcal · Carbs: 19 g · Protein: 46 g · Fat: 27 g · Sodium: 419 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 8 g

title: Instant Pot Lasagna
description: A compact layered lasagna with meat sauce, seasoned ricotta, mozzarella and tender pasta, cooked in a pan above the water.

items: ½ pound ground beef or ground Italian sausage
¼ teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes (optional)
6-8 no-boil or oven ready lasagna noodles

step_groups.name: Method
steps: For a meat lasagna, select sauté, warm 2 teaspoons olive oil and add the ground meat, onion, 1 teaspoon Italian seasoning, 1 teaspoon minced garlic and 1/2 teaspoon kosher salt. Break up the meat and cook until fully browned.
Cancel sauté, lift the cooked meat mixture out of the pot and reserve it for layering.
Add 1-1/2 cups water to the inner pot and scrape away every browned spot. Set the trivet over the water.
Mix the ricotta, egg, 1 teaspoon Italian seasoning, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/4 teaspoon red-pepper flakes and 1/3 cup Parmesan in a bowl.

Coat the base of a 7 by 3-inch springform or oven-safe cake pan with a little sauce. Break noodles to cover the base, then layer half the ricotta, half the meat, sauce and a light scattering of mozzarella. Repeat with noodles, the remaining ricotta and meat, more sauce and mozzarella. Finish with noodles, a generous sauce layer and the remaining cheese in the same 7 by 3-inch pan.
Make a foil sling beneath the pan, lower it onto the trivet and cover the top loosely with foil.
Seal the cooker and cook on high pressure for 27 minutes.
At the end of cooking, wait at least 10 minutes before releasing the remaining pressure quickly.
For a browned top, transfer the uncovered pan to a tray and broil for 3-6 minutes, watching closely. Alternatively, fit an air-fryer lid at 360°F for 3 to 5 minutes.
Rest the lasagna in its pan for 10 minutes before releasing the sides, slicing and serving.

notes: The foil sling makes the hot lasagna pan safer to lift from the cooker.
nutrition: Calories: 442 kcal · Carbs: 36 g · Protein: 29 g · Fat: 18 g · Sodium: 1279 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 9 g

title: Instant Pot French Dip
description: Tender shredded chuck, sweet onions and melted Swiss cheese served in crusty bread with concentrated beef jus for dipping.

items: 2 - 3 pounds (965g) USDA choice grade chuck roast/Canada AAA grade blade roast (, 2 - 2.5 inches thick)
1 cup (250ml) unsalted chicken stock or high quality beef stock
1 tablespoon (15ml) fish sauce ((can substitute with regular soy sauce))

step_groups.name: Method
steps: Heat the cooker on Sauté More until HOT, about 8 mins. Pat the chuck dry, season the first face with salt and pepper and add 1 tbsp olive oil. Lay the seasoned face down, season the upper face and brown each side for 10 minutes. Skip browning only if using a dump-and-cook approach.

Transfer the roast out. Cook the sliced onions for 3 - 4 mins, then reserve roughly ⅓ of them. Add the garlic, rosemary, thyme and 2 bay leaves; stir for another minute. If sauté switches off after 30 minutes, turn it on again.
Pour in 1 cup unsalted stock and scrape the base with a wooden spoon until no browned residue remains stuck.

Mix in 1 tbsp (15ml) soy sauce, 1 tbsp (15ml) fish sauce and 1 tbsp (15ml) Worcestershire sauce. Return the chuck and push it partly below the liquid. Cook on high pressure for 45 minutes, followed by a 25-minute natural release.

Move the beef to a bowl and pull it apart with 2 forks. Meanwhile, bring the jus to a boil on sauté, return the reserved onions and reduce for 5 - 10 minutes. Adjust the seasoning and remove excess fat if needed.

If melting the cheese in the oven, heat it to 350°F. Split the 1 loaf of French bread, cover with Swiss cheese and warm until the cheese softens. Add the beef and onions, and serve the hot jus alongside.

notes: Skim or separate excess fat from the jus before reducing it.
nutrition: Calories: 369 kcal · Carbs: 12 g · Protein: 33 g · Fat: 21 g · Sodium: 410 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Red Lentils Curry (Masoor Dal)
description: A quick vegan masoor dal of red lentils, tomato, warming spices and lime, with an optional pot-in-pot rice method.

items: 3 cups Water (or reduce to 2.5 cups for a thicker dal)
1/2 teaspoon Red Chili powder (Mirchi powder) (adjust to taste, I used mild Kashmiri red chili powder)

step_groups.name: Instant Pot
steps: Heat the cooker on sauté. Add the oil and cumin seeds, and cook for 30 seconds.
Once the cumin darkens slightly, add the green chilli, onion, ginger and garlic. Stir frequently for 3 minutes, until the onion is golden.
Mix in the tomato and spices and continue sautéing for 2 minutes.
Tip in the rinsed lentils and water, stirring to combine.
For pot-in-pot rice, set in the trivet, combine all the basmati-rice ingredients in a bowl and place it on top. Fit the lid with the valve at sealing.
Cancel sauté, seal the lid and cook on high pressure for 5 mins.
When cooking finishes, leave the cooker alone for 5 minutes, then vent the remainder manually; this is a 5-minute natural release.
Open the cooker, stir lime juice through the dal and finish with cilantro. Serve with rice, roti or naan.

step_groups.name: Stovetop pressure cooker
steps: Prepare the ingredients as for the electric method. Bring the stovetop cooker to 1 whistle, roughly 5 minutes, then wait for it to depressurise by itself.

step_groups.name: Stovetop pot
steps: Prepare the aromatics in a large pot over medium heat. After adding the lentils and water, bring them to the boil, cover, lower the heat and simmer for 15 to 20 minutes, until tender.

notes: Use 2.5 cups water instead of 3 cups when you prefer a thicker dal.
nutrition: Calories: 217 kcal · Carbs: 32 g · Protein: 12 g · Fat: 4 g · Sodium: 640 mg · Fiber: 15 g · Sugar: 3 g

title: Instant Pot Roast Beef
description: A small eye-of-round or top-sirloin roast cooked to medium rare, rested and sliced with a savoury pan gravy.

items: 2.5 - 3 pounds beef (Eye of Round or Top Sirloin) (, 2.5 inches thick)
1 cup (250ml) unsalted chicken stock or high-quality unsalted beef stock

step_groups.name: Method
steps: Choose Sauté More and wait for HOT, about 8 mins. Add 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil and coat the base. Pat the roast dry; of its 6 faces, sear only the 4 long sides. Season the first face and brown it for 4 mins. Season the opposite face and brown for 3 mins 30 secs, then sear each of the other 2 sides for 2 mins 30 secs. Set the beef aside.
Add the remaining 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil and cook the onion for 3 mins. Stir in 2 bay leaves, ¼ tsp (0.3g) rosemary and the garlic for 30 seconds.
Pour in 1 cup (250ml) unsalted stock and use a wooden spoon to free all browned residue from the base.

Stir in 1 tbsp (15ml) soy sauce and insert the rack. Put the beef on the rack and seal. For a 2.5-inch-thick roast, cook on high pressure for 3 minutes with a 25-minute natural release. For a 3.25-inch-thick roast, use high pressure for 3 minutes and a 30-minute natural release. Keep Keep Warm active. Check the centre: medium rare is 120°F. If it is low, close the lid and continue on Keep Warm; expect a rise of 7°F - 10°F per 5 minutes, checking every 5 - 10 minutes according to size.

At 120°F, move the roast to a board, cover loosely and rest for 8 - 10 minutes. Simmer the pot liquid on sauté and decide whether it needs another 1 tbsp soy sauce. Blend 1.5 tbsp (12g) flour with 3 tbsp (45ml) water, then whisk it into the gravy one third at a time until sufficiently thick. Adjust salt and pepper.
Carve across the grain. Slice eye of round very thinly for deli-style beef; cut top sirloin into steak-size portions and serve with the gravy.

notes: Use a reliable instant-read thermometer; the final temperature matters more than elapsed time.
nutrition: Calories: 362 kcal · Carbs: 6 g · Protein: 52 g · Fat: 13 g · Sodium: 309 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Instant Pot Ham
description: A cooked ham warmed under pressure in a pineapple-and-honey glaze, with an optional glossy reduction.

items: 2 tablespoons water (for slurry + more for pressure cooking)

step_groups.name: Ham
steps: Set a trivet in the inner pot. Add 1 cup cold water for a 6-quart cooker, or 1-½ cups for an 8-quart cooker.
Whisk the pineapple juice, honey and brown sugar together to make the glaze.
Remove all packaging and plastic caps from the ham. Set it on a large sheet of heavy-duty foil and turn up the edges into a bowl. If spiral cut, ease the slices apart, then pour over the glaze.
Close the foil securely around the ham, place it on the trivet and lock the lid with the valve at sealing.

For spiral-sliced ham, calculate 2 minutes per pound on high pressure, then add 10 minutes when using heavy-duty foil or 5 minutes for regular foil. For an unsliced ham, calculate 3 minutes per pound and again add 10 minutes for heavy-duty foil or 5 minutes for regular foil.
After cooking, leave the pressure to release completely by itself, about 10-15 minutes.
Check that the centre has reached at least 145°F. If it has not, pressure-cook on high for a further 3-5 minutes.
Lift out the ham, open the foil and save the liquid collected inside. Rest the meat on a board for 10 minutes before carving.
After the 10-minute rest, slice and serve with the reserved liquid or the reduced glaze.

step_groups.name: Optional glaze reduction
steps: Remove the trivet and discard the pressure-cooking water. Return the inner pot, add the liquid saved from the foil and bring it to a bubble on sauté.
Combine 1 tablespoon cornstarch with 2 tablespoons water. Whisk the slurry into the bubbling glaze continuously for about 3-5 minutes, until reduced and thick, then pour it over the ham.

notes: Calculate pressure time from the ham's weight and the type of foil used.
nutrition: Calories: 267 kcal · Carbs: 8 g · Protein: 32 g · Fat: 10 g · Sodium: 1795 mg · Sugar: 8 g

title: Instant Pot Pork Roast
description: Pork shoulder cooked with potatoes, carrots and browned mushrooms in a rich, lightly tangy gravy.

items: 2 (700g or ~1⅕ lb) large russet potatoes (, cut into large chunks)
8 (228g or ~½lb) large cremini mushrooms (, roughly chopped)
1 tablespoon (15ml) grapeseed oil or olive oil
Kosher salt & ground black pepper to taste
Optional Garnish: Italian parsley (, finely chopped)

step_groups.name: Method
steps: Heat the cooker on Sauté More until HOT. Season the pork well. Add 1 tbsp (15ml) oil, coating the base, and brown the meat for exactly 5 minutes per side without repeatedly turning it. Transfer to a board and rest for 5 minutes.

Melt 2 tbsp (28g) butter in the pot. Add the mushrooms, season lightly and stir to coat. Let their liquid evaporate, then stir from time to time until browned and slightly crisp, about 7 to 12 minutes.
While the mushrooms cook, cut the rested pork into 0.5-inch slices.
Stir in the garlic for about 20 seconds. Add the carrots and 2 bay leaves and sauté for another 2 minutes.
Splash in balsamic vinegar and scrape the base clean. Stir through 1 cup (250ml) chicken stock and 2 tbsp (30ml) soy sauce.
Return the sliced pork and its juices, then arrange the potatoes on top. Seal and cook on high pressure for 5 minutes, followed by a 10-minute natural release. Vent and open carefully.

Transfer the pork and vegetables to a platter. Season the gravy if needed. Combine 2 tbsp cornstarch and 2 tbsp cold water, then incorporate the slurry in three small additions, stopping when the sauce reaches the preferred thickness. Return everything to the pot and coat gently.
Serve the pork, vegetables and mushroom gravy together.

notes: Browning the mushrooms until their released moisture evaporates gives the gravy a deeper flavour.
nutrition: Calories: 477 kcal · Carbs: 41 g · Protein: 35 g · Fat: 20 g · Sodium: 674 mg · Fiber: 4 g · Sugar: 5 g

title: Instant Pot Birria Tacos
description: Mexican birria with tender shredded meat and a chile-rich broth, served as a bowl or folded into crisp cheese tacos.

items: 3 pounds beef (chuck roast, beef shank, short ribs, oxtail), lamb, or goat meat
3 (47.5g) dried ancho chilies ((very mild, smoky, 1000 – 2000 SHU))
3 (13.5g) dried guajillo chilies ((sweet, smoky, not very spicy, 2500–5000 SHU))
1 (8g) new Mexican dried chiles ((mild, sweet 800 - 1400 SHU))
Optional: 1 (0.7g) dried chile de árbol ((quite spicy, 15000 - 30000 SHU))
½ (2.4g) cinnamon stick (or ½ teaspoon (1g) cinnamon powder)
2 teaspoons (2g) dried Mexican oregano or oregano
2 cups (500ml) unsalted chicken stock or high-quality unsalted beef stock
Good melting cheese (i.e. Oaxaca cheese or mozzarella cheese) (, freshly grated)

step_groups.name: Method
steps: Stem and seed the dried chiles, then cut them smaller. For a milder birria omit chile de árbol; for more heat add 1 extra chile de árbol.
Put the dried chiles in the cooker and heat on Sauté More, stirring occasionally. Once fragrant, about 4 to 5 mins, remove them.

Wait for HOT. Dry and season one side of the meat. Add 1 tbsp (15ml) vegetable oil and coat the base. Lay the seasoned face down, season the other side and brown for 5 minutes. Turn and brown the second face for another 5 minutes, then set aside.

Add the other 1 tbsp (15ml) oil and sauté the onions for 3 minutes. Stir in garlic, 2 bay leaves, ½ (2.4g) cinnamon stick, 2 tsp (2g) oregano, 1½ tsp (3g) cumin, 1 tsp (1.8g) coriander and 2 tsp (10g) tomato paste for another minute.
Add 1 cup (250ml) stock and scrape the base completely clean with a wooden spoon.

Mix in 2 tbsp (30ml) apple-cider vinegar, 1 tbsp (15ml) soy sauce, 1 tbsp (15ml) fish sauce and the remaining 1 cup (250ml) stock. Submerge the toasted chiles and place the browned meat above them. Seal and use the timing for the chosen meat: 1.5- to 2-inch beef shank or chuck pieces need 40 minutes at high pressure plus 15 minutes natural release; 1.5- to 2-inch short ribs need 42 minutes plus 15 minutes natural release; a 3lbs bone-in lamb shoulder measuring 3.25 inches needs 2 hours plus 15 minutes natural release.

Lift the meat into a bowl. Remove and discard the bay leaves and cinnamon, leaving the chiles. Blend the sauce smooth with an immersion blender, shred the beef with two forks and return it to the pot.
Taste and add salt as required; roughly 4 - 6 large pinches is a useful reference.
Serve with grated melting cheese, some sauce for dipping, diced white onion and cilantro.

For tacos, follow 3 safeguards: 1) coat both faces of each tortilla in sauce, 2) oil cast iron or use a little oil for non-stick cookware, and 3) heat the skillet fully over medium before frying. Put in the wet tortilla, add cheese and let it melt. When the tortilla begins to crisp, add birria meat, fold and serve immediately with lime and dipping broth.

notes: Select the pressure time by meat type and thickness; the timings are not interchangeable.
nutrition: Calories: 263 kcal · Carbs: 8 g · Protein: 24 g · Fat: 16 g · Sodium: 119 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 3 g

title: Instant Pot Oxtail Recipe
description: Oxtails pressure-cooked with wine, broth, vegetables and herbs until the meat is tender and the sauce is deeply savoury.

items: ⅓ cup red wine (optional - I recommend dry sherry)
2 sprigs fresh thyme (or ½ teaspoon dried thyme)

step_groups.name: Method
steps: Press Sauté twice for MEDIUM and heat the oil until HOT appears. Add the oxtails, season with salt and pepper and turn until browned all over. Work in batches with extra oil if the pot is crowded.
Cancel sauté. Pour in the wine, if using, followed by the broth, scraping the base clean.
Stir in the carrots, celery, onion, garlic and tomato paste.
Add the bay leaf and the fresh or dried thyme.

Lock the lid, move the valve to SEALING and cook on high pressure for 45 minutes. Allow about 10-15 minutes for the cooker to reach pressure. When the programme finishes, let the pressure release naturally.
Open the lid carefully and transfer the oxtails for serving.
If desired, skim fat from the cooking liquid and thicken it with a cornstarch slurry.

notes: Brown in batches when necessary so the oxtails sear instead of steaming.
nutrition: Calories: 429 kcal · Carbs: 6 g · Protein: 47 g · Fat: 22 g · Sodium: 749 mg · Fiber: 1 g · Sugar: 3 g

title: Pressure Cooker Japanese Beef Curry
description: Japanese beef curry built on deeply caramelised onions, tender chuck and curry roux, served over rice.

items: ~2 pounds (937g) chuck steak (blade steak) (, 1.5 inch in thickness)
2 - 3 (74g – 110g) Japanese curry roux cube or homemade Japanese curry roux
1.5 pound (680g) yellow onions and shallots (, thinly sliced)
Kosher salt and ground black pepper to taste

step_groups.name: Method
steps: Preheat the pressure cooker over medium heat; on an Instant Pot use Sauté and wait until HOT.

Melt 3 tbsp (45g) butter, then add the onions, shallots and ⅓ tsp (1.3g) baking soda. Cook for about 5 mins, until the onions begin releasing liquid. Seal and cook on high pressure for 20 minutes, then quick-release and open.

Cook the onion mixture over medium-high heat until most liquid is gone, about 16 - 17 mins, stirring constantly. Reduce to medium and continue until deeply golden and dry. Season, remove and reserve the purée.
Return the cooker to medium-high and wait for HOT. Season the chuck and add 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil, coating the base. Brown the meat untouched for 6 - 8 mins on each side, then move it to a board.
Add the garlic and stir for about 30 secs, until aromatic.
Pour in roughly ½ cup (100 ml) stock and use a wooden spoon to release all browned material from the base.

Cut the chuck into 1.5- to 2-inch cubes and return it, with its juices, to the pot. Add the remaining stock, 1 tbsp (15ml) soy sauce and the onion purée. Seal, cook on high pressure for 32 minutes and allow a 10-minute natural release before venting the rest.
Taste the beef-and-onion base, then dissolve the curry roux cubes one at a time. Add more roux or soy sauce only as needed; roughly 2.5 cubes (95g) were used in the source test.
Serve over Calrose rice. For the optional restaurant-style finish, top with mozzarella and bake until melted and browned.
Serve immediately.

notes: Reduce the onion purée patiently; its deep colour provides much of the curry's flavour.
nutrition: Calories: 447 kcal · Carbs: 13 g · Protein: 31 g · Fat: 30 g · Sodium: 346 mg · Fiber: 2 g · Sugar: 6 g

title: Beef Gyros (Slow Cooker or Instant Pot)
description: Greek-style seasoned beef with fresh salad and cool tzatziki, with pressure-cooker and slow-cooker methods.

items: 3 pounds beef chuck roast (, sliced into thin quarter inch strips)
1 English cucumber (, sliced, and then halved)
salt and freshly ground black pepper (to taste)

step_groups.name: Tzatziki
steps: Stir all the tzatziki ingredients together thoroughly and refrigerate until serving.

step_groups.name: Slow cooker
steps: Trim large fat deposits from the roast, cut the beef into thin 1/4-inch strips and arrange them in the slow cooker.
Blend the olive oil, garlic powder, oregano, cumin, thyme, lemon juice, salt and pepper. Pour this mixture over the beef, cover and leave on LOW; after 6 hours, check that the meat is tender.
Line each pita with lettuce, then add beef, tomatoes, cucumber, onion and tzatziki.

step_groups.name: Instant Pot
steps: Trim away large pieces of fat, slice the roast into thin 1/4-inch strips and put them in the Instant Pot.
Mix 1/3 cup WATER with the olive oil, garlic powder, oregano, cumin, thyme, lemon juice, salt and pepper, then pour it over the beef.
Lock the lid, set the valve to sealing and cook on manual or high pressure for 30 minutes.
When the timer sounds, leave the cooker untouched for 15 minutes before venting the remaining pressure. Open, taste and correct the seasoning.
Fill lettuce-lined pitas with the beef, tomatoes, cucumber, onion and tzatziki.

notes: The tzatziki can be made ahead and kept chilled while the beef cooks.
nutrition: Calories: 798 kcal · Carbs: 40 g · Protein: 63 g · Fat: 44 g · Sodium: 898 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 4 g

title: Instant Pot Chipotle Chicken Bowls with Cilantro Lime Quinoa
description: Smoky chipotle chicken, corn and fresh toppings served over lime-and-cilantro quinoa for a complete bowl meal.

step_groups.name: Method
steps: Mix the salt, cumin, black pepper, chipotle paste and salsa in a medium bowl.

Put the chicken in the pressure cooker and cover with the salsa mixture. Cook on high pressure for 20 minutes and use either a quick or natural release. Add the corn, reseal and cook on high pressure for 2 minutes.

While the chicken cooks, combine quinoa, water and salt in a saucepan over high heat. Bring to the boil, cover and reduce the heat to low. Cook for about 18 to 22 minutes, until the liquid is absorbed. Fluff, remove from the heat and fold in lime juice, olive oil and 2T cilantro.
Once pressure cooking is complete, lift out the corn and place it on a dish.
Transfer the chicken to a bowl, shred it and mix in 1/2 cup sauce. Slice the kernels from the cobs.
Into every bowl, measure 2/3 cup quinoa and 1/2 cup chicken with extra sauce. Add the toppings separately: 1/3 cup corn, 1/4 cup tomatoes and 1 ounce avocado.
Finish the bowls with the rest of the cilantro and accompany them with lime wedges.

notes: Rinse and drain the quinoa before cooking to remove its natural bitter coating.
nutrition: Calories: 400 kcal · Carbs: 40 g · Protein: 29.5 g · Fat: 14 g · Sodium: 648 mg · Fiber: 7 g · Sugar: 6.5 g

title: Instant Pot HK Egg Custard 超滑鮮奶燉蛋
description: A light Hong Kong-style steamed egg custard with milk and sugar, pressure-cooked in individual ramekins.

step_groups.name: Method
steps: Combine 4 tbsp (52g) - 5 tbsp (65g) sugar, the salt and 1 cup (250ml) milk in the cooker. Use Slow Cook, or low heat on a stovetop cooker, stirring until the sugar dissolves. Remove from the heat, cool slightly, add the remaining ½ cup (125 ml) milk and mix. The liquid should now feel cool.
Whisk the 3 large eggs in a large glass measuring jug until evenly blended.
Keep whisking while slowly adding the sweetened milk to the eggs, then mix thoroughly.
Pass the custard through a fine strainer twice. Divide it among 4 - 5 ramekins measuring 3 inches x 1.5 inches, skim away bubbles and seal each dish tightly with foil.

Rinse the inner pot, add 1 cup (250ml) cold water and insert the trivet. Arrange the 4 - 5 ramekins on it, stacking in two tiers if necessary. Cook at low pressure for 0 minute, or 1 minute away from sea level or with thick foil, then allow a 10-minute natural release. The pin may fall at about 7 - 8 minutes; still wait for the full 10 minutes before opening. Remove the ramekins promptly.
Serve the custards hot or warm.

notes: Straining twice and removing surface bubbles produces the smoothest custard.
nutrition: Calories: 119 kcal · Carbs: 13 g · Protein: 5 g · Fat: 4 g · Sodium: 69 mg · Sugar: 13 g

title: Instant Pot Shrimp Boil
description: A one-pot shrimp boil with potatoes, smoked sausage and corn, finished with parsley-lemon butter.

items: 2 ears corn on the cob (cut in thirds)
1 pound raw, frozen jumbo shrimp (tails on, peeled or unpeeled)
8 ounces light beer or stock *for 6-quart model
12 ounces light beer or stock *for 8-quart model
1 ½ tablespoons fresh lemon juice (for shrimp boil)
½ tablespoon lemon juice (for seasoned butter)

step_groups.name: Method
steps: Layer the potatoes, 1-inch sausage slices, corn, garlic and bay leaves in that order, then set the frozen shrimp on top. Add 8 ounces beer or stock to a 6-quart cooker, or 12 ounces to an 8-quart cooker; consult the scaling note when using a 3-quart model.
Drizzle the shrimp with 1 ½ tablespoons lemon juice and scatter over 2 tablespoons Old Bay.
Lock the lid with the valve sealed, select high pressure and set 2 minutes; confirm the display reads 2.
As soon as cooking ends, use a long spoon to move the valve to venting for a quick release, keeping clear of the steam.
Stir together 3 tablespoons melted butter, 1 tablespoon parsley and ½ tablespoon lemon juice. Remove the bay leaves, pour the butter over the cooked mixture and toss gently.
Serve at once.

notes: Use raw frozen jumbo shrimp for this timing; thawed, smaller or pre-cooked shrimp will overcook.
nutrition: Calories: 425 kcal · Carbs: 28 g · Protein: 26 g · Fat: 22 g · Sodium: 1148 mg · Fiber: 3 g · Sugar: 2 g

title: Instant Pot Rice Pudding
description: A creamy spiced rice pudding enriched with whole milk, vanilla and sweetened condensed milk.

items: 1 cup (175 grams) medium-grain white rice, uncooked
1 (14 ounce) can (397 grams) sweetened condensed milk

step_groups.name: Method
steps: Rinse the rice under cold running water, then drain it well.
Put the rice in the cooker and add the milk, water, cinnamon, nutmeg, vanilla and salt.
Stir, lock the lid and seal the valve. Cook on manual high pressure for 20 minutes.
Allow a 10-minute natural release, then vent the remaining steam. Open, stir in the condensed milk and mix until evenly creamy before serving.

notes: The pudding thickens as it cools; serve warm for a looser consistency.
nutrition: Calories: 288 kcal · Carbs: 50 g · Protein: 7 g · Fat: 6 g · Sodium: 237 mg · Sugar: 30 g
